job.title: Women's Team Pathway Manager
job.location: AFC Bournemouth Performance Centre, Wimborne, Wimborne, Dorset
job.html_description:
The role brings about an exciting opportunity to join AFC Bournemouth's Womens Team.

AFC Bournemouth Women are looking for individuals to idenitfy with and embody the following values:
  • Professionalism: Always acting with integrity and respect to the badge in a competent and reliable manner.  
  • Togetherness: Working collaboratively through high levels of communication in a manner that supports and environment where everyone feels included, respected and valued.  
  • Opportunity: Providing fair and equitable chances to all individuals by encouraging a growth mindset through empowering the natural abilities of others in service of the overall mission.  
  • Team First: Prioritising the team beyond personal or individual interests.
The purpose of the Women’s Team Pathway Manager role is to lead and manage the Women’s Development Squad environment, ensuring players are developed safely, effectively and appropriately for progression into the Women’s First Team. The role plays a critical part in the long‑term strategic pathway of AFC Bournemouth Women by developing players, staff and the wider environment in a manner that maximises potential, prioritises welfare and supports holistic development. 

Key Attributes
AFCB Women are looking for an individual who possesses:
  • A relentless pursuit of long-term player and staff development. 
  • Strong leadership, communication and interpersonal skills. 
  • Ability to design, adapt and realise long, medium and short-term plans.  
  • Highly reliable in all operational capacities.
  • Understanding of environments that develop talent. 
  • Outstanding growth mindset and a belief that player and staff talent can be developed. 
  •  Alignment with AFCB values.  
  • Committed to diversity, inclusion, and the growth of women’s football. 
Key information about this role:
Please note this role is offered on a permanent, part-time employed basis. This consists of 20 hours a week with the following delivery schedule:
Monday - 6-8 PM
Friday - 7-9 PM
Sunday - 12-4 PM
The remaining hours pattern will be discussed and agreed by both patries upon job offer.
1. Leadership and Environment
  • Lead and oversee the daily environment of the Women’s Development Squad.
  • Uphold the cultural and operational standards outlined in the Club’s long-term planning document — including sustainability, professionalism, holistic care and creating opportunities for local players.  
  • Line manage and support all Development Squad staff in line with Club policies and Human Resources procedures. 
2. Player and Staff Development 
  • Work closely with the Girls’ Academy to ensure that the player pathway into the Women’s Development Squad remains safe and productive. 
  • Lead on individual development planning (IDPs) for Development Squad players, ensuring regular reviews, appropriate progression opportunities and clear communication with the Women’s First Team and Girls’ Academy where relevant. 
  • Lead on development action plans (DAPs) for Development Squad staff, ensuring regular reviews aligned with the Women’s First Team. 
  • Contribute to the Club’s broader pathway strategy by supporting talent identification and progression discussions with the Girls’ Academy and local clubs. 
  • Ensure that training and match demands are age-appropriate and safely managed to support long-term player progression.
3. Safeguarding, Welfare and Compliance
  • Ensure adherence to all relevant league rules, competition regulations and registration requirements.
  • Ensure safeguarding standards are consistently applied, working closely with the club’s Safeguarding Team to create a safe and supportive environment.
4. Operations and Stakeholder Management  
  • Ensure all operations including training, match preparation, communication and scheduling run smoothly and support safe, appropriate player development. 
  • Work collaboratively with the AFC Bournemouth media and communications team to support appropriate public representation of the Development Squad, prioritising player safety, welfare and the Club’s strategic messaging. 
  • Work collaboratively with all additional internal and external stakeholders to optimise the pursuit of the long-term strategic plan.  
  • Undertake relevant additional tasks as required by business needs, upon request by Head of Women’s Football or President of Football Operations.  
  • Act as a key member of Women’s management team, attending regular meetings as required. 
5. Personal Development  
  • Maintain up-to-date knowledge and pursue CPD opportunities. 
  • Complete and update Development Action Plan. 
  • Engage with all staff development processes as outlined by Head of Women’s Football
6. General Duties
  • To ensure the club’s mental health & wellbeing objectives are embedded in your department's actions, procedures and dealings and to have a good knowledge of the processes to support staff's mental health & wellbeing. 
  • To ensure the club’s equality aims, action plan and policies are in evidence in all departmental actions, procedures and dealings. 
  • To ensure commitment to the club’s safeguarding vision and to actively promote the policies within your department, embedding safeguarding into procedures.
  • Obligation to promote and monitor the club’s health and safety policy and procedures within your department.
  • Responsibility to comply with the Health and Safety at Work etc. Act 1974, the Regulatory Reform (Fire Safety) Order 2005, and all related Company policies, taking reasonable care for their own safety and that of others, reporting hazards or incidents, following safe systems of work, and co-operating with all training and emergency procedures.
The duties and responsibilities described are not a comprehensive list and additional tasks may be assigned to the employee from time to time; or the scope of the job may change as necessitated by business demands.
Essential  
  • Experience working in an elite youth development sporting environment. 
  • A deep understanding of safe and age‑appropriate player development processes. 
  • Ability to manage a development environment effectively and consistently. 
  • Understanding of safeguarding best practice. 
  • Safeguarding and DBS clearance. (Club provided upon job offer)
Desirable 
  • Experience working in an elite youth development football environment, ideally a girls’ pathway
  • Familiarity with PGA standards and WSL/WNL pathway models. 
  • UEFA coaching qualifications and/or youth‑specific development awards.
  • Emergency First Aid for Sport
  • Academic qualifications relating to pedagogy, coaching or sport at degree level or higher.  
  • Experience in media engagement and public representation. 
  • FA Talent ID qualifications.  
  • Free onsite parking.
  • Season ticket and allocation of complimentary/purchased tickets.
  • Subsidised lunches and complimentary healthy snacks throughout the day.
  • Discounts at the club Superstore.
  • Access to Health Cash Plan with Simplyhealth.
  • Employee Assistance Programme
  • Contribution towards eye tests and glasses.
  • Discounts and benefits from partners and local businesses.
  • Club pension & Life Assurance Scheme.
  • Paid parental leave (bank of five days per year).
  • Club events or other social events throughout the year run by our club social team.
  • Paid volunteer opportunities (2 days per year).
  • Paid day’s leave on your birthday.
job.plain_text_description:
The role brings about an exciting opportunity to join AFC Bournemouth's Womens Team.
AFC Bournemouth Women are looking for individuals to idenitfy with and embody the following values:
- Professionalism: Always acting with integrity and respect to the badge in a competent and reliable manner. - Togetherness: Working collaboratively through high levels of communication in a manner that supports and environment where everyone feels included, respected and valued. - Opportunity: Providing fair and equitable chances to all individuals by encouraging a growth mindset through empowering the natural abilities of others in service of the overall mission. - Team First:Prioritising the team beyond personal or individual interests.
The purpose of the Women’s Team Pathway Manager role is to lead and manage the Women’s Development Squad environment, ensuring players are developed safely, effectively and appropriately for progression into the Women’s First Team. The role plays a critical part in the long‑term strategic pathway of AFC Bournemouth Women by developing players, staff and the wider environment in a manner that maximises potential, prioritises welfare and supports holistic development.
Key Attributes AFCB Women are looking for an individual who possesses:
- A relentless pursuit of long-term player and staff development. - Strong leadership, communication and interpersonal skills. - Ability to design, adapt and realise long, medium and short-term plans. - Highly reliable in all operational capacities. - Understanding of environments that develop talent. - Outstanding growth mindset and a belief that player and staff talent can be developed. - Alignment with AFCB values. - Committed to diversity, inclusion, and the growth of women’s football.
Key information about this role: Please note this role is offered on a permanent, part-time employed basis. This consists of 20 hours a week with the following delivery schedule: Monday - 6-8 PM
Friday - 7-9 PM
Sunday - 12-4 PM The remaining hours pattern will be discussed and agreed by both patries upon job offer.
1. Leadership and Environment
- Lead and oversee the daily environment of the Women’s Development Squad. - Uphold the cultural and operational standards outlined in the Club’s long-term planning document — including sustainability, professionalism, holistic care and creating opportunities for local players. - Line manage and support all Development Squad staff in line with Club policies and Human Resources procedures.
2. Player and Staff Development
- Work closely with the Girls’ Academy to ensure that the player pathway into the Women’s Development Squad remains safe and productive. - Lead on individual development planning (IDPs) for Development Squad players, ensuring regular reviews, appropriate progression opportunities and clear communication with the Women’s First Team and Girls’ Academy where relevant. - Lead on development action plans (DAPs) for Development Squad staff, ensuring regular reviews aligned with the Women’s First Team. - Contribute to the Club’s broader pathway strategy by supporting talent identification and progression discussions with the Girls’ Academy and local clubs. - Ensure that training and match demands are age-appropriate and safely managed to support long-term player progression.
3. Safeguarding, Welfare and Compliance
- Ensure adherence to all relevant league rules, competition regulations and registration requirements. - Ensure safeguarding standards are consistently applied, working closely with the club’s Safeguarding Team to create a safe and supportive environment.
4. Operations and Stakeholder Management
- Ensure all operations including training, match preparation, communication and scheduling run smoothly and support safe, appropriate player development. - Work collaboratively with the AFC Bournemouth media and communications team to support appropriate public representation of the Development Squad, prioritising player safety, welfare and the Club’s strategic messaging. - Work collaboratively with all additional internal and external stakeholders to optimise the pursuit of the long-term strategic plan. - Undertake relevant additional tasks as required by business needs, upon request by Head of Women’s Football or President of Football Operations. - Act as a key member of Women’s management team, attending regular meetings as required.
5. Personal Development
- Maintain up-to-date knowledge and pursue CPD opportunities. - Complete and update Development Action Plan. - Engage with all staff development processes as outlined by Head of Women’s Football
6. General Duties
- To ensure the club’s mental health & wellbeing objectives are embedded in your department's actions, procedures and dealings and to have a good knowledge of the processes to support staff's mental health & wellbeing. - To ensure the club’s equality aims, action plan and policies are in evidence in all departmental actions, procedures and dealings. - To ensure commitment to the club’s safeguarding vision and to actively promote the policies within your department, embedding safeguarding into procedures. - Obligation to promote and monitor the club’s health and safety policy and procedures within your department. - Responsibility to comply with the Health and Safety at Work etc. Act 1974, the Regulatory Reform (Fire Safety) Order 2005, and all related Company policies, taking reasonable care for their own safety and that of others, reporting hazards or incidents, following safe systems of work, and co-operating with all training and emergency procedures.
The duties and responsibilities described are not a comprehensive list and additional tasks may be assigned to the employee from time to time; or the scope of the job may change as necessitated by business demands.
Essential
- Experience working in an elite youth development sporting environment. - A deep understanding of safe and age‑appropriate player development processes. - Ability to manage a development environment effectively and consistently. - Understanding of safeguarding best practice. - Safeguarding and DBS clearance. (Club provided upon job offer)
Desirable
- Experience working in an elite youth development football environment, ideally a girls’ pathway - Familiarity with PGA standards and WSL/WNL pathway models. - UEFA coaching qualifications and/or youth‑specific development awards. - Emergency First Aid for Sport - Academic qualifications relating to pedagogy, coaching or sport at degree level or higher. - Experience in media engagement and public representation. - FA Talent ID qualifications.
- Free onsite parking. - Season ticket and allocation of complimentary/purchased tickets. - Subsidised lunches and complimentary healthy snacks throughout the day. - Discounts at the club Superstore. - Access to Health Cash Plan with Simplyhealth. - Employee Assistance Programme - Contribution towards eye tests and glasses. - Discounts and benefits from partners and local businesses. - Club pension & Life Assurance Scheme. - Paid parental leave (bank of five days per year). - Club events or other social events throughout the year run by our club social team. - Paid volunteer opportunities (2 days per year). - Paid day’s leave on your birthday.

job.title: Academy Performance Coach (Casual)
job.location: AFC Bournemouth Performance Centre, Wimborne, Wimborne, Dorset
job.html_description:
The post holder will strive to develop gold standard practice within the academy Performance & Medicine department at AFC Bournemouth. This will be achieved through the understanding and implementation of best practice strength and conditioning, and appropriate level of scientific support. 
Departmental Requirements  
  • To work and liaise effectively within the department and integrate with current staff across the academy. 
  • To be willing to further their knowledge through continuous professional development and implement relevant knowledge into practice, of which the club will support.  
  • Willingness to work across a range of age groups spanning from the U9’s to the U16’s.   
Strength and Conditioning Responsibilities 
  •  Deliver best practice strength and conditioning coaching in line with the department philosophies, to academy squads U9-U16.  
  • Assist in the production and delivery of gym and field-based conditioning programmes tailored for differing needs of each age group and individuals within those teams.   
  • Lead/Assist in physical assessment procedures with academy squads, and ensure all data is recorded and interpreted, with future programmes tailored to individual needs.   
  • Collaborate with the medical team ensuring best practice injury prevention methods are conducted.    
  • Adhere to the club movement curriculum that can be applied across different age groups highlighting key movements that players should be able to attain during certain stages of development with clear progressions as key motor skills are learnt.    
Sport Science Responsibilities  
  • Ensure that all playing staff are offered high quality scientific support on a day-to-day basis.   
  • To keep abreast with sport science research development and advances and maintain an open and flexible stance regarding best sport science practice.   
  • Monitor and interpret individual and team training data whilst providing knowledgeable feedback to medical department and coaches.  
  • Use training data to try to offer advice on subsequent team training periodization.    
  • Demonstrate a willingness to further personal knowledge into all disciplines of sport science including nutrition, physiology, psychology, and biomechanics, by attending relevant educational courses to positively enhance future sport science practice.   
  • Partake and contribute to department in service sessions on a regular basis, aimed at enhancing the knowledge of the department and ensuring best practice.  
  • Contribution to 8 weekly player reports providing individual feedback in conjunction with the medical team on players’ athletic profile and development.
General
  • Ensure in all matters, you follow and actively promote the club’s mental health policies and practices to ensure an open, non-judgmental and inclusive environment for everyone engaged with the club. 
  • Ensure that you to adhere to the club’s safeguarding policies and procedures and are committed to our safeguarding values, creating a safe and positive environment for all those who interact with the club. 
  • Obligation to adhere to the club’s health and safety policy and procedures. 
  • Ensure in all matters you follow and actively promote the club’s equality policies and practices to ensure a welcoming, safe and inclusive environment for everyone engaged with the club. 
  • Responsibility to comply with the Health and Safety at Work etc. Act 1974, the Regulatory Reform (Fire Safety) Order 2005, and all related Company policies, taking reasonable care for their own safety and that of others, reporting hazards or incidents, following safe systems of work, and co-operating with all training and emergency procedures.

The duties and responsibilities described are not a comprehensive list and additional tasks may be assigned to the employee from time to time; or the scope of the job may change as necessitated by business demands.
Essential:
  • BSc. (or equivalent) qualification in Sport Science or Strength & Conditioning 
  • Experience working at youth level in professional football. 
  • Extensive knowledge of growth and maturation 
  • Strong operational ability with an attention to detail. 
Desirable:
  • MSc. (or equivalent) qualification in Sport Science or Strength & Conditioning 
  • UKSCA or NSCA strength & conditioning accreditation

job.title: Women's Team Lead Physiotherapist
job.location: AFC Bournemouth Performance Centre, Wimborne, Wimborne, Dorset
job.html_description:
Women’s Club Values: 
  • Professionalism: Always acing with integrity and respect to the badge in a competent and reliable manner.  
  • Togetherness: Working collaboratively through high levels of communication in a manner that supports and environment where everyone feels included, respected and valued.  
  • Opportunity: Providing fair and equitable chances to all individuals by encouraging a growth mindset through empowering the natural abilities of others in service of the overall mission.  
  • Team First: Prioritising the team beyond personal or individual interests.  
We are looking for a Women's Team Lead Physiotherapist to provide high-quality physiotherapy services that support injury prevention, assessment, treatment, and rehabilitation of players within an elite Women’s Football environment. The role contributes to player availability, long-term athlete health, and integrated performance support in line with WSL Performance Support Regulations.
  • Lead the women’s team performance and medicine department on an operational level in conjunction with the co-ordinator of performance and medicine.  
  • Deliver comprehensive physiotherapy services including assessment, diagnosis, treatment, and rehabilitation of injuries. 
  • Design and deliver individualised rehabilitation programmes aligned with best practice and performance demands. 
  • Support injury prevention strategies in collaboration with strength & conditioning and medical staff.
  • Contribute to return-to-play planning and decision-making through multidisciplinary processes.
  • Provide pitch-side medical and emergency cover for training sessions and matches as required.
  • Maintain accurate medical records in line with data protection, confidentiality, and clinical governance standards. 
  • Communicate effectively with coaching and performance staff regarding player availability and progression. 
  • Support ongoing monitoring of player health, load tolerance, and injury trends. 
  • Adhere to club medical policies, FA regulations, and WSL medical requirements. 
  • As a member of staff to ensure in all matters, you follow and actively promote the club’s mental health policies and practices to ensure an open, non-judgmental and inclusive environment for everyone engaged with the club. 
  • As a member of staff to ensure that you to adhere to the club’s safeguarding policies and procedures and are committed to our safeguarding values, creating a safe and positive environment for all those who interact with the club. 
  • Obligation to adhere to the club’s health and safety policy and procedures.
  • As a member of staff to ensure in all matters you follow and actively promote the club’s equality policies and practices to ensure a welcoming, safe and inclusive environment for everyone engaged with the club. 
  • Responsibility to comply with the Health and Safety at Work etc. Act 1974, the Regulatory Reform (Fire Safety) Order 2005, and all related Company policies, taking reasonable care for their own safety and that of others, reporting hazards or incidents, following safe systems of work, and co-operating with all training and emergency procedures.

The duties and responsibilities described are not a comprehensive list and additional tasks may be assigned to the employee from time to time; or the scope of the job may change as necessitated by business demands.
Essential
  • BSc or MSc in Physiotherapy. 
  • Registered with the Health and Care Professions Council (HCPC).
  • Member of the Chartered Society of Physiotherapy (CSP). 
  • Level 5 ATMMIF certificate. 
  • ATMMIF-R reaccreditation certificate. 
  • Appropriate professional indemnity insurance. 
  • Emergency First Aid for Sport. 
  • Safeguarding and DBS clearance. 
  • UK anti-doping introduction to clear sport certificate. 
  • Experience working in elite sport or high-performance environments. 
  • Experience managing musculoskeletal injuries in athletes. 
  • Experience working as part of a multidisciplinary performance or medical team. 
Desirable 
  • Experience leading a multidisciplinary performance or medical team.
  • Experience within professional or elite Women’s football. 
  • Postgraduate qualification in sports physiotherapy or related discipline. 
  • Experience with pitch-side emergency care. 
  • Knowledge of FA or WSL medical governance frameworks. 

KEY ATTRIBUTES:
  • Strong clinical reasoning and hands-on treatment skills. 
  • Knowledge of football-specific injury profiles and demands. 
  • Understanding of return-to-play frameworks and risk management. 
  • Excellent communication and organisational skills. 
  • Awareness of female athlete health considerations. 
  • Player-centred and evidence-based approach. 
  • High standards of professionalism and clinical integrity. 
  • Collaborative team member within an integrated performance model. 
  • Calm and effective under pressure. 
  • Committed to diversity, inclusion, and the growth of women’s football. 
  • Effective communicator with players, staff, and stakeholders. 
  • Free onsite parking.
  • Season ticket and allocation of complimentary/purchased tickets.
  • Subsidised lunches and complimentary healthy snacks throughout the day.
  • Discounts at the club Superstore.
  • Access to Health Cash Plan with Simplyhealth.
  • Employee Assistance Programme
  • Contribution towards eye tests and glasses.
  • Discounts and benefits from partners and local businesses.
  • Club pension & Life Assurance Scheme.
  • Paid parental leave (bank of five days per year).
  • Club events or other social events throughout the year run by our club social team.
  • Paid volunteer opportunities (2 days per year).
  • Paid day’s leave on your birthday.

job.title: Matchday Steward (Casual)
job.html_description:
Location: Vitality Stadium, and other venues as directed by AFC Bournemouth 
Hours: Varied 

AFC Bournemouth has an exciting opportunity to grow its Stewarding team and are looking for enthusiastic candidates to be an integral part of AFC Bournemouth’s matchdays, helping to keep all persons present at Vitality Stadium safe to enjoy the excitement of competing in the English Premier League (EPL). 
  • To provide an excellent service to Home and Visiting Supporters and represent the Club’s welcoming and friendly ethos.  
  • Attend all training required by the club. 
  • Implement the League’s Ground Regulations to maintain a safe environment while ensuring a good matchday experience for all persons present. 
  • Control or direct spectators who are entering or leaving the ground, to help achieve a safe, even flow of people in, to and from the viewing areas. 
  • To monitor crowd movements and density within viewing areas, so as to prevent overcrowding. 
  • Recognise potential issues and report them to a supervisor or the Control Room.  
  • Staff entrances, exits and other strategic points to, from and within the stadium to ensure, and maintain, all safe exit routes remain clear during the progress of the specified activity. 
  • Undertake specific duties in an emergency, or as otherwise directed by the Safety Officer. 
  • To be confident and competent in the use of technology and aids supplied by the club, such as radios and tablets. 
  • Assist in the safe operation of the ground, not to view the activity taking place; and understand general responsibilities towards Health & Safety.  
  • Assist with safeguarding of all persons present, including vulnerable adults, children and themselves. 
  • Check accreditation in restricted areas and ensure only those authorised to do so have access. 
  • Ensure a full report of any incidents is accurately completed before finishing duty. 
  • To be the first point of call for any incidents or issues in the stadium. 
  • Adhere to the club’s safeguarding policies and procedures and remain committed to our safeguarding values, creating a safe and positive environment for all those who interact with the club.  
  • Adhere to the club’s health and safety policy and procedures.  
  • Actively promote the club’s equality policies and practices to ensure a welcoming, safe and inclusive environment for everyone engaged with the club

The duties and responsibilities described are not a comprehensive list and additional tasks may be assigned to the employee from time to time; or the scope of the job may change as necessitated by business demands.



  • Possess or be willing to undertake NVQ Level 2 in Spectator Safety, such training to be provided by the Club. 
  • Excellent customer service and communication skills, including a calm approach to difficult situations. 
  • A strong desire to work as part of a team to deliver a safe and enjoyable matchday. 
  • An understanding and ability to communicate verbal and written instructions in English 
The Club’s values are at the core of its staff behaviour and how they approach everything they do; therefore, we are looking for someone is honest, inclusive, kind, hardworking, loyal, and respectful. 

The successful candidate will be required perform a reasonable level of physical activity (standing for prolonged periods etc), along with having the maturity, reliability, and interpersonal skills needed to carry out duties effectively and responsibly.
job.plain_text_description:
Location: Vitality Stadium, and other venues as directed by AFC Bournemouth
Hours:Varied
AFC Bournemouth has an exciting opportunity to grow its Stewarding team and are looking for enthusiastic candidates to be an integral part of AFC Bournemouth’s matchdays, helping to keep all persons present at Vitality Stadium safe to enjoy the excitement of competing in the English Premier League (EPL).
- To provide an excellent service to Home and Visiting Supporters and represent the Club’s welcoming and friendly ethos. - Attend all training required by the club. - Implement the League’s Ground Regulations to maintain a safe environment while ensuring a good matchday experience for all persons present. - Control or direct spectators who are entering or leaving the ground, to help achieve a safe, even flow of people in, to and from the viewing areas. - To monitor crowd movements and density within viewing areas, so as to prevent overcrowding. - Recognise potential issues and report them to a supervisor or the Control Room. - Staff entrances, exits and other strategic points to, from and within the stadium to ensure, and maintain, all safe exit routes remain clear during the progress of the specified activity. - Undertake specific duties in an emergency, or as otherwise directed by the Safety Officer. - To be confident and competent in the use of technology and aids supplied by the club, such as radios and tablets. - Assist in the safe operation of the ground, not to view the activity taking place; and understand general responsibilities towards Health & Safety. - Assist with safeguarding of all persons present, including vulnerable adults, children and themselves. - Check accreditation in restricted areas and ensure only those authorised to do so have access. - Ensure a full report of any incidents is accurately completed before finishing duty. - To be the first point of call for any incidents or issues in the stadium. - Adhere to the club’s safeguarding policies and procedures and remain committed to our safeguarding values, creating a safe and positive environment for all those who interact with the club. - Adhere to the club’s health and safety policy and procedures. - Actively promote the club’s equality policies and practices to ensure a welcoming, safe and inclusive environment for everyone engaged with the club
The duties and responsibilities described are not a comprehensive list and additional tasks may be assigned to the employee from time to time; or the scope of the job may change as necessitated by business demands.
- Possess or be willing to undertake NVQ Level 2 in Spectator Safety, such training to be provided by the Club. - Excellent customer service and communication skills, including a calm approach to difficult situations. - A strong desire to work as part of a team to deliver a safe and enjoyable matchday. - An understanding and ability to communicate verbal and written instructions in English
The Club’s values are at the core of its staff behaviour and how they approach everything they do; therefore, we are looking for someone is honest, inclusive, kind, hardworking, loyal, and respectful.
The successful candidate will be required perform a reasonable level of physical activity (standing for prolonged periods etc), along with having the maturity, reliability, and interpersonal skills needed to carry out duties effectively and responsibly.

job.title: Academy Lead Physiotherapist (U9-16)
job.location: AFC Bournemouth Performance Centre, Wimborne, Wimborne, Dorset
job.html_description:
AFC Bournemouth's Academy are now looking for a talented Physiotherapist to become an integrated member of the AFC Bournemouth Academy Performance & Medical team, providing evidence-based advice, treatment, and medical assistance to players in the Foundation and Youth Development Phases, but predominantly with the Under 9s to Under 16s.

Academy Values:
  • Players First: every player valued. Every decision made with care. 
  • Togetherness: working as part of a wider team and willing to contribute beyond own interests.
  • Integrity: carrying out academy and club values in an open and honest manner.
  • Accountability: willing to take responsibility for actions, allowing for learning, growth and reflection. 
  • To provide appropriate medical support to all U9-16 players in the Academy, primarily focusing on the U13 to U16 age groups.
  • To have a close working relationship with other Academy and First team medical staff when expertise is required for complex injury management.
  • To work within a multi-disciplinary team (MDT) and liaise effectively with lead coaches, Performance Coaches and other MDT staff regarding player availability, injury status and rehabilitation plans.
  • Ensure all Academy training nights, injury clinics & matches are adequately covered medically.
  • Perform pre-season medical testing as deemed appropriate for the FP and YDP groups and provide subsequent injury risk reduction programmes for Academy players. 
  • Assist in the organization of cardiac screening for all players in compliance with the FA/PL.
  • Be a point of contact for parents/guardians of Academy players regarding any medical queries.
  • To ensure all YDP medical records and documentation is kept up to date on PMA in conjunction with EPPP requirements. 
  • To assist in undertaking injury audits for all FP and YDP players ensuring information is kept in-line with EPPP guidelines, and feedback findings to MDT. 
  • Accompany Academy players to medical appointments when required.
  • Assist with the medical support for the U18’s and U21’s when required.
  • Contribute to the Elite Performance CPD programme by participating in the monthly CPD’s and joining in with weekly medical team discussions regarding long term injured players.
  • To carry out any other tasks directed by your line manager.
  • As a member of staff to ensure in all matters, you follow and actively promote the club’s mental health policies and practices to ensure an open, non-judgmental and inclusive environment for everyone engaged with the club. 
  • As a member of staff to ensure that you to adhere to the club’s safeguarding policies and procedures and are committed to our safeguarding values, creating a safe and positive environment for all those who interact with the club.
  • Responsibility to comply with the Health and Safety at Work etc. Act 1974, the Regulatory Reform (Fire Safety) Order 2005, and all related Company policies, taking reasonable care for their own safety and that of others, reporting hazards or incidents, following safe systems of work, and co-operating with all training and emergency procedures. 
  • Obligation to adhere to the club’s health and safety policy and procedures. 
  • As a member of staff to ensure in all matters you follow and actively promote the club’s equality policies and practices to ensure a welcoming, safe and inclusive environment for everyone engaged with the club. 
The duties and responsibilities described are not a comprehensive list and additional tasks may be assigned to the employee from time to time; or the scope of the job may change as necessitated by business demands.
Essential
  • BSc (Hons) Degree in Physiotherapy
  • A member of the CSP and HCPC
  • Up to date ATMMiF (Advanced Trauma Medical Management in Football) qualification
  • A good understanding of long-term athletic development and its implications for the multi-disciplinary approach across the Elite Player Performance Pathway (EPPP).
  • Experience of working with elite developing athletes 
  • Evidence of Continued Professional Development (CPD) Portfolio
  • Up to date FA Safeguarding Children certificate
  • Prior to commencement, an Enhanced DBS will be required
Desirable 
  • MSc in a MSK/Rehabilitation or sport related field.
  • Dry Needling/Acupuncture Qualification
  • Free onsite parking.
  • Season ticket and allocation of complimentary/purchased tickets.
  • Subsidised lunches and complimentary healthy snacks throughout the day.
  • Discounts at the club Superstore.
  • Access to Health Cash Plan with Simplyhealth.
  • Employee Assistance Programme
  • Contribution towards eye tests and glasses.
  • Discounts and benefits from partners and local businesses.
  • Club pension & Life Assurance Scheme.
  • Paid parental leave (bank of five days per year).
  • Club events or other social events throughout the year run by our club social team.
  • Paid volunteer opportunities (2 days per year).
  • Paid day’s leave on your birthday.

job.title: Senior Applications and Development Manager
job.location: Arsenal Football Club, London, N5 1BU, GB
job.html_description:
The Role

We are looking for a hands-on Senior Applications and Development Manager to drive the world‑class development of our business‑critical applications and software solutions. Leading a team of Developers, Analysts and application specialists, you will oversee the full lifecycle of all applications and software, ensuring security requirements, onboarding processes and technology standards are consistently met.

In this exciting role, you will manage development pipelines, oversee application lifecycles, and collaborate with a wide range of stakeholders to deliver innovation and efficiency across our Technology department. You will be entrusted to lead a team responsible for ensuring applications remain secure, scalable and fully aligned with evolving business needs.

This is an excellent opportunity to shape the maintenance, optimisation and ongoing improvement of technology across our club - combining effective leadership with exceptional application development to deliver world‑class software solutions.

Who we are

We are one of the most famous clubs in world football, with a rich heritage and history of success – read more about our history, here.

Beyond that, we are passionate about our local community and, behind the scenes, we have a wide variety of opportunities and career paths for all. We have a very defined purpose: to act for a winning team, culture and community. We achieve this by ensuring we are courageous in the pursuit of progress, we champion our community and each other, and that we do the right thing (even when no one is looking).

Our Commitment to Safeguarding

We are committed to ensuring that our environments for children, young people and adults at risk remain safe and inclusive. We therefore require successful candidates to complete a DBS check and other essential background checks prior to starting their employment. Depending on the responsibilities of the role, a DBS will be either a basic, standard, or enhanced check.

Your day-to-day

  • Lead and empower your team - you’ll guide Developers, Analysts, and application specialists, supporting growth while consistently ensuring high‑quality delivery

  • Set development standards - you will outline and maintain best practices and governance frameworks that support secure and efficient application development

  • Maintain application performance - you will ensure systems remain secure, scalable, and up to date, managing upgrades and lifecycle activities

  • Collaborate with stakeholders - you will work closely with cross-functional teams to align on requirements, communicate progress, and deliver solutions that meet expectations

  • Manage external partners - you’ll oversee relationships with third‑party vendors, ensuring effective management of delivery and budget

  • Ensure compliance and mitigate risk - you will uphold regulatory, security, and governance requirements across all application development activity

 

What we are looking for

  • Application development experience - you have proven experience working in application development, ideally in football or sports environments

  • Demonstrated leadership expertise - you’re highly skilled in managing Software Development teams, supporting direct reports and leading team projects

  • Operational resilience and risk awareness - you can expertly analyse issues, assess risks, manage incidents and design effective security solutions

  • Exceptional knowledge of programming languages - you’re confident in understanding and interpreting programming languages (eg. Java, .NET & Python)

  • Cloud platform experience - you’re familiar with cloud platforms (Azure, AWS) and DevOps practices

  • Excellent communication skills - you possess exceptional written and verbal communication capabilities, with the ability to make technical concepts accessible to non-technical audiences

  • Software development methodology proficiency - you’re experienced in methodologies and frameworks such as SDLC, Agile and DevOps

  • Organised, strategic and adaptable mindset - you excel in navigating ambiguity and fast-changing environments, delivering smart, compelling solutions while managing projects simultaneously

Why choose us

At Arsenal, we want everyone to feel a sense of trust and belonging, so we are proud of both our club values and also what we offer to our employees. As one of our Gunners, you will receive:

  • An exciting reward and recognition scheme

  • Generous holiday allowance which increases with your length of service

  • Great internal learning and development programmes

  • A flexible hybrid working model

  • Priority access to apply for match tickets

  • A competitive health and wellbeing benefits package

  • A leading Employee Assistance Programme

  • Great discounts with some of our Partners

  • An opportunity to play a vital role in delivering our ED&I strategy by joining one of our Employee Networks

Discover why ‘Our Gunners’ are proud to be part of our team - view their stories here.

Arsenal for Everyone

Arsenal for Everyone is our commitment to promoting and embracing equality, diversity and inclusion, so that everyone connected to the club feels like they belong to the same Arsenal family. We believe that diversity of background, skills and experience drives our success on and off the pitch. 


Disability Confident Leader

We are a Disability Confident Leader. We actively welcome and encourage applications from people with disabilities and long-term health conditions. If you need disability-related adjustments to the recruitment process, please indicate this in your application.

If you are likely to meet the definition of being a ‘disabled person’ according to the Equality Act 2010, you may be eligible to apply for an interview via the Disability Confident Scheme - please indicate this in your application form below. The information you share with us about your health or disability will not be used in recruitment decisions.


Application Closing Date - Friday 6th March 2026

Please note: we reserve the right to close the position early if application volumes are particularly high. We encourage you to get your application in sooner rather than later.

Updates on the status of your application will be sent via email, please routinely check your spam and junk folders, as occasionally emails sent via the Teamtailor platform will find themselves there.

Good luck!

job.title: People Services Administrator
job.location: Arsenal Football Club, London, N5 1BU, GB
job.html_description:
The Role

We are looking for a People Services Administrator to provide high impact, value add People/HR support services to our managers, colleagues and the wider People team.

This exciting opportunity will allow you to play a pivotal role in supporting employees at all levels; you will be the first point of contact for our people and through practical employee lifecycle administration, you will ensure everyone gets a consistently brilliant experience with the People team.

 

Who we are

We are one of the most famous clubs in world football, with a rich heritage and history of success – read more about our history, here.

Beyond that, we are passionate about our local community and, behind the scenes, we have a wide variety of opportunities and career paths for all. We have a very defined purpose: to act for a winning team, culture and community. We achieve this by ensuring we are courageous in the pursuit of progress, we champion our community and each other, and that we do the right thing (even when no one is looking).

Our Commitment to Safeguarding

We are committed to ensuring that our environments for children, young people and adults at risk remain safe and inclusive. We therefore require successful candidates to complete a DBS check and other essential background checks prior to starting their employment. Depending on the responsibilities of the role, a DBS will be either a basic, standard, or enhanced check.

Your day-to-day

  • Handle first-line query management - you’ll deliver prompt, accurate and supportive responses to a range of queries, providing clear guidance aligned with policies, processes and people practices

  • Provide comprehensive data and systems administration - you’ll ensure accuracy when updating HR systems and electronic files for new starters, changes, absences and leavers

  • Champion efficient document creation - you’ll prepare a high volume of varied contracts and agreements, working with pace and accuracy, ensuring all details are fully checked and compliant before issuing

  • Oversee onboarding and compliance coordination - you’ll oversee pre‑employment checks, including DBS and right‑to‑work, with thorough attention to accuracy, timelines and regulatory requirements

  • Support the payroll and employee lifecycle - you’ll prepare payroll inputs and reports to a high standard and contribute to annual people routines such as pay review, bonus cycles, performance cycles and surveys

  • Aid financial processing - you’ll raise purchase orders and approve invoices promptly and accurately, ensuring alignment with internal financial procedures and documentation requirements

  • Partner with our wider People team - you’ll provide reliable support across operations, talent development, reward and talent acquisition when required

  • Uphold compliance and governance - you’ll apply GDPR, employment legislation and internal policies effectively, producing well‑structured notes for employee relations casework and demonstrating fairness and inclusion in all interactions

 

What we are looking for

  • Track record in a People or HR team - you bring hands‑on experience having managed day‑to‑day HR and People administration, maintaining accurate records, documentation and databases with precision

  • Trusted first point of contact - you’ve supported a wider HR or People team, taking pride in delivering timely, end‑to‑end resolutions to employee queries

  • On boarding and off boarding expert - you have proactively led processes across multiple systems to prepare contracts and complete a range of vetting checks with confidence and efficiency

  • Proactive payroll support experience - you have provided accurate and timely employee data, including starters, leavers, absences and contractual changes, taking full ownership of data integrity

  • Employee focused mindset - you're genuinely passionate about helping others and providing a reliable and approachable service

  • Confident communicator - you communicate clearly and professionally across written, verbal and in‑person interactions, building impactful and effective working relationships

  • Proactive and accountable approach - you take ownership of your work, ensuring every task is driven through to completion and issues are resolved but also understand when to escalate and reach out for support

  • Exceptional attention to detail - you take pride in producing accurate work and spotting potential inconsistencies early

We encourage you to submit a cover letter outlining how you meet our criteria.

Why choose us

At Arsenal, we want everyone to feel a sense of trust and belonging, so we are proud of both our club values and also what we offer to our employees. As one of our Gunners, you will receive:

  • An exciting reward and recognition scheme

  • Generous holiday allowance which increases with your length of service

  • Great internal learning and development programmes

  • A flexible hybrid working model

  • Priority access to apply for match tickets

  • A competitive health and wellbeing benefits package

  • A leading Employee Assistance Programme

  • Great discounts with some of our Partners

  • An opportunity to play a vital role in delivering our ED&I strategy by joining one of our Employee Networks

Discover why ‘Our Gunners’ are proud to be part of our team - view their stories here.

Arsenal for Everyone

Arsenal for Everyone is our commitment to promoting and embracing equality, diversity and inclusion, so that everyone connected to the club feels like they belong to the same Arsenal family. We believe that diversity of background, skills and experience drives our success on and off the pitch. 


Disability Confident Leader

We are a Disability Confident Leader. We actively welcome and encourage applications from people with disabilities and long-term health conditions. If you need disability-related adjustments to the recruitment process, please indicate this in your application.

If you are likely to meet the definition of being a ‘disabled person’ according to the Equality Act 2010, you may be eligible to apply for an interview via the Disability Confident Scheme - please indicate this in your application form below. The information you share with us about your health or disability will not be used in recruitment decisions.


Application Closing Date - Sunday 8th March 2026

Please note: we reserve the right to close the position early if application volumes are particularly high. We encourage you to get your application in sooner rather than later.

Updates on the status of your application will be sent via email, please routinely check your spam and junk folders, as occasionally emails sent via the Teamtailor platform will find themselves there.

Good luck!

Updates on the status of your application will be sent via email, please routinely check your spam and junk folders, as occasionally emails sent via the Teamtailor platform will find themselves there.

job.title: Visual Merchandiser
job.location: Birmingham Discipline: Retail Job type: Full Time, Permanent Salary: Up to £30,000 per annum dependent upon experience Contact email: Careers@avfc.co.uk Job ref: 011683 Published: 20 days ago Expiry date: 15 Mar 2026 23:59
job.html_description:
Department: Retail and Licensing Department

Hours of Work: 35 hours per week (including evenings and weekends)

Contract Type: Permanent

Salary: Up to £30,000 per annum dependent upon experience

Location: Bullring Store / Villa Park

Closing Date: Sunday 15 March 2026

1. The Department

At Aston Villa Football Club, our Retail and Licensing department plays a vital role in supporting the club's commercial success and fan engagement. We take pride in offering a wide variety of high-quality football merchandise and leisurewear to our supporters, both online and in-store, while maintaining a strong connection to our brand and culture. As the Club has grown, we have expanded our team to enhance the produce offering and ensure we continue delivering an exceptional customer experience. This is a fantastic opportunity to be part of an innovative department that works collaboratively to bring our fans closer to the Club through products they love.

2. The Role

You will play a vital role in creating and maintaining compelling visual displays and store layouts that enhance the customer experience and drive sales across all retail touchpoints, including the Club’s stores in the Bullring and at Villa Park. You will ensure that the Club’s merchandise is presented in a way that engages fans and reflects the club’s identity and brand.

Your key responsibilities and accountabilities will include designing, implementing and maintaining window displays, in-store layouts and product placement to maximise product visibility, sales and brand identity ensuring alignment with the Club’s brand and calendars.   You will create a strategic customer journey in-store by creating displays which tell a story whilst ensuring that product discovery is intuitive.

You will also lead on developing and executing seasonal campaigns and specialist merchandising displays in store including exclusive product launches, matchday and in-store promotions. You will also report and analyse the performance of displays and merchandising, providing real time data to the Retail Store Manager.

For further information about the Role, please see the Role Profile.

3. The Person

We are looking for a talented and creative individual with proven experience working in a similar visual merchandising role, ideally in fashion or sports retail.  You will need keen creative and artistic ability with a keen eye for colour, detail and overall presentation with a strong market awareness of visual and retail merchandising techniques and best practice.

You will have excellent organisational and attention to detail and be able to meet a challenge head on, being able to adapt to dynamic situations including changing stock, layouts and customer preferences.

You must also be available to work flexibly as the Club requires, this will include evenings and weekends. This role will also be required to provide support across our various retail locations therefore a driving licence with access to a vehicle or the ability to travel between our Club sites, including Villa Park, Bullring, and Bodymoor Heath Training Ground is needed.

4. Why join us?

There has never been a better time to join Aston Villa Football Club. Our drive to succeed is visible on the global stage, we are building a family ethos and culture to be proud of and realising our ambitious plans for the expansion and improvement of our world-class facilities. If you are already excited by this challenge and want to play your part in our historic Club, then you'll be pleased to hear about our amazing staff benefits you'll receive whilst working for us! See our Staff Benefits booklet  to find out more.

 

As part of your application, you will be asked to:

1.  Upload your CV

2.  Answer the following question:

a) Please outline your proven visual merchandising experience which make you the ideal candidate for this role (400 words maximum) 

Right to Close Vacancy Posting Early

The Club reserves the right to close any advertised vacancies earlier than the advertised closing date if sufficient applications have been received.

Equality Statement

Aston Villa Football Club celebrates the diversity of its Club and embrace equal opportunities for all.

We welcome applications from all candidates regardless of age, race, disability, gender reassignment, pregnancy and maternity, sexual orientation, marriage and civil partnership, sex and religion or belief.

Safeguarding Statement 

Aston Villa Football Club is fully committed to safeguarding children and adults at risk across our Club. As such, we adhere to Safer Recruitment processes and for some roles a satisfactory enhanced disclosure via the Disclosure & Barring Service may be required prior to starting in a role at the Club. For more information, please see Aston Villa Football Club | The official club website | AVFC - Safeguarding

job.title: Senior Strategic Partner Manager
job.location: Birmingham Discipline: Professional/Technical Job type: Full Time, Permanent Salary: Attractive Remuneration Package Contact email: careers@avfc.co.uk Job ref: 011703 Published: 20 days ago Expiry date: 15 Mar 2026 23:59
job.html_description:
Department: Retail and Licensing

Hours of Work: 35 hours per week

Contract Type: Permanent

Salary: Attractive Remuneration Package

Location: Bodymoor Heath Training Ground

Closing Date: Sunday 15 March 2026

1. The Department

At Aston Villa Football Club, our Retail and Licensing department plays a vital role in supporting the club's commercial success and fan engagement. We take pride in offering a wide variety of high-quality football merchandise and leisurewear to our fans, both online and in-store, creating a strong connection to our brand and culture. This is a fantastic opportunity to be part of an innovative department that works collaboratively to bring our fans closer to the Club through products they love and continue to deliver exceptional customer experience

2. The Role

We have an amazing opportunity for a Senior Strategic Partner Manager to come and lead on all aspects  our flagship strategic partnership with adidas. You will act as a senior, single point of ownership for the relationship, ensuring the partnership delivers exceptional value across the football operations, retail performance, brand positioning and global growth.

You will shape and deliver a long-term strategy roadmap for the partnership, fulfilling all contractual rights driving best – in – class collaboration across our internal departments and ensuring seamless integration across our Men’s, Women’s and Academy teams, whilst championing innovative retail and product strategies to maximise commercial performance. You will also proactively identify opportunities to elevate Aston Villa’s profile with the adidas global organisation – positioning us as a benchmark club partnership for adidas worldwide.

For further information about the Role, please see the Role Profile.

3. The Person

You will need to have senior-level experience managing and leading global, strategic, or technical partnerships in a similar role—ideally within sport, retail, or lifestyle brands. You will also have strong commercial awareness with experience in negotiation, rights optimisation and partnership growth. You must be a highly organised, process-driven and analytical, with the ability to manage complexity across football, retail and commercial environments.

You must also have a flexible approach to working hours, including matchdays, evenings and weekends and access to a vehicle and / or the ability to travel between our Club sites; Villa Park and Bodymoor Heath Training Ground.

4. Why join us?

There has never been a better time to join Aston Villa Football Club. Our drive to succeed is visible on the global stage, we are building a family ethos and culture to be proud of, and realising our ambitious plans for the expansion and improvement of our world-class facilities. If you are already excited by this challenge and want to play your part in our historic Club, then you'll be pleased to hear about our amazing staff benefits you'll receive whilst working for us! See our Staff Benefits booklet  to find out more.

As part of your application, you will be asked to:

1. Upload your CV

2. Answer the following question:

a) Please outline your key skills, qualities and experience which make you the ideal candidate for this role (400 words maximum)

Right to Close Vacancy Posting Early

The Club reserves the right to close any advertised vacancies earlier than the advertised closing date if sufficient applications have been received.

Equality Statement

Aston Villa Football Club celebrates the diversity of its Club and embrace equal opportunities for all.

We welcome applications from all candidates regardless of age, race, disability, gender reassignment, pregnancy and maternity, sexual orientation, marriage and civil partnership, sex and religion or belief.

Safeguarding Statement 

Aston Villa Football Club is fully committed to safeguarding children and adults at risk across our Club. As such, we adhere to Safer Recruitment processes and for some roles a satisfactory enhanced disclosure via the Disclosure & Barring Service may be required prior to starting in a role at the Club. For more information, please see Aston Villa Football Club | The official club website | AVFC - Safeguarding

job.title: Retail Supervisor
job.location: Birmingham Discipline: Retail Job type: Full Time, Permanent Salary: £29,120 per annum Contact email: careers@avfc.co.uk Job ref: 011723 Published: 14 days ago Expiry date: 15 Mar 2026 23:59
job.html_description:
Department: Retail Department

Hours of Work: 40 hours a week (including evenings and weekends)

Contract Type: Permanent

Salary: £29,120 per annum

Location: Bullring Store, Birmingham

Closing Date: Sunday 15 March 2026

Interview Date: Week Commencing 30 March 2026

1. The Department

The Club are continuing our rapid growth trajectory both on and off the pitch, further expanding our commercial footprint with the launch of our brand-new retail store in the heart of Birmingham’s iconic Bullring shopping centre which opened in May 2025.

The store design has taken inspiration from our rich 150-year history incorporating cultural touchpoints from Villa Park whilst integrating a museum section that celebrates the Club’s deep-rooted connection to both Birmingham and football history.

The store spans 10,000 square feet across a ground and mezzanine floor which showcases our full retail offering including our kits, training wear and a significant adidas collection.

We are now looking to continue to build our team for the store who create a best-in-class customer experience which is inclusive and welcoming for all.

2. The Role

To support our ambitious plans, we are looking for Retail Supervisors to join our team. You will work closely with our Retail Sales Assistants, acting as a product specialist and a point of escalation. You will welcome our customers and offer any assistance required, supervising and supporting the team in ensuring the daily operations of the store run smoothly and efficiently and ultimately delivering a best – in – class service.

For further information about the Role, please see the Role Profile.

3. The Person

You will need to have proven experience working as a supervisor in a fast – paced, retail environment. You will need to have excellent customer service skills and be able to handle challenging situations, requests and queries in a professional manner.

You must also be highly efficient and organised with the ability to work to tight deadlines and have a strong attention to detail to deliver work to a high standard, achieving goals and allowing for process efficiencies in store.

You will be required to work flexibly including evenings and weekends – your weekly rota will be communicated to you in advance.

4. Why join us?

There has never been a better time to join Aston Villa Football Club. Our drive to succeed is visible on the global stage, we are building a family ethos and culture to be proud of and realising our ambitious plans for the expansion and improvement of our world-class facilities. If you are already excited by this challenge and want to play your part in our historic Club, then you'll be pleased to hear about our amazing staff benefits you'll receive whilst working for us! See our  Staff Benefits booklet  to find out more.

As part of your application, you will be asked to:

1. Upload your CV

2. Answer the following question:

a) Please outline your key skills, qualities and experience which make you the ideal candidate for this role (400 words maximum)

b)  Please outline the maximum size of a team you have managed in a retail environment (200 words maximum)

Right to Close Vacancy Posting Early

The Club reserves the right to close any advertised vacancies earlier than the advertised closing date if sufficient applications have been received.

Equality Statement

Aston Villa Football Club celebrates the diversity of its Club and embrace equal opportunities for all.

We welcome applications from all candidates regardless of age, race, disability, gender reassignment, pregnancy and maternity, sexual orientation, marriage and civil partnership, sex and religion or belief.

Safeguarding Statement 

Aston Villa Football Club is fully committed to safeguarding children and adults at risk across our Club. As such, we adhere to Safer Recruitment processes and for some roles a satisfactory enhanced disclosure via the Disclosure & Barring Service may be required prior to starting in a role at the Club. For more information, please see Aston Villa Football Club | The official club website | AVFC - Safeguarding

job.title: Retail Sales Assistants
job.location: Birmingham Discipline: Retail Job type: Full Time, Permanent Salary: £13.45 per hour Contact email: careers@avfc.co.uk Job ref: 011724 Published: 12 days ago Expiry date: 15 Mar 2026 23:59
job.html_description:
Department: Retail Department

Hours of Work: See Below

Contract Type: Permanent

Salary: £13.45 per hour

Location: Bullring Store, Birmingham

Closing Date: Sunday 15 March 2026

Interview Date: Week Commencing 23 March 2026

1. The Department

The Club are continuing our rapid growth trajectory both on and off the pitch, further expanding our commercial footprint with the launch of our brand-new retail store in the heart of Birmingham’s iconic Bullring shopping centre which opened in May 2025.

The store design has taken inspiration from our rich 150-year history incorporating cultural touchpoints from Villa Park whilst integrating a museum section that celebrates the Club’s deep-rooted connection to both Birmingham and football history.

The store spans 10,000 square feet across a ground and mezzanine floor which showcases our full retail offering including our kits, training wear and a significant adidas collection.

We are now looking to continue to build our team for the store who create a best-in-class customer experience which is inclusive and welcoming for all.

2. The Role

We have exciting opportunities for Retail Sales Assistants to join our team who will be our ‘front of house’ team creating our overall ultimate customer shopping experience. We have several different contracts available as follows:

*         Full time - 35 hours a week

*         Variable hours

You will be responsible for providing excellent customer service which will include providing support and assistance to our customers and processing sales at payment points in an efficient and friendly manner.   You will also support in replenishing stock, maintaining merchandising stands and tidying item displays.

You will use effective selling skills to highlight promotional items to customers and help achieve sales targets whilst maintaining high standards of professionalism with a ‘customer first’ approach.

For further information about the Role, please see the Role Profile.

3. The Person

We are looking for pro-active and positive individuals with a customer focus along with the ability to work towards sales targets.

You will need proven sales experience working in a similar role as well as excellent customer service skills.

Working within a fast-paced and dynamic environment, you must have excellent organisation and communication skills which includes being an active listener with the ability to be assertive and persuasive as and when required.

You will be required to work flexibly including evenings and weekends – your weekly rota will be communicated to you in advance.

4. Why join us?

There has never been a better time to join Aston Villa Football Club. Our drive to succeed is visible on the global stage, we are building a family ethos and culture to be proud of and realising our ambitious plans for the expansion and improvement of our world-class facilities. If you are already excited by this challenge and want to play your part in our historic Club, then you'll be pleased to hear about our amazing staff benefits you'll receive whilst working for us! See our  Staff Benefits booklet to find out more.

As part of your application, please ensure you upload your CV.

Right to Close Vacancy Posting Early

The Club reserves the right to close any advertised vacancies earlier than the advertised closing date if sufficient applications have been received.

Equality Statement

Aston Villa Football Club celebrates the diversity of its Club and embrace equal opportunities for all.

We welcome applications from all candidates regardless of age, race, disability, gender reassignment, pregnancy and maternity, sexual orientation, marriage and civil partnership, sex and religion or belief.

job.title: Head of Procurement
job.location: Birmingham Discipline: Professional/Technical Job type: Full Time, Permanent Salary: £70,000 - £90,000 per annum dependent upon experience Contact email: careers@avfc.co.uk Job ref: 011725 Published: 14 days ago Expiry date: 15 Mar 2026 23:59
job.html_description:
Department: Finance Department

Hours of Work: 35 hours per week

Contract Type: Permanent

Salary: £70,000 - £90,000 per annum dependent upon experience

Location: Bodymoor Heath Training Ground

Closing Date: Sunday 15 March 2026

1. The Department

The Finance Department plays a pivotal role at the Club in supporting our long-term ambitions both on and off the pitch. Operating at the heart of a fast-paced, high-performance environment, it is the engine that keeps our Club stable and able to compete sustainably.  The team provides the insight and control that underpin our decisions – from managing budgets and cash flow to overseeing player transfers and regulatory reporting.

2. The Role

We are seeking an experienced and qualified individual to join us as Head of Procurement. This is an exciting opportunity to shape the function – you will have the freedom to define and deliver the Club’s procurement strategy to reduce costs, obtain value for money and to ensure that the Club is compliant and commercially always protected. You will drive value, improve efficiency and create a procurement culture that supports long-term growth – making a visible impact on our day-to-day operations to our major strategic projects.

You will proactively engage with budget holders to understand forward requirements and source suppliers, issue RFIs and RFQs and manage procurement end-to-end to drive costs down and mitigate supply risks.

You will be responsible for analysing spend data to identify trends, aggregation opportunities and cost efficiencies and keep up to date with current procurement practices and advise on any potential supply chain risks that may affect the Club’s internal and external delivery of operations. You will also lead on developing procurement policies, templates and standard terms to ensure consistent and commercial protection.

You will oversee the Club’s fleet operations, ensuring all vehicles, documentation, insurance, servicing and compliance requirements are maintained to the highest standard. You will also manage fuel cards, traffic violations and authorised‑driver records, keeping everything accurate, up to date and fully aligned our polices.

For further information about the Role, please see the Role Profile.

3. The Person

You must hold be a member of the Chartered Institute of Procurement and Supply (MCIPS) and have significant experience of working in a senior procurement role within complex and fast paced environments.

You must have experience in managing high-value capital expenditure projects (eg. infrastructure and training round) and managing supplier due diligence processes including financial stability assessments and compliance checks.

A key element of this role will be engaging and influencing a variety of internal stakeholders at all levels of the Club including senior management and non-financial stakeholders demonstrating integrity, inclusivity and high levels of accountability and resilience.

As part of the role, you may also be required to travel therefore you will need to have access to vehicle or have the ability to travel between our Club sites: Villa Park and Bodymoor Heath Training Ground as well as travelling to visit potential suppliers as and when required.

4. Why join us?

There has never been a better time to join Aston Villa Football Club. Our drive to succeed is visible on the global stage, we are building a family ethos and culture to be proud of, and realising our ambitious plans for the expansion and improvement of our world-class facilities. If you are already excited by this challenge and want to play your part in our historic Club, then you'll be pleased to hear about our amazing staff benefits you'll receive whilst working for us! See our  Staff Benefits booklet to find out more.

As part of your application, please ensure you upload your CV.

 

Right to Close Vacancy Posting Early

The Club reserves the right to close any advertised vacancies earlier than the advertised closing date if sufficient applications have been received. 

Equality Statement

Aston Villa Football Club celebrates the diversity of its Club and embrace equal opportunities for all.

We welcome applications from all candidates regardless of age, race, disability, gender reassignment, pregnancy and maternity, sexual orientation, marriage and civil partnership, sex and religion or belief.

Safeguarding Statement 

Aston Villa Football Club is fully committed to safeguarding children and adults at risk across our Club. As such, we adhere to Safer Recruitment processes and for some roles a satisfactory enhanced disclosure via the Disclosure & Barring Service may be required prior to starting in a role at the Club. For more information, please see Aston Villa Football Club | The official club website | AVFC - Safeguarding
job.plain_text_description:
Department: Finance Department
Hours of Work: 35 hours per week
Contract Type: Permanent
Salary: £70,000 - £90,000 per annum dependent upon experience
Location: Bodymoor Heath Training Ground
Closing Date: Sunday 15 March 2026
1. The Department
The Finance Department plays a pivotal role at the Club in supporting our long-term ambitions both on and off the pitch. Operating at the heart of a fast-paced, high-performance environment, it is the engine that keeps our Club stable and able to compete sustainably. The team provides the insight and control that underpin our decisions – from managing budgets and cash flow to overseeing player transfers and regulatory reporting.
2. The Role
We are seeking an experienced and qualified individual to join us as Head of Procurement. This is an exciting opportunity to shape the function – you will have the freedom to define and deliver the Club’s procurement strategy to reduce costs, obtain value for money and to ensure that the Club is compliant and commercially always protected. You will drive value, improve efficiency and create a procurement culture that supports long-term growth – making a visible impact on our day-to-day operations to our major strategic projects.
You will proactively engage with budget holders to understand forward requirements and source suppliers, issue RFIs and RFQs and manage procurement end-to-end to drive costs down and mitigate supply risks.
You will be responsible for analysing spend data to identify trends, aggregation opportunities and cost efficiencies and keep up to date with current procurement practices and advise on any potential supply chain risks that may affect the Club’s internal and external delivery of operations. You will also lead on developing procurement policies, templates and standard terms to ensure consistent and commercial protection.
