- To develop effective strategies to recruit the right volunteers with the right skills, utilising the current intake and interview protocol for potential volunteers to ensure the best match between the skills, qualifications, and interests of the volunteers and the needs of Everton in the Community
Safeguarding
- To understand the Club’s Safeguarding policy, procedures, and best practice guidelines.
- To use this understanding to ensure safe working practices, appropriate reporting of concerns and contribute positively to an Anti-bullying environment.
Who we are looking for:
We are looking for an individual who is highly motivated with a passion for volunteering and work within the charity/community sector and who has relevant experience and a proven track record within a related field e.g. volunteer support, youth/social work, human resources, training/employment services.
With sound knowledge and awareness of trends in volunteering, including current legislation and best practice our ideal candidate will also demonstrate the below skills and experience:
- Proven ability to lead, motivate and inspire people from a range of diverse backgrounds
- Ability to assist the delivery of policy and strategy, produce, implement and monitor business/project development plans, and prepare and present clear well-structured reports
- Good confidence in using a diverse range of IT systems i.e Sales Force
- Excellent personal organisational skills, including the ability to balance competing priorities and manage a varied workload within deadlines and to maintain a high standard of personal efficiency and appearance
- Approachable and empathic, with excellent communication skills and the ability to motivate a diverse range of people to strive towards ambitious life goals
This position will be offered on Permanent Contract, with the role being based across our Everton in the Community campus.
The closing date for applications is Tuesday 24th March 2026 however, we reserve the right to close this vacancy early should we receive a substantial amount of applications
Everton Family Safer Recruitment Practices
The Everton Family is committed to safeguarding and promoting the welfare of children and young people and expects all staff and volunteers to share this commitment.
As a requirement of our safer recruitment practices, this role will require an Enhanced DBS check or evidence that you are subscribed to the DBS Update Service information of which can be found here
This role is subject to both evidence and verification of relevant qualifications including proof of eligibility to work in the UK which will be discussed with you if your application is successful.
Equity & Inclusion
Everton Football Club is committed to ensuring everyone is respected, celebrated and empowered for who they are, regardless of their identity. We welcome applications from people with diverse backgrounds, and underrepresented communities. We are dedicated to supporting the physical and mental/emotional wellbeing of all our people. Should you have a disability or long-term health condition and require reasonable adjustments to be made to the application/interview/onboarding process, please let us know by contacting the Talent Acquisition Team via email - careers@evertonfc.com
To support our pledge to diversify our organisation and through our commitment to the Football Association’s, Football Leadership Diversity Code, Everton welcomes applications from people of all walks of life. As part of our commitment to Disability, Inclusion and Accessibility we are more than happy to make reasonable adjustments to the recruitment process should you require.

job.title: Academy Driver (Part-Time)
job.plain_text_description:
The Academy Driver is responsible for providing a safe, reliable and professional driving service across. the Academy. Reporting to the Head of Academy Operations, the role includes, management of part-time drivers (exclusively Academy), and to work alongside the Club Driver & Fleet Compliance Manager ensuring all vehicles are maintained and presented to a professional standard. The Academy Driver delivers a consistent, reliable service to ensure safe transport of Academy players, staff and guests.
This will be a part-time (20-hour) role, with hours split across a 7-day week.
Responsibilities
- Provide transport for identified registered players to and from our Motspur Park training site. - Provide transport for trialist players to and from airports, train stations, accommodation, and club activities. - Support the player induction process when identifying players in need of transport support. - Act as a guide for visits to Motspur Park and Craven Cottage. - Assist the club on 1st team match days for corporate driving requirement. - Support the Academy across key events and wider activities. - Promote the work of Fulham Academy amongst the London football community. - Always represent Fulham Football Club to a high standard in line with club values.
Requirements
- Hold a full clean driving licence - Interact with others in a positive manner - Strong understanding of the local area - Positive ability to work within a diverse organisation - Self-starter who takes pride in the delivery of a professional service - Bi-Lingual (Desired) - D1 driving licence (Desired)

job.title: Sales Operations Manager, Hospitality
job.plain_text_description:
The Sales Operations Manager, Hospitality is responsible for the systems, processes, data governance, and operational readiness that the Club’s end-to-end premium sales, servicing, renewal, and matchday delivery operation rely on.
Your goal is to ensure that every part of the premium customer journey - from first enquiry through to matchday delivery and retention - is supported by disciplined workflows, accurate and trusted data, and clear cross-functional ownership.
This role exists to protect and enable premium revenue by ensuring sales activity is operationally sound, delivery-ready, and consistently executed. Your work directly supports revenue confidence, operational consistency, and industry-leading customer experience across both seasonal and match-by-match hospitality products.
This is a hands-on, cross-functional role that connects commercial performance, operational delivery, and technical systems, with a clear focus on preventing failure, reducing risk, and enabling sales teams to perform with confidence.
Responsibilities
The following is a non-exhaustive list of the responsibilities of the role:
Own systems, workflows, and operational standards:
Own, maintain, and continuously improve the systems, workflows, and operational standards that underpin premium sales, servicing, renewals, and matchday delivery, ensuring they are robust, scalable, and consistently applied.
Own revenue operations discipline:
Maintain strong operational discipline across premium sales activity, including pipeline hygiene, deal progression, forecast accuracy, and renewal readiness, proactively resolving issues that put revenue delivery or customer experience at risk.
Own CRM governance and data integrity:
Act as the operational owner of the premium CRM and related commercial systems, defining and enforcing data standards, workflows, lifecycle stages, and reporting logic so systems operate as a single source of truth.
Design and manage end-to-end customer workflows:
Design, document, and manage end-to-end premium customer workflows from enquiry through to renewal, ensuring clear ownership, consistency of execution, and scalability across teams.
Embed accountability and cross-functional alignment:
Work with sales, account management, and operational teams to assign clear ownership for key processes and handovers, partnering with Finance, Marketing, Legal, Technology, and Operations to ensure alignment in practice.
Own matchday readiness and handover:
Own the commercial-to-operations handover for premium matchdays, setting readiness cut-offs and ensuring guest data, access, product integrity, and service information are accurate, complete, and delivered on time.
Protect financial and access accuracy:
Own the operational accuracy of contracting, invoicing, payment, and access workflows across premium products, working closely with Finance to ensure clean reconciliation, minimal revenue leakage, and timely issue resolution.
Provide insight, risk management, and improvement:
Use operational and data insight to identify risks and opportunities, inform decision-making, and drive continuous improvements that strengthen revenue protection, efficiency, and customer experience.
Lead change and system adoption:
Lead the rollout and adoption of new systems, tools, and processes, ensuring clear documentation, training, and sustained adoption across commercial and operational teams.
Requirements
TheSales Operations Manager, Hospitality must have:
- Demonstrable experience in sales operations, commercial operations, or business operations, ideally within sport, venues, hospitality, or membership-led environments - Strong workflow design and operational management skills, with confidence operating in complex, revenue-critical environments - Advanced experience using and owning commercial systems (e.g. CRM, ticketing, finance, or workflow automation tools) - Strong data literacy, with the ability to build reporting, support forecasting, and translate insight into operational or commercial action - Confidence to enforce standards, challenge poor practice, and hold teams accountable where process discipline impacts revenue or delivery - High personal accountability, attention to detail, and the ability to manage multiple priorities under pressure - Flexibility to support matchday delivery and peak operational periods as required
Ideally, theSales Operations Manager, Hospitality will also have:
- Experience supporting system rollouts, process automation, or operational change in live environments - Familiarity with premium customer journeys, hospitality memberships, or seasonally renewing revenue models - Experience working across integrated systems (e.g. CRM ↔ ticketing ↔ finance ↔ access control) - A strong interest in live sport, hospitality, premium experiences, or client-led service environments

job.title: Club Driver & Fleet Compliance Manager
job.html_description:
The Club Driver is responsible for providing a safe, reliable and professional driving service across the Club and for the effective day-to-day management of the Club vehicle fleet. Reporting to the Head of Security, the role has ownership of driver eligibility and compliance (excluding Academy), management of part-time drivers (excluding Academy), and ensuring all Club vehicles are maintained, presented and operated to the required legal, insurance and Club standards. The Club Driver delivers a consistent, discreet service to First Team, Medical, Administration and Security departments, while coordinating vehicle availability with the Academy’s transport function where required.

Responsibilities

The following is a non-exhaustive list of the responsibilities of the role:

Driving & Transport Services
• Provide professional driving services within contracted hours for:
o First Team players and staff
o Medical and performance teams
o Club administration
o Security operations
• Deliver a consistent, punctual and discreet service, adapting to operational priorities and short-notice
requirements where reasonably practicable.

Fleet Management
• Take day-to-day responsibility for the Club vehicle fleet, including cars, vans and any specialist
vehicles.
• Ensure all Club vehicles are:
o Clean and presented to a professional standard
o Fuelled and/or charged as required
o Roadworthy and maintained in line with manufacturer and Club standards
• Coordinate servicing, MOTs, repairs and inspections.
• Maintain accurate records for vehicle usage, servicing and maintenance.

Club Driver Compliance & Eligibility
• Hold primary responsibility for compliance relating to drivers, including:
o Driving licence verification
o Insurance eligibility
o Internal approval and record-keeping
• Ensure only approved and eligible drivers operate Club vehicles.
• Liaise with HR, insurers and internal stakeholders to maintain compliance.
• Maintain auditable records to support internal and external reviews.

Management of Part-Time / Casual Drivers
• Manage, schedule and oversee part-time or casual drivers engaged to support Club driving
requirements.
• Ensure all drivers are appropriately approved, briefed and compliant.
• Monitor service standards and address performance or conduct issues where required.
Academy Liaison
• Act as the Club point of contact for coordination of vehicle availability and scheduling with the Academy.
• Work collaboratively with the Academy Transport & Fleet Compliance Lead to enable shared vehicle
use where appropriate.
• Ensure Club vehicles are released for Academy use only where confirmation of driver eligibility has
been provided by the Academy.
• Maintain clear records to ensure transparency of responsibility and avoid duplication of compliance
functions.

Security, Standards & Confidentiality
• Operate in line with security protocols and instructions issued by the Head of Security.
• Maintain the highest standards of discretion, confidentiality and situational awareness.
• Report any security-related incidents, concerns or breaches promptly through agreed channels.
• Represent the Club professionally at all times when interacting with players, staff, officials and external
stakeholders.

Working Hours
  • 40 Contracted hours per week with flexibility required to meet operational and security demands.
  • Some early mornings, evenings and weekends may be required.

Requirements

The Club Driver & Fleet Compliance Manager must:

• Full, clean UK driving licence.
• Proven professional driving experience.
• Experience of fleet management or transport coordination.
• Strong understanding of insurance and driver eligibility requirements.
• High levels of discretion and trustworthiness.
• Excellent organisational skills and attention to detail.
• Strong communication and interpersonal skills.

Ideally, the Club Driver & Fleet Compliance Manager will:

• Experience working within elite sport or a high-security or high-profile environment.
• Experience managing part-time or casual staff.
• Knowledge of safeguarding and security principles.

job.title: Security Officer - Day
job.html_description:
To be responsible and protect the Clubs assets, security of the grounds, its contents & the safety of staff and visitors across all Fulham Football Club & Fulham Pier premises.

Requirements

• To open and secure the premises at the correct times and in the correct manner.

• To be fully conversant with the intruder and fire alarm systems and to report any problems that may arise with these systems.

• To carry out regular & varied patrols of the grounds to deter theft, anti-social behaviour and to detect fire.

• To record all incidents and tasks undertaken during every shift. These entries will be made in the security Daily Occurrence Book.

• To report any matter having a bearing on the safety of the club and its staff and visitors.

• To inform the Security Manager of any incident this may involve dishonesty or when Company rules and regulations have been broken. 

• To carry out investigation as directed by the Security Manager involving cash, stock and property of the club and the personal effects of the staff.

• To conduct bag and vehicle searches on staff and contractors as directed by the Security Manager.

• To keep, the confidentiality of any matter concerning the club and Security department.

• To maintain a good working relationship with the staff and managers and encourage a high standard of Security.

• To maintain a smart appearance and to act in a courteous and professional manner when dealing with members of the public and staff.

• To issue keys from the security office and to record the issue and receiving of those keys in the appropriate register, reporting any missing keys or damaged locks.

• To record lost property in the lost property, register and to update the register when required.

• To ensure that all visitors are recorded in the Visitors Register, for the purpose of maintaining accurate records.

• To be fully conversant with the operation of CCTV, Access Control and Two way radio systems used by the Club.

• To be fully conversant with the Company rules and regulations, health and Safety at work and the Fire and Emergency procedures and ensure accurate logs of these are kept.

• To ensure accurate goods inwards records are kept.

• Other ad hoc duties as required by the business.

job.title: U9 – 16 Academy Physiotherapist
job.html_description:
In this role, you will be responsible for delivering physiotherapy services across all Academy squads within an interdisciplinary framework, while supporting the development and implementation of injury‑prevention and rehabilitation programmes designed to maximise player performance, enhance recovery, and reduce injury risk. Additionally, you will contribute to the effective transition of FP and YDP players into the PDP programme and ultimately towards the First Team.

Responsibilities

The following is a non-exhaustive list of the responsibilities of the role: 

 

  • Work with Academy medical staff in the assessment, treatment and rehabilitation of all injuries sustained within the Academy predominately within the FP & YDP phase.
  • Support delivery of sessions to players within PDP phase as required.
  • To liaise with the sports scientist, nutritionist, sports therapist, psychologists and other members of the medical team, regarding player performance, assessment and rehabilitation of injury and return to play.
  • To liaise and co-ordinate the with the appropriate people (including parents) to facilitate external medical management (NHS or Private Healthcare) of all players in FP & YDP phase.
  • To carry out regular comprehensive screening of all Academy players and use findings to identify injury prevention priorities and devise individual prevention/performance programmes alongside S&C team member.
  • To liaise with coaching staff regarding load management of players during phases of physical development.
  • Deliver individual and group injury prevention exercise sessions for the Academy squads based on their individual needs.
  • Design and implement injury rehabilitation programmes for Academy players, including objective markers for return to play for all players in FP & YDP.
  • Match day medical provision for U16’s, U9-15’s and PDP games as required.
  • To support the transition of FP & YDP players into the PDP phase co-ordinating with relevant staff on the players previous medical and movement history.
  • Keep accurate up to date records of all injuries sustained by players as well as all treatment, prevention and rehabilitation sessions and record them on the club medical database.
  • Collate and analyse Injury Audit data and present to medical team/coaching staff.
  • When required, accompany players to and from consultations with surgeons or specialists, scans or other medical appointments.
  • Co-ordinate cohort of part time staff working within the U9-16’s for all match day cover, overseas and premier league tournaments.
  • Maintain excellent communication and inter-departmental collaboration with all other facets of the Academy to ensure that the standards expected through the EPPP are upheld at all times
  • Participate and deliver CPD sessions within the team, contributing to the research and development aspect of paediatric sports medicine within FFC’s elite development programme.
  • Undertake training and development courses to keep up to date with current research and developments in sports medicine and progress through stages of personal progression.

Applications close on Monday 30th March.

Requirements

The u9 – 16 Academy Physiotherapist must: 

  • Experience of working within sport.
  • Experience of working within a multi-disciplinary team.
  • BSc degree in Physiotherapy
  • Member of the Chartered Society of Physiotherapy
  • HCPC Registered
  • Able to work as part of team
  • Flexible, enthusiastic, reliable & organised

 

Ideally, the u9 – 16 Physiotherapist will: 
  • Experience and understanding of paediatric sports medicine
  • Experience of working within a football academy.
  • FA safeguarding certification
  • Working towards MSc

job.title: IT Support Analyst
job.html_description:
An exciting opportunity has arisen for an IT Support Analyst to join our IT team based at our training Ground at Motspur Park. The position purpose is to provide 1st/2nd line technical and operational support for all IT hardware and services throughout Fulham Football Club. Supporting users both remotely and on site.

Responsibilities

Responsibilities include but are not limited to:

· Respond to 1st and 2nd line technical support queries professionally and efficiently, maintaining a high degree of     customer service

· Escalate 3rd line support queries effectively including the correct troubleshooting steps

· Support users in the use of computer equipment by providing necessary training and advice

· Take ownership of assigned issues, perform technical diagnosis and resolve, either on-site or remotely

· Arrange external/third-party support when relevant following our support contracts

· Perform daily maintenance tasks on all IT services as required by Fulham Football Club

· Perform hardware & software installations, upgrades, updates, configurations and troubleshooting.

· Maintain Asset Register for all hardware devices/software licences.

· Provide out of hours support as part of a rota.

· Provide Matchday support as part of a rota.

· Produce documentation on troubleshooting steps to aid all team members.

Requirements

The following qualifications and experience are essential:

· Windows 10/11, MacOS

· MS Office 365 Suite

· Installation/Configuration/Troubleshooting of PC/Laptop/Mac hardware/software

· Basic Active Directory account administration

· Understanding of Backup Technologies

· Mobile devices, iOS, Android

· Working in a high-pressure environment including supporting VIPs

· Experience working On Call and out of hours


The following qualifications and experience are desirable:

· Experience in understanding VLAN’s and Network topologies

· Experience in JAMF and/or Intune

· Experience with Oracle or other POS related software


Candidates should have the ability to work collaboratively with colleagues, aiding where necessary. It is essential that candidates are proactive, punctual/flexible, and eager to learn and improve.

job.title: Host Family
job.html_description:
Are you passionate about football and looking for a unique opportunity to make a difference in the lives of young athletes, aged 15-21?


Fulham FC is seeking warm and welcoming host families to provide a safe and supportive home environment to support our Academy players who will be required to live away from home to fulfil their education and football training at our club.


As a host parent, you will play an essential role in helping our players thrive both on and off the pitch. You'll provide a comfortable living space, home-cooked meals, emotional support and help them to adjust to life away from home as they focus on their training and academic pursuits.


What we are looking for:
  • A friendly, caring family or individual with a spare room
  • A willingness to provide support, guidance, and encouragement to our young players
  • A safe and welcoming home environment
  • Support our young people by teaching them basic independent living and self-care skills such as washing, cooking and cleaning up after themselves.
  • Providing meals in line with nutritional guidance from the Academy.
  • Accommodation has access to public and local amenities with good transport links to the Academy training ground.
  • Report any welfare concerns to the Academy Safeguarding and Player Care team should they occur.
  • Include our young people in family life as much as they feel comfortable with.
  • The club will regularly inspect and visit the placement during the season.
  • The club will provide further guidance to host families, young people and their parents explaining what would be expected of them.

In order to be considered for this position you must live within 30 minutes travel for the player from the training ground.

Fulham Football Club is committed to safeguarding and ensuring the welfare and protection of children and young people and expects host families to share this commitment. As such, the host family provider will be subject to an Enhanced Disclosure & Barring Service (DBS) check and risk assessments prior to a placement commencing.


Any other person over the age of 18, who resides in the household or has regular contact with the player will be required to complete an enhanced DBS. Host Families will be required to attend safeguarding training and workshops arranged by the Academy.


We are committed to supporting an environment where all staff have a personal responsibility to uphold the Club’s Equal Opportunities Policy by treating fellow employees, prospective employees, casual workers, prospective casual workers, players, prospective players and customers fairly and impartially.

As a host parent, you will play an essential role in helping our players thrive both on and off the pitch. You'll provide a comfortable living space, home-cooked meals, emotional support and help them to adjust to life away from home as they focus on their training and academic pursuits.
What we are looking for:
- A friendly, caring family or individual with a spare room - A willingness to provide support, guidance, and encouragement to our young players - A safe and welcoming home environment - Support our young people by teaching them basic independent living and self-care skills such as washing, cooking and cleaning up after themselves. - Providing meals in line with nutritional guidance from the Academy. - Accommodation has access to public and local amenities with good transport links to the Academy training ground. - Report any welfare concerns to the Academy Safeguarding and Player Care team should they occur. - Include our young people in family life as much as they feel comfortable with. - The club will regularly inspect and visit the placement during the season. - The club will provide further guidance to host families, young people and their parents explaining what would be expected of them.
In order to be considered for this position you must live within 30 minutes travel for the player from the training ground.
Fulham Football Club is committed to safeguarding and ensuring the welfare and protection of children and young people and expects host families to share this commitment. As such, the host family provider will be subject to an Enhanced Disclosure & Barring Service (DBS) check and risk assessments prior to a placement commencing.
Any other person over the age of 18, who resides in the household or has regular contact with the player will be required to complete an enhanced DBS. Host Families will be required to attend safeguarding training and workshops arranged by the Academy.
We are committed to supporting an environment where all staff have a personal responsibility to uphold the Club’s Equal Opportunities Policy by treating fellow employees, prospective employees, casual workers, prospective casual workers, players, prospective players and customers fairly and impartially.

job.title: Night Security Officer
job.html_description:
• To open and secure the premises at the correct times and in the correct manner.

• To be fully conversant with the intruder and fire alarm systems and to report any problems that may arise with these systems.

• To carry out regular & varied patrols of the grounds to deter theft, anti-social behaviour and to detect fire.

• To record all incidents and tasks undertaken during every shift. These entries will be made in the security Daily Occurrence Book.

• To report any matter having a bearing on the safety of the club and its staff and visitors.

• To inform the Security Manager of any incident this may involve dishonesty or when Company rules and regulations have been broken. 

• To carry out investigation as directed by the Security Manager involving cash, stock and property of the club and the personal effects of the staff.

• To conduct bag and vehicle searches on staff and contractors as directed by the Security Manager.

• To keep, the confidentiality of any matter concerning the club and Security department.

• To maintain a good working relationship with the staff and managers and encourage a high standard of Security.

• To maintain a smart appearance and to act in a courteous and professional manner when dealing with members of the public and staff.

• To issue keys from the security office and to record the issue and receiving of those keys in the appropriate register, reporting any missing keys or damaged locks.

• To record lost property in the lost property, register and to update the register when required.

• To ensure that all visitors are recorded in the Visitors Register, for the purpose of maintaining accurate records.

• To be fully conversant with the operation of CCTV, Access Control and Two way radio systems used by the Club.

• To be fully conversant with the Company rules and regulations, health and Safety at work and the Fire and Emergency procedures and ensure accurate logs of these are kept.

• To ensure accurate goods inwards records are kept.

• Other ad hoc duties as required by the business.


Requirements

Essential
  • Experience of working independently
  • Excellent attention to detail
  • One year’s previous security experience
  • SIA Door Supervisor Licence  
  • Full and clean drivers licence
  • Competent PC skills, word and excel
  • Flexible attitude to work tasks
  • Respect for confidentiality at all times
  • Genuine commitment to high quality client service
  • Honest and Reliable

Preferred
​​​​​​​​​• Working knowledge of Genetec Security Centre or Similar VMS & Security Software.
• Previous experience of working in an environment offering 1st class customer experience. 
• SIA CCTV Licence
• SIA Door Supervisor Licence
• ACT online 
• Customer Focussed
• Strong attention to detail

job.title: Network Engineer
job.html_description:
As a Network Engineer at Fulham Football Club, you’ll assist in maintaining and improving the Club’s network infrastructure across all sites, including offices, training grounds, and Craven Cottage stadium. You’ll ensure that the Club’s wired and wireless networks are reliable, secure, and performant, supporting everything from day-to-day business operations to matchday and broadcast requirements.

 

You’ll work closely with the Infrastructure Manager to deliver robust connectivity solutions, resolve complex network issues, and contribute to ongoing network upgrades and projects. The role will work alongside our third party network support partner to perform the hands-on activity required on site. 

Responsibilities

The following is a non-exhaustive list of the responsibilities of the role: 

•  Work alongside our MSP to maintain, monitor, and support all Club network infrastructure (LAN, WAN, WLAN, firewalls, VPNs, and internet connectivity).
•  Troubleshoot and resolve complex network incidents, acting as an escalation point for the Service Desk.
•  Support matchday and event operations by ensuring stable, high-performance connectivity for staff, broadcast, and operational systems.
•  Install network switches, routers, firewalls, and wireless access points. This role will provide hands and eyes for troubleshooting and perform emergency hardware swap outs.
•  Assist in monitoring network performance, capacity, and security using appropriate tools.
•  Ensure regular hygience tasks are completed such as monitoring licences, certificates etc. 
•  Assist in implementing network improvements, upgrades, and expansions.
•  Work with MSP to maintain accurate documentation of network topology, configurations, and assets. Work with the service desk to provide training and knowledge share on 1st / 2nd line troubleshooting. 
•  Working to network security best practices, including access controls and patching.
•  Collaborate with the Infrastructure team to support integration between network and server environments.
•  Collaborate with the MSP to drive network initiatives and standards, building a strong relationship with the MSP. 
•  Supporting OOH upgrades, managing internal comms, testing and CAB requirements. 
•  Assist the infrastructure manager in looking at solutions, new hardware and opportunities. Providing in depth feedback and recommendations. 

Requirements

The Network Engineer must: 

•  3 years of experience in network engineering.
•  Strong understanding of networking concepts (TCP/IP, VLANs, routing, switching, DNS, DHCP, NAT).
•  Hands-on experience with enterprise-grade network hardware, preferably Cisco. 
•  Experience managing firewalls, VPNs, and wireless access points.
•  Familiarity with network monitoring and troubleshooting tools.
•  Knowledge of network security principles and practices.
•  Experience with structured cabling, patching, and rack management.
•  Strong problem-solving and analytical skills.
•  Ability to work under pressure during live events or matchdays.


Desirable Skills:
•  Experience supporting multi-site or event-driven environments.
•  Exposure to cloud networking.
•  Familiarity network automation tools.
•  Understanding of VoIP systems and QoS configuration.
•  Scripting or automation knowledge.
•  CCNA or equivalent certification.

Qualifications
•  Degree, diploma, or equivalent experience in IT, Computer Networking, or related field.
•  Relevant certifications (Cisco CCNA/CCNP, CompTIA Network+, Fortinet NSE) preferred.

job.title: DisAbility Foundation Coach (20hrs)
job.html_description:
DisAbility Foundation Coach – Fulham FC Foundation
Fulham Football Club Foundation is looking for an inspiring, enthusiastic, and ambitious coach with a genuine passion and desire to work with children, young people, and adults with disabilities and additional needs. This is an exciting opportunity to join a dedicated team delivering high-quality sports and enrichment programmes.

Role Overview
As a DisAbility Foundation Coach, you will deliver engaging Physical Education and community football sessions across a variety of programmes, including Pan DisAbility Hubs, Active Autism, DS Badgers, Frame Football, Blind Football, and Pan DisAbility Soccer Schools.

Working closely with the Senior DisAbility Development Manager, you will support curriculum development, programme delivery, and teacher training. You will also build strong relationships with schools and community partners, contribute to holiday programmes, and ensure the highest level of customer service. The role typically involves working Monday to Saturday, with occasional Sundays depending on need.

What We’re Looking For
We are seeking a coach with experience in delivering football, PE, and extracurricular activities to children and young people with disabilities and additional needs. The ideal candidate will be a confident communicator, well organised, and passionate about the Foundation’s mission. You will be a positive role model, demonstrating excellent personal conduct, strong time management, and the ability to work independently.

Salary: £12,850 - £13,700                       Closing date for applications: 22 March 2026

Responsibilities

- Act as an ambassador for FFCF, building and maintaining strong relationships with participants, parents/carers, partner organisations, and colleagues to ensure exceptional experiences. 

- Plan and deliver high-quality PE lessons, extra-curricular, and community activities in line with the national curriculum, NGB guidelines, and FFCF standards. 

- Collaborate with the Senior DisAbility Development Manager to develop and enhance the curriculum for Schools, Colleges, SEN Units, and Resource Bases.

 - Lead the delivery of Pan DisAbility Hubs, disability-specific sessions, Pan DisAbility Soccer Schools, and other Foundation programmes. - Facilitate smooth transitions between school-based activities, wider Foundation programmes, and community opportunities. 

- Identify and support progression pathways for talented young people with disabilities, including entry into the Para Talent Hub. 

- Oversee data collection and monitoring to ensure accurate and timely reporting for programme evaluation and improvement. - Work with the Senior DisAbility Development Manager to maintain programme standards, achieve attendance targets, and drive promotion of FFCF activities. 

- Ensure the efficient operation of the DisAbility Department, collaborating across programmes in line with Foundation values and strategic objectives.

Requirements

Qualifications & Skills
Essential:
  • Experience working with children, young people, or adults with disabilities/additional needs
  • Minimum Coaching Qualification  - Introduction to Coaching Football
  • FA  First Aid
  • FA  Safeguarding
  • Ability to travel independently
Desirable:
  • UEFA C/B Coaching Qualifications
  • FA Level 3 Supporting the Delivery of PE & School Sport
  • Non-football NGB sports qualifications
  • Other disability/Paralympic sports qualifications

job.title: Maintenance Engineer – Electrician
job.html_description:
Position Purpose
 
Provide a comprehensive high standard of Maintenance support to the Club and proactively identify areas for improvement and develop and implement solutions 


Responsibilities

Essential and Desirable
 

The Maintenance Engineer – Electrician must: 

 

  • Experience of electrical installation within a large commercial environment 
  • NVQ level 1, 2, 3 in Electrical Installation
  • 18th Edition

Requirements

Requirements and Responsibilities
 

The following is a non-exhaustive list of the responsibilities of the role: 

 

  • To carry out electrical work, both reactive and planned maintenance

  • To carry out general day to day maintenance duties, which will sometimes be on very tight deadlines and to a high-level finish
  • General planned maintenance is divided between the team dependant on site coverage
  • Site electrical installations and upgrade works
  • Assist in ad-hoc projects where required
  • Matchday preparation carried out 24-72hrs prior to each game
  • Matchday & Event cover along with other team members
  • To ensure that all work is carried out in a safe manner and within relevant Health and Safety Guidelines
  • Covering Events where required

job.title: Academy Scout
job.html_description:
The Academy Scout (Local Recruitment) plays a vital role in identifying and monitoring talented young players within the local area, whether from grassroots clubs or other academies.


Working as part of the local Academy scouting team, you will be responsible for producing detailed player reports, sharing insights with Academy staff, and contributing to a comprehensive understanding of the player landscape within your assigned region.


We have a particular interest in recruiting scouts with strong networks and knowledge within the following areas:

  • Grassroots: South London, West London

  • Professional Academies: East London, North London

Responsibilities

• Manage your individual weekend schedule and attend fixtures within your allocated region or phase.
• Observe and report on players demonstrating potential within your area and age phase.
• Network with other local scouts to exchange information and intelligence on potential targets.
• Keep Local Recruitment Managers updated on high-potential players within your area.
• Support communication with families of players involved in Talent ID programmes and trial processes.
• Assist in the delivery of Talent ID events and scouting programmes as required.
• Contribute to scouting team meetings to review succession planning and player targets.
• Attend department meetings, CPD sessions, and all required safeguarding training.

Requirements

• Strong knowledge and network within your designated local area.
• Previous experience scouting for another Academy (Category 1, 2, or 3).
• FA Talent ID Level 1 qualification.
• FA Safeguarding certification.

job.title: Academy Driver (Full Time)
job.html_description:
The Academy Driver is responsible for providing a safe, reliable and professional driving service across. the Academy. Reporting to the Head of Academy Operations, the role includes, management of part-time drivers (exclusively Academy), and to work alongside the Club Driver & Fleet Compliance Manager ensuring all vehicles are maintained and presented to a professional standard. The Academy Driver delivers a consistent, reliable service to ensure safe transport of Academy players, staff and guests.

Responsibilities

  • Provide transport for identified registered players to and from our Motspur Park training site.
  • Provide transport for trialist players to and from airports, train stations, accommodation, and club activities.
  • Manage the working hours of all part time drivers to best deliver all Academy transport requests.
  • Support the player induction process when identifying players in need of transport support.
  • Act as a guide for visits to Motspur Park and Craven Cottage.
  • Assist the club on 1st team match days for corporate driving requirement.
  • Support the Academy across key events and wider activities.
  • Promote the work of Fulham Academy amongst the London football community.
  • Always represent Fulham Football Club to a high standard in line with club values.

Requirements

  • Hold a full clean driving licence
  • Interact with others in a positive manner
  • Strong understanding of the local area
  • Positive ability to work within a diverse organisation
  • Self-starter who takes pride in the delivery of a professional service
  • Bi-Lingual (Desired)
  • D1 driving licence (Desired)

job.title: Financial Reporting & Compliance Manager
job.html_description:
The Financial Reporting & Compliance Manager is a key role in the Fulham Group’s finance function.  We are seeking a highly skilled finance professional to join our finance team and play a key role in financial reporting, compliance and regulatory submissions in support of the Club’s strategic objectives.

 

Although our brand is most closely associated with Premier League football, the Fulham Group now comprises three main businesses: 

 

  1. Fulham Football Club, incorporating the First Team and Academy; 
  2. The Fulham Football Club Foundation, and   
  3. Fulham Pier, a new 365 day venue on the banks of the River Thames, comprising bars, restaurants, conference and events spaces, a hotel and a spa.  

  

The Finance Reporting & Compliance Manager, reporting to the Financial Controller, will lead the preparation and delivery of comprehensive financial reports and ensure full compliance with statutory, Premier League, and UEFA regulatory requirements. You will work closely with internal stakeholders, lead external audit processes, and support the finance leadership in maintaining best-in-class financial controls and reporting processes. 

Responsibilities

Working as the Finance Reporting & Compliance Manager in a business incorporating a high-profile, Premier League football club, is a unique role. It requires a diligent and a detail-oriented team player, who embraces challenges, has the ability to teach and learn, and thrives on working in fast-paced environment.  We are looking to hire someone with the drive and capacity to deliver a strong controls environment, whilst at the same time not being afraid to get their hands dirty. 

 

The following is a non-exhaustive list of the responsibilities of the role: 

 

Financial Reporting & Compliance

  • Prepare and deliver accurate statutory financial statements for interim and year-end reporting.
  • Lead the annual audit process, manage external auditors, and oversee audit deliverables.
  • Prepare and review monthly balance sheet reconciliations
  • Ensure compliance with Premier League financial regulations (including SCR and PSR where applicable), UEFA submissions and statutory requirements 
  • Support the creation of monthly and quarterly financial reporting packs, balance sheet reconciliations, and variance analysis.
  • Support the preparation of the annual budget and reforecast cycles.
  • Manage the transactional finance team, ensuring ledgers are well maintained and transactions are recorded in a timely and accurate manner.

 

Controls & Process Improvement

  • Actively review existing procedures and proactively recommend improvements where deficiencies are identified and lead the change process.
  • Maintain and enhance financial controls, policies, and procedures across the Club.
  • Drive continuous improvement in reporting processes and financial systems.
 

Stakeholder Engagement

  • Collaborate with senior leadership, commercial and operational teams to support business decision-making.
  • Serve as the finance lead for regulatory submissions to governing bodies including the Premier League and UEFA.
  • Help to support and drive a service-led finance function, acting as a strong advocate for the finance function across the business.

Requirements

The Financial Reporting & Compliance Manager should have: 

 

  • An accountancy qualification (ACA, ACCA, CIMA or equivalent) with preferably 3+ years PQE
  • Experience managing audit processes and compliance submissions.
  • Excellent technical accounting knowledge and reporting skills.
  • Excellent analytical skills, with attention to accuracy and detail. 
  • Someone who enjoys a fast-paced environment, dynamic environment. 
  • Enjoys managing and supporting others in their development.
  • An interest in football is a plus but not essential.

- Actively review existing procedures and proactively recommend improvements where deficiencies are identified and lead the change process. - Maintain and enhance financial controls, policies, and procedures across the Club. - Drive continuous improvement in reporting processes and financial systems.
Stakeholder Engagement
- Collaborate with senior leadership, commercial and operational teams to support business decision-making. - Serve as the finance lead for regulatory submissions to governing bodies including the Premier League and UEFA. - Help to support and drive a service-led finance function, acting as a strong advocate for the finance function across the business.
Requirements
The Financial Reporting & Compliance Manager should have:
- An accountancy qualification (ACA, ACCA, CIMA or equivalent) with preferably 3+ years PQE - Experience managing audit processes and compliance submissions. - Excellent technical accounting knowledge and reporting skills. - Excellent analytical skills, with attention to accuracy and detail. - Someone who enjoys a fast-paced environment, dynamic environment. - Enjoys managing and supporting others in their development. - An interest in football is a plus but not essential.

job.title: Account & Membership Experience Executive, Hospitality
job.plain_text_description:
The Account & Member Experience Executive, Hospitality supports the delivery of a high-quality, consistent, and detail-led hospitality member and guest experience across The Riverside, working closely with the Account Management and Member Experience teams.
The role helps ensure premium members receive professional, reliable and exceptional service across day-to-day interactions, matchdays, and year-round events. You will act as a trusted operational support, owning defined member interactions and tasks through to completion, and ensuring issues are followed up, resolved, or escalated appropriately.
This is a hands-on role requiring strong organisation, attention to detail, and a clear sense of accountability. Your work directly contributes to member satisfaction, retention, and the overall credibility of The Riverside’s premium hospitality experience.
Responsibilities
The following is a non-exhaustive list of the responsibilities of the role:
Day-to-day member servicing:
Handle member enquiries across email, phone and in person, ensuring communication is clear, accurate and followed up in a timely manner, escalating issues where required.
Matchday delivery:
Deliver on-site experience assistance across hospitality areas on matchdays, handling member queries, assisting with experience moments and escalating issues in line with agreed procedures.
Benefits, events and activations:
Assist with the planning and delivery of member benefits, events and matchday activations, including logistics preparation, running orders, supplier coordination and on-site delivery.
CRM administration and accuracy:
Maintain accurate and up-to-date member records in MS Dynamics, logging interactions, actions and outcomes promptly to support effective servicing and renewals.
Renewal activity:
Prepare renewal materials, gather required information and assist with follow-up activity during renewal periods to support Account Managers and retention outcomes.
Operational coordination:
Coordinate defined tasks with Ticketing, Hospitality Operations, Finance and Legal, ensuring information is accurate, deadlines are met and issues are escalated appropriately.
Member communications:
Draft and prepare member-facing communications, including newsletters, event invitations and offers, ensuring accuracy, clarity and alignment with approved messaging.
Feedback and reporting:
Capture member feedback, track attendance and sentiment indicators, and prepare simple summaries to support service improvement.
Year-round experience delivery:
Assist with the delivery of non-matchday events and Fulham Pier activations, coordinating guest lists, logistics and on-site requirements.
Service standards:
Deliver consistently professional, detail-led service across all interactions, recognising the importance of reliability and presentation in a premium environment.
Requirements
TheAccount & Member Experience Executive, Hospitalitymust have:
- Experience in customer service, hospitality, events, or a similar client-facing role, ideally within a premium or service-led environment. - Strong communication skills across written, verbal, and in-person interactions. - Excellent organisation and attention to detail, with the ability to manage multiple tasks and priorities reliably. - A proactive mindset with a clear sense of ownership, accountability, and follow-through. - Confidence using digital systems; CRM experience (ideally MS Dynamics) is beneficial. - A calm, professional approach on matchdays, including availability to work evenings and weekends. - A genuine commitment to delivering high-quality service and contributing to a premium member experience.
Ideally, the Account & Member Experience Executive, Hospitality will also have:
- Experience supporting premium hospitality, membership, or event programmes. - An understanding of high-end service expectations in hospitality, sport, or live events. - Familiarity with ticketing systems (e.g. Ticketmaster XR or equivalent). - An interest in football, live events, or membership-led environments. - Experience working across multiple internal teams to deliver service touchpoints, benefits, or events.

job.title: Senior Sales Executive, Hospitality
job.html_description:
The Senior Sales Executive, Hospitality is responsible for delivering premium hospitality revenue across  both match-by-match experiences and seasonal hospitality memberships, working with some of the Club’s  warmest and most valuable audiences, including inbound enquiries, previous buyers and existing  relationships. 

You’ll operate as a trusted, high-performing seller, leading consultative, value-led sales conversations that  convert interest into repeat matchday visits and longer-term seasonal commitments, while supporting the  development of Sales Executives through informal mentoring and best practice. 

This is a senior individual contributor role, requiring confidence handling high-value enquiries, guiding  buying decisions across different usage types, and positively influencing team standards and performance.

Responsibilities

The following is a non-exhaustive list of the responsibilities of the role:

Deliver premium hospitality sales: Convert inbound enquiries, warm leads and previous buyers into 
confirmed matchday bookings and seasonal hospitality memberships.

Own personal revenue performance: Deliver against an individual sales target across both 
matchday and seasonal products, maintaining strong sales pace and conversion.

Own complex and high-value opportunities: Take responsibility for higher-value, more complex or 
at-risk deals, ensuring clear progression, pricing confidence and timely closure.

Provide day-to-day coaching and escalation: Act as a coaching and escalation point for Sales 
Executives, supporting live opportunities, objection handling and deal strategy.

Lead by example: Demonstrate consistently high standards of preparation, follow-up, CRM discipline and client care, reinforcing premium sales standards.

Support onboarding and development: Accelerate the effectiveness of Sales Executives through 
shadowing, live feedback and best-practice guidance.

Ensure opportunity progression: Maintain clear next steps, timelines and accountability across all 
opportunities, intervening directly where momentum stalls.

Maintain pipeline quality: Ensure opportunities are accurately qualified, staged and updated to 
support reliable forecasting.

Reduce opportunity leakage and slippage: Proactively identify and address stalled, at-risk or 
underperforming opportunities.

Work closely with the Sales Lead, Hospitality: Deliver agreed priorities, campaigns and focus 
areas, escalating only where appropriate.

Collaborate cross-functionally: Work with Marketing and CRM teams to execute fixture-led 
campaigns and targeted outreach activity.

Ensure clean handovers: Pass complete and accurate information into Account Management and 
Operations teams to support strong onboarding and delivery.

Champion continuous improvement: Identify recurring objections, process gaps or proposition 
weaknesses and contribute constructively to improvements.

Represent the Club: Act as a credible, professional ambassador across all customer touchpoints, 
including selected matchdays, events and client appointments.

Requirements

The Senior Sales Executive, Hospitality must have:
• Proven experience in a sales or revenue-generating role, ideally within hospitality, premium services or live events
• Strong consultative selling skills, with confidence handling high-value or complex enquiries
• Experience managing a personal sales pipeline, including follow-up, qualification and closing
• The credibility to mentor and support Sales Executives through example and informal coaching
• Clear, professional communication skills across phone, email and in-person interactions
• Strong organisation and attention to detail
• Confidence using CRM systems to manage opportunities (MS Dynamics or similar desirable)
• A calm, professional approach in live environments, including evenings and weekends
• A client-first mindset and commitment to premium standards

Ideally, the Senior Sales Executive, Hospitality will also have:
• Experience selling premium hospitality, memberships or high-value events
• Understanding of high-end service expectations in sport, hospitality or live entertainment
• Familiarity with ticketing or inventory-based sales systems (e.g. Ticketmaster)
• Experience supporting repeat business, upgrades or renewals
• Prior experience onboarding or informally coaching colleagues
• An interest in football, live sport or entertainment environments

job.title: Business Development Lead, Hospitality
job.plain_text_description:
The Business Development Lead, Hospitality is accountable for delivering net-new premium revenue across The Riverside’s seasonal and matchday hospitality experiences. This is a pure new business role, focused entirely on acquiring and converting new premium customers across seasonal memberships and ad-hoc experiences.
Leading a dedicated new business function, you will develop and own our end-to-end proactive acquisition strategy: identifying priority audiences, building clear outreach and engagement plans, setting proactive activity standards, and converting strong prospects into long-term clients. You will drive sustainable premium new revenue growth, working closely with the Head of Sales & Business Development and cross-functionally to keep all new business activity consistent and aligned with wider commercial objectives.
Responsibilities
Drive new premium revenue:
Lead the acquisition of new customers for The Riverside’s seasonal and matchday hospitality experiences, taking full accountability for individual and team revenue targets and activity.
Build and manage a high-quality pipeline:
Maintain clear oversight of pipeline value, progression, conversion rates and sales cycle times, ensuring opportunities progress at pace, conversion improves over time, and forecasts remain accurate and aligned with revenue goals.
Develop audience-level growth plans:
Identify and prioritise key target segments (e.g. corporates, SMEs, HNWIs, leisure buyers, existing fans) and build focused outreach and engagement strategies for each.
Lead and develop your team:
Manage and coach a small team to deliver excellent prospecting, consistent outreach and strong conversion performance.
Set and track activity standards:
Establish clear expectations and frameworks for high-quality outbound activity and monitor KPIs such as contact volume, outreach quality, pipeline movement and conversion to keep performance strong.
Collaborate on commercial insight:
Work closely with Sales, Marketing and Account Management teams to provide regular updates on buyer behaviour, lead sources and pipeline trends to support wider commercial planning.
Support product and pricing decisions:
Use customer feedback and market insight to help shape premium product positioning, pricing tiers, and new offer design.
Activate campaign-led acquisition:
Partner with Marketing and CRM to deliver internal and external lead-generation campaigns and workflows that build and progress the new business pipeline.
Maintain CRM excellence:
Ensure all activity logs and pipeline data are accurate and up to date to support reliable reporting and decision-making.
External representation:
Attend a wide variety of trade shows, exhibitions, industry events and networking opportunities across relevant sectors to promote Fulham FC’s premium offering and connect with new prospects.
Requirements
TheBusiness Development Lead, Hospitality must have:
- Experience leading a new-business or business-development team in a premium, experience-led or B2B environment.
- A strong track record of securing and converting high-value new business against clear revenue targets. - Confident team leader and coach, able to set high standards and drive consistent prospecting, outreach quality and conversion. - Strong CRM skills (Microsoft Dynamics preferred), with hands-on pipeline management, forecasting and data accuracy. - Skilled consultative seller, comfortable engaging senior decision-makers across multiple segments. - A keen eye for process improvement, able to refine workflows, remove friction and raise the quality and efficiency of new-business activity. - Strong communication and relationship-building skills, able to represent the Club confidently at all levels. - Excellent organisation and discipline, able to manage multiple opportunities and maintain momentum.
Ideally, theBusiness Development Lead, Hospitality will also have:
- An established network of relevant hospitality buyers and corporate decision-makers, including a useful black book of active buyers. - Experience within London’s premium corporate, lifestyle or hospitality markets. - Exposure to membership or subscription-led commercial models. - Understanding of segmentation, profiling and value-based pricing. - Experience shaping outbound messaging, sales collateral or outreach strategies. - Confidence representing a premium brand at trade shows, exhibitions, industry events and networking opportunities.

job.title: Sales Executive, Hospitality
job.plain_text_description:
The Sales Executive, Hospitality plays a frontline role in driving premium hospitality revenue across both match-by-match experiences and seasonal hospitality memberships, converting enquiries into bookings and first-time buyers into repeat guests.
This is a fast-paced, target-driven role suited to someone who enjoys engaging customers, building relationships and closing value-led sales, while developing their skills in a premium, experience-led environment.
You’ll be trusted with real responsibility, clear targets and a genuine opportunity to build a career in sales within premium sports hospitality and live events.
Responsibilities
Engage existing customers and enquiries:
Confidently convert previous buyers, returning guests and inbound enquiries into the most appropriate matchday or seasonal hospitality experience through consultative, value-led selling.
Build repeat behaviour:
Develop familiarity and credibility over time, driving repeat matchday bookings and progression into seasonal hospitality memberships where appropriate.
Own opportunities end to end:
Take full ownership of enquiries from first contact through to confirmed matchday bookings or seasonal commitments, maintaining pace through disciplined follow-up and clear next steps.
Proactively re-engage customers:
Reconnect with past guests around fixtures, availability and relevant matchday or seasonal offers, aligned to team priorities and sales campaigns.
Run effective sales conversations:
Lead structured, well-prepared discussions that focus on how customers plan to use hospitality - whether on a match-by-match basis or across a season - and the value it delivers.
Deliver against targets:
Consistently convert interest into confirmed sales, achieving individual revenue and conversion targets across both matchday and seasonal products.
Maintain a clean pipeline:
Keep CRM activity, preferences, opportunities and outcomes accurate and up to date to support reliable forecasting and follow-up.
Develop through coaching:
Work closely with senior sales colleagues, taking on feedback and applying it quickly to improve performance and results.
Support seamless delivery:
Ensure complete, accurate handovers to hospitality operations and experience teams post-sale.
Represent the Club:
Act as a credible, professional ambassador across all customer touchpoints, including selected matchdays, events and client appointments.
Raise sales standards:
Contribute ideas, insight and feedback that improve how the team sells, engages customers and drives revenue.
Requirements
TheSales Executive, Hospitalitymust have:
- A confident, professional communication style and clear comfort holding sales conversations with customers - Experience in a premium sales, hospitality, membership or service-led role, with accountability for outcomes - Strong organisation and follow-up discipline; able to manage multiple conversations without losing momentum - A results-oriented mindset and willingness to work to clear targets - Confidence using CRM or digital systems to manage revenue-driving activity and opportunities - The ability to remain calm, credible and focused in live, fast-paced environments - Flexibility to work evenings and weekends as required by the fixture calendar - A genuine interest in delivering high-quality customer experiences, not just closing transactions
Ideally, theSales Executive, Hospitalitywill also have:
- Experience selling hospitality, events or premium experiences - Familiarity with ticketing, membership or inventory-led sales environments - Experience working to revenue, conversion or activity targets - An interest in football, live sport or live entertainment environments

job.title: Casual Football & Sports Coach
job.html_description:
Fulham FC Foundation are currently seeking to recruit an enthusiastic and committed person to take up the casual position of Football & Sports Coach.

Reporting directly to our Operations Officers the Football & Sports Coaches are responsible for delivering high-quality football & sports coaching to children, young people and adults. This role involves delivery across a wide-range of FFCF programmes, including Skills Club, Development Centres, Soccer Schools, Primary Education, Kicks, Health & Wellbeing and DisAbility. This is a casual role where flexibility is essential, as the role necessitates involvement in supporting the delivery of programme activities as and when required.  

The successful candidate will be somebody who has prior experience delivering football & sports coaching sessions to children & young people in grassroots & school settings, holds at least a FA Level 1/Introduction to Coaching Football qualification and is committed to the charitable purpose of Fulham FC Foundation. 

As this role is casual, there is no set timetable. Sessions may be offered on an adhoc or regular basis, depending on the needs of Fulham FC Foundation. It is the responsibility of the Football & Sports Coaches to maintain consistent and effective communication with our Operations Officers in ensure the continuation of work. 

Whilst there is no obligation for Fulham FC Foundation to provide, or for the Football & Sports Coaches to accept, work. Football & Sports Coaches who do not work for an extended period of time, will be deactivated. 

The successful candidates should be available to work across at least one of the below times, in order to receive work. 

Weekday Afternoons –3-5 pm;
Weekday Evenings - 5-9pm 
Weekends – Saturdays – 8am to 12noon. 
Work in other timings is available, however on a far reduced frequency. 
Salary Range: £12.95 p/h to £16.50 p/h

Next steps: 
There is no closing date for this role. Applications will be reviewed on a bi-weekly basis.

If successful, you will join a vibrant team who are passionate about achieving the organisation's vision of “an active, inclusive and thriving community”, and you’ll have the opportunity to build on your existing skills through a variety of training and development opportunities and experiences.

*This job description should not be regarded as exclusive or exhaustive. It is intended as an outline indication of areas of activity and responsibility and will be amended in the light of the changing needs of the organisation.


Responsibilities

  • To act as an Ambassador for Fulham FC Foundation (FFCF), representing the organisation professionally in line with its values. 
  • To plan and deliver high-quality football and sports coaching sessions to children, young people & adults in-line with FFCF standards.
  • To provide a safe, positive and inclusive learning environment at all times,
  • To adhere to FFCF’s safeguarding policies and procedures, ensuring the timely reporting of any safeguarding concerns or incidents within sessions. 
  • To engage, include, inspire and grow participation across FFCF activities.
  • To support the transition of participants between different FFCF programmes and activities.
  • To champion opportunities for individuals from underrepresented groups within FFCF activities. 
  • To identify and support progression pathways for talented young people. 
  • To support the development of FFCF programmes through positive collaboration with Operations Officers. 
  • To support the promotion of FFCF programmes across the local community. 
  • To assist in data collection and completion of monitoring & evaluation on an ongoing basis.
     
     
     
     
     

Requirements

Essential: 

Experience: 
•  Delivery of football coaching sessions in a grassroots environment
•  Delivery of sports coaching sessions to children & young people.
•  Delivery within a primary school.
•  Working in a customer-facing role.

Skills, Knowledge & Qualifications:
•  Introduction to Coaching Football qualification
•  In-date FA Emergency First Aid 
•  In-date FA Safeguarding Children certificate
•  Base knowledge of and willingness to learn & contribute towards FFCF strategic aims. 

Personal Qualities:
•  A positive role-model for children & young people.
•  Ability to travel independently across South-West London, including transport of equipment.
•  Well presented, with a professional manner.
•  Positive, energetic persona. Able to build rapport with participants.
•  Excellent time-keeping & punctuality.
•  Willingness to learn & develop personally & professionally.

Experience: 
•  Delivery of football & sports coaching sessions in a Club Community Organisation (CCO).
•  Delivery of Physical Education within a primary school. 
•  Delivery within a classroom environment.
•  Marketing & Promoting participation activities in the community.
•  Delivering an activity to children & young people with a Disability.
•  Delivery of football & sports coaching sessions to females.

Skills, Knowledge & Qualifications:
•  FA Level 2/UEFA C in Coaching Football qualification (or higher). 
•  Additional PE/HLTA qualification
•  Mentoring qualification
•  Non-football NGB sports qualifications.
