job.title: Academy Goalkeeping Coach
job.html_description:
To actively lead our schoolboy Goalkeeping Programme here at Fulham FC. You will be responsible for the development of our U9-15 GK’s.

Responsibilities

Responsibilities include but are not limited to:

  • Plan, prepare & Lead on Academy GK Sessions across the academy pathway.

  • Support all GK’s with purposeful ILP’s to aid their development.

  • Lead on both Player & Parent Reviews.

  • Participate in MDT meetings to support the GK’s across different phases.

  • Work with age group coaches to ensure each GK has the best programme possible.

  • Log all GK sessions on Kitman Labs.

Requirements

  • UEFA B GK Licence

  • UEFA B Licence

  • Emergency First Aid in Football (EFAiF)

  • FA Safeguarding Certificate

  • FA Youth Award
job.plain_text_description:
To actively lead our schoolboy Goalkeeping Programme here at Fulham FC. You will be responsible for the development of our U9-15 GK’s.
Responsibilities
Responsibilities include but are not limited to:
- Plan, prepare & Lead on Academy GK Sessions across the academy pathway. - Support all GK’s with purposeful ILP’s to aid their development. - Lead on both Player & Parent Reviews. - Participate in MDT meetings to support the GK’s across different phases. - Work with age group coaches to ensure each GK has the best programme possible. - Log all GK sessions on Kitman Labs.
Requirements
- UEFA B GK Licence - UEFA B Licence - Emergency First Aid in Football (EFAiF) - FA Safeguarding Certificate - FA Youth Award

job.title: Sessional Coach (Goalkeeping specific)
job.html_description:
To deliver high quality football coaching sessions to accomplished & aspiring grassroots players aged 6 to 16.

To deliver football & sports sessions across the wider Foundation to a range of children, young people and adults.


Responsibilities

To act as an Ambassador for FFCF and maintain and develop relationships to ensure high quality experiences for young people, partner organisations, other staff and customers

To effectively plan and deliver high quality football sessions in-line with coaching syllabus, philosophy and values.

To effectively support the development of individual players through the creation of individual development plans throughout the season.

To identify and support the development of progression pathways for talented young people

To effectively plan and deliver extra-curricular and community activities across the wider Foundation.

To support the transition between school-based activities, wider Foundation activities and where appropriate, other community activities

Assist in data collection and completion of monitoring for reports on an ongoing basis, within the timescales required

To work closely with Football Development Officer's to understand programme standards and attendance targets and support the promotion of FFCF activities

To support the smooth function of the Operations Department in line with the Foundation values and targets and work across programmes, when required

Requirements

Essential:

Experience
Leading football sessions to age groups U7 to U16
Delivery of extracurricular and community activities to all age groups
Supporting and developing coaches in a sports environment
Working with external partners to achieve successful outcome
Monitoring and Evaluation 
Marketing and promotion 

Skills and Qualifications:
Minimum of FA Level 1 in Coaching Goalkeepers, National Goalkeeping Course or equivalent experience
FA First Aid - in date
FA Safeguarding - in date
Ability to drive, has access to a car or ability to travel independently. 

Personal Qualities
Committed and motivated by the purpose of the Foundation 
Demonstrates respect for colleagues, partners and customers and when possible operates above and beyond expectations
Well presented with a good manner
Excellent punctuality
Ability to prioritise work and consistently meet deadlines
Effective communicator

Preferable:
Experience
Delivery in a Club Community Organisation (CCO)
Delivery of activity to young people with a Disability. 
Delivery of CPD events to coaches.
Delivery of Physical Education lessons to primary age children

Skills and Qualifications
FA Level 2 in Coaching Goalkeepers or UEFA Goalkeeping B Diploma
Non-football NGB sports qualifications
Mentoring qualification
Talent Identification qualification
Understanding of Physical Education standards in schools (Ofsted guidance)
job.plain_text_description:
To deliver high quality football coaching sessions to accomplished & aspiring grassroots players aged 6 to 16.
To deliver football & sports sessions across the wider Foundation to a range of children, young people and adults.
Responsibilities
To act as an Ambassador for FFCF and maintain and develop relationships to ensure high quality experiences for young people, partner organisations, other staff and customers
To effectively plan and deliver high quality football sessions in-line with coaching syllabus, philosophy and values.
To effectively support the development of individual players through the creation of individual development plans throughout the season.
To identify and support the development of progression pathways for talented young people
To effectively plan and deliver extra-curricular and community activities across the wider Foundation.
To support the transition between school-based activities, wider Foundation activities and where appropriate, other community activities
Assist in data collection and completion of monitoring for reports on an ongoing basis, within the timescales required
To work closely with Football Development Officer's to understand programme standards and attendance targets and support the promotion of FFCF activities
To support the smooth function of the Operations Department in line with the Foundation values and targets and work across programmes, when required
Requirements
Essential:
Experience
Leading football sessions to age groups U7 to U16
Delivery of extracurricular and community activities to all age groups
Supporting and developing coaches in a sports environment
Working with external partners to achieve successful outcome
Monitoring and Evaluation
Marketing and promotion
Skills and Qualifications:
Minimum of FA Level 1 in Coaching Goalkeepers, National Goalkeeping Course or equivalent experience
FA First Aid - in date
FA Safeguarding - in date
Ability to drive, has access to a car or ability to travel independently.
Personal Qualities
Committed and motivated by the purpose of the Foundation
Demonstrates respect for colleagues, partners and customers and when possible operates above and beyond expectations
Well presented with a good manner
Excellent punctuality
Ability to prioritise work and consistently meet deadlines
Effective communicator
Preferable:
Experience
Delivery in a Club Community Organisation (CCO)
Delivery of activity to young people with a Disability.
Delivery of CPD events to coaches.
Delivery of Physical Education lessons to primary age children
Skills and Qualifications
FA Level 2 in Coaching Goalkeepers or UEFA Goalkeeping B Diploma
Non-football NGB sports qualifications
Mentoring qualification
Talent Identification qualification
Understanding of Physical Education standards in schools (Ofsted guidance)

job.title: Sales Lead, Hospitality
job.plain_text_description:
The Sales Lead, Hospitality is accountable for the conversion, growth and integrity of premium hospitality revenue generated from the Club’s existing and inbound audiences.
The role owns the premium inside sales operating model, defining how demand is curated, qualified and converted. It is responsible for ensuring that every sale is accurately scoped, clearly communicated and operationally deliverable, protecting experience quality, delivery credibility and long-term customer value.
Operating as the senior authority for premium inside sales execution, the role works closely with Business Development, Account Management, Operations, Marketing and CRM to ensure disciplined conversion and reliable forecasting.
Responsibilities
Own the premium inside sales function, with full accountability for prioritising, converting and growing hospitality revenue from existing audiences and inbound enquiries, ensuring demand is handled effectively and consistently.
Lead and develop a high-performing sales team, setting clear expectations for sales quality, deal progression, customer experience and revenue delivery, and promoting individual accountability sales for outcomes and behaviours.
Own premium revenue performance, actively managing sales pace, conversion quality, forecast accuracy and deal integrity to deliver predictable, sustainable growth.
Set and enforce the standard for how premium hospitality is sold, acting as the senior authority on sales judgement, deal structure and customer experience.
Exercise sound commercial judgement under pressure, prioritising long-term value, delivery credibility and customer trust over short-term conversion where required.
Maintain clear ownership of pipeline health and forecasting, ensuring opportunities, next steps and revenue projections are current, accurate and trusted across the business.
Maintain strong sales process and CRM discipline, ensuring activity, data quality and reporting support effective planning, performance management and decision-making.
Share sales insight and best practice across the commercial team, contributing constructively while maintaining clear ownership boundaries with Business Development.
Ensure a seamless, member-focused handover into Account Management, providing clear scope, context and expectations to support onboarding, delivery and long-term value.
Work closely with Hospitality Operations to align sales commitments with delivery capability and reduce execution and matchday risk.
Partner with Marketing and CRM on fixture-led campaigns, inbound demand management and continuous improvement in conversion efficiency and customer experience.
Requirements
TheSales Lead, Hospitalitymust have:
- Proven experience owning and leading an inside-sales or conversion-focused sales function within a premium, hospitality, membership or service-led environment. - Clear accountability for revenue performance, including sales pace, conversion quality, forecast accuracy and deal integrity. - Strong people leadership skills, with experience setting expectations, coaching performance and holding individuals accountable for results and behaviours. - A consultative, value-led sales approach, with a track record of converting inbound enquiries, warm leads and existing customers into repeat or longer-term relationships. - Sound commercial judgement, able to balance short-term conversion with experience quality, delivery credibility and long-term customer value. - Confidence owning pipeline management, forecasting and CRM discipline, using data to prioritise activity and make decisions (MS Dynamics or similar preferred). - Excellent organisation and prioritisation skills, able to manage volume without losing control, quality or pace. - A calm, professional approach in live environments, comfortable working matchdays, evenings and weekends when required.
Ideally, theSales Lead, Hospitalitywill also have:
- Experience within premium sport, live events, hospitality or membership-based organisations. - Understanding of how sales behaviour impacts experience delivery, retention and renewal, not just conversion. - Experience working closely with Operations, Account Management, Marketing or CRM teams in a multi-function commercial environment. - Familiarity with ticketing or inventory-based sales environments(e.g. Ticketmaster or equivalent). - Exposure to retention, renewal or account-based sales models. - A genuine interest in football, live sport or elite hospitality environments.

job.title: Employee belonging to a protected category
job.location: Viale della Liberazione, Milano, MI, 20124, IT
job.html_description:
Are you a dynamic, precise person interested in the world of sport, media & entertainment? Would you like to join an international club and work in a young and stimulating environment? FC Internazionale is constantly looking for professionals belonging to the Protected Categories (art 1 l.68/99) with whom to get in touch in order to evaluate together future collaboration possibilities for one of our departments, such as, for example:


  • Supply Chain and Procurement
  • Technology
  • E-commerce and Product Development
  • Marketing and CRM
  • Social Media & Digital
  • Ticketing & Contact Centre

In order to best align your interests and our needs, please specify function and area of potential interest when sending your application. Would you like to join our team? Then what are you waiting for, send us your CV! We look forward to getting to know you!


Those most in line may be evaluated for future needs to strengthen company structures.

job.title: Head Of Performance - Men's 1st Team
job.html_description:
At Juventus, performance It’s a culture.
It’s how we prepare, protect, and push our athletes to compete at the highest level, every single day.

We’re looking for a Head of Performance to lead the strategic integration and continuous optimisation of our multidisciplinary performance function, working at the heart of an elite football environment.

🎯 The challenge
You will act as the central performance integrator, aligning sports science, strength & conditioning, nutrition, and return‑to‑play to maximise player availability, physical output, and long‑term athlete development.
From first team to academy pathway, you’ll translate scientific insight into applied football practice — driving evidence‑based decisions that make a real impact on the pitch.

⚽ What you’ll lead

  • A world‑class performance programme for the Men’s First Team

  • Integrated frameworks for load management, injury risk reduction, and return‑to‑play

  • Cross‑functional collaboration with technical and medical leadership

  • Performance intelligence that supports strategic and match‑critical decision‑making

👤 Who we’re looking for

You'll bring a rare mix of scientific expertise, applied football experience, and leadership, including:

  • At least 10 years of experience in Performance roles within professional football

  • Proven delivery of elite football performance and periodisation models, including RTP and athletic development

  • Advanced expertise in performance technologies (GPS, AMS, testing systems, gym programming)

  • Strong capability to monitor, interpret, and prescribe load using multi‑source physiological data

  • Deep knowledge of injury epidemiology, reconditioning principles, and RTP progressions in elite football

  • Confidence in communicating complex performance data clearly to players, coaches, and staff

  • Experience in integrating multidisciplinary teams, breaking silos and driving shared performance objectives

  • Sound judgement in high‑pressure, time‑critical decisions (match‑day availability, congested schedules)

  • A track record of developing staff and scaling performance structures within elite environments

🚀 Why Juventus

Because here, performance means competing in another league — every day.
You’ll work in a club where ambition, innovation, and accountability set the standard, and where your expertise will directly shape how we prepare to win.

👉 Ready to make performance count?
Apply now and help define the future of elite football performance at Juventus.

job.title: Kitchen Team Leader
job.html_description:
Join our team at the AXA Training Centre and play a vital role in keeping our kitchens running smoothly and efficiently. We’re looking for someone with strong kitchen porter experience. You’ll ensure all catering goods are ordered, received, and distributed accurately and on time — while also supporting with travel packing and loading when needed.

Key Responsibilities

  • Support the Head Chef and Front of House Manager in ensuring smooth back-of-house operations.
  • Manage food, chemical, and cleaning stock levels using Procure Wizard, ensuring accurate ordering, storage, and rotation.
  • Oversee food deliveries, storage, and stock control, maintaining full compliance with food safety standards.
  • Lead, rota, and support the kitchen porter team to maintain high hygiene and cleanliness standards across all kitchen areas.
  • Monitor food safety using Navitas systems, ensuring correct temperatures and traceability for all products.
  • Ensure all food hygiene, COSHH, and health & safety policies are consistently met and documented.
  • Process purchase orders, delivery notes, and invoices accurately and on time.
  • Conduct regular stock checks and work with external auditors to ensure accurate stock counts.
  • Assist the Head Chef with supplier management, ensuring approved suppliers meet quality and safety standards.
  • Complete all required paperwork, reports, and compliance checks in line with company policies.
  • Conduct annual performance reviews for direct reports and support team development.

 Essential Requirements

  • Strong experience as a lead kitchen porter/ kitchen assistant, or similar role.
  • Proven stock management skills with attention to accuracy and organisation.
  • Good knowledge of food safety and HACCP procedures.
  • Confident using Microsoft Office and other computer systems.
  • Strong communication skills with the ability to work effectively with chefs, managers, and colleagues.
  • Positive, professional, and team-focused approach to work.

Why should you apply?

This is a full-time permanent role working 35 hours per week, 5 from 7 days, including evenings and weekends. Your main base will be our AXA Training Centre. 

To reward your hard work and commitment we offer a competitive salary, 25 days holiday (plus 8 bank holidays and the option to purchase up to an additional 5 days) and a contributory pension scheme.

You will have access to our benefits kit bag where you can get high street discounts, and a selection of benefit schemes you can join. There are opportunities to get involved with volunteering through our LFC Foundation to give back to the local community.

At Liverpool Football Club, we have an unwavering commitment to equality, diversity and inclusion and are always looking to making a positive difference in the communities that we operate within. We are proud of our achievements in this area; maintaining the Premier League Equality Standard Advanced Level, becoming a founding signatory of the Football Association’s Football Leadership Diversity Code, and being recognised as a leader in this important area on and off the pitch. We take our responsibilities in this area seriously and through the work being done across the club, we are committed to increasing the diversity of our people and becoming an increasingly inclusive workplace for all. We are committed to hiring great people representative of diverse backgrounds, perspectives, and skills across our entire business. If you share our enthusiasm and passion for inclusivity, then we want to hear from you.

Liverpool FC is committed to safeguarding and promoting the welfare of children and vulnerable adults and expects all Colleagues and Volunteers to share this commitment.

job.title: Warehouse Assistant, DC Operations Support
job.html_description:
We have an exciting opportunity for an individual to join our Liverpool FC team as a Warehouse Assistant, Troubleshooter located at our distribution centre. 

What will you be doing?

In this role, you will act as a key technical and operational resource across all warehouse functions. You’ll support Supervisors and colleagues by providing hands‑on operational expertise, resolving issues on the floor, and helping maintain smooth operational workflow across the Distribution Centre.

Operational Support

  • Competently perform all warehouse tasks (inbound, picking, packing, replenishment, outbound, returns, and production/personalisation) with efficiency and accuracy.
  • Act as the first point of support when operational issues arise, resolving problems quickly and escalating where appropriate.
  • Provide cover during peak periods, colleague absences, or unexpected operational challenges.

Stock Control & Accuracy

  • Support all stock management activities including scanning, put‑away, picking, cycle counts, and reconciliation.
  • Investigate discrepancies and errors in stock movements, providing solutions or escalating recurring patterns to Supervisors.
  • Assist in periodic inventory counts (PI) and ensure accurate stock records and reporting.
  • Model and support loss‑prevention practices and proper stock‑handling procedures.

Training & Coaching Support

  • Mentor and coach new or less‑experienced warehouse operatives on operational procedures and best practices.
  • Support Supervisors in delivering training on SOPs, systems, equipment use, and safety processes.
  • Share expertise across shifts to help raise overall team capability and confidence.

Support for Supervisors

  • Provide additional operational oversight and support to Supervisors during the shift.
  • Monitor team compliance with SOPs, safety requirements, and quality standards, raising concerns when necessary.
  • Recommend process improvements based on hands‑on operational experience.

Health, Safety & Compliance

  • Ensure full compliance with safety policies, PPE requirements, and safe working practices.
  • Conduct equipment checks and report any basic maintenance needs (FLT, scanners, conveyors, packing equipment).
  • Support investigations into incidents, near‑misses, or safety breaches as required.

Problem Solving & Continuous Improvement

  • Identify inefficiencies, bottlenecks, and recurring issues in real‑time and recommend improvements.
  • Support Supervisors and Operations Managers with process improvement initiatives.
  • Champion change on the warehouse floor, promoting adoption of new systems, tools or ways of working.

Operational Administration Support

  • Assist with day‑to‑day administrative tasks relating to stock, production documentation, returns, or reporting.
  • Ensure data is accurately captured in WMS/ERP systems in line with site standards.
  • Support ad‑hoc reporting or investigation requests from Supervisors or Operations Managers.

Who are we looking for? 

  • Experience working within a distribution centre
  • Leadership skills and the confidence to support colleagues
  • Strong teamwork ethic and communication skills
  • Retail operations knowledge
  • Excellent attention to detail
  • Sound IT knowledge
  • Flexible and adaptive approach to work
  • WMS experience
  • Experience working in an automated or system‑driven environment

Why should you apply?

This is a full‑time permanent position working 35 hours per week, across any five days out of seven, which will include regular weekend working. Your main base will be our Distribution Centre, in Prescot.  

Please note, this vacancy may close early if we receive a high volume of applications, so we encourage you to apply as soon as possible.

To reward your hard work and commitment we offer a competitive salary, 25 days holiday (plus 8 bank holidays and the option to purchase up to an additional 5 days) and access to our benefits kit bag which includes but not limited to:

  • Contributory pension scheme matching up to 5.5%
  • Life Assurance Cover
  • Free financial guidance and mortgage advice
  • Car salary sacrifice scheme for affordable driving
  • Cycle to Work scheme to keep you active
  • Purchase season ticket loans for easier commuting
  • Exclusive shopping discounts & cashback with top retailers
  • Will Writing Service for future planning
  • Employee Assistance Programme for confidential support
  • Medicash Health Cash Plan for everyday healthcare needs
  • Volunteering opportunities to give back to the community
  • Special LFC perks – retail discounts, partner offers, free LFCTV GO access, and priority tickets for matches, events & concerts

…and much more! LFC Benefits.pdf

At Liverpool Football Club, we have an unwavering commitment to equality, diversity and inclusion and are always looking to making a positive difference in the communities that we operate within. We are proud of our achievements in this area; maintaining the Premier League Equality Standard Advanced Level, becoming a founding signatory of the Football Association’s Football Leadership Diversity Code and being recognised as a leader in this important area on and off the pitch. We take our responsibilities in this area seriously and through the work being done across the club, we are committed to increasing the diversity of our people and becoming an increasingly inclusive workplace for all. We are committed to hiring great people representative of diverse backgrounds, perspectives, and skills across our entire business. If you share our enthusiasm and passion for inclusivity, then we want to hear from you.

Liverpool FC is committed to safeguarding and promoting the welfare of children and vulnerable adults and expects all Colleagues and Volunteers to share this commitment.

job.title: Casual Stadium Kitchen Assistants
job.html_description:
We are looking for experienced Kitchen Assistants to join our casual catering team at Anfield Stadium. You’ll work in our state-of-the-art kitchens alongside our talented and creative chefs, supporting the delivery of first-class food and service on match days and events.

 What will you be doing?

  • Assisting with basic food preparation.
  • Unloading and storing deliveries safely and efficiently.
  • Maintaining cleanliness by emptying bins, sweeping, and mopping floors.
  • Ensuring all kitchen equipment is cleaned and stored correctly.
  • Deep cleaning fridges, storage areas, and other kitchen spaces to maintain hygiene standards.
  • Operating automated dishwashers to clean crockery and utensils used by guests.
  • Supporting with porter duties, ensuring all back-of-house areas are tidy and ready for the next event.

Who are we looking for?

We’re looking for hardworking individuals who thrive in a fast-paced kitchen environment.

Please note you must be 18 or over to be considered for this position.

 You’ll have:

  • Previous experience as a Kitchen Assistant, Kitchen Porter, or in a similar role.
  • A good understanding of manual handling techniques and safe working practices.
  • Experience using a range of kitchen equipment.
  • A positive attitude and a commitment to maintaining high standards of cleanliness and teamwork.

Why should you apply?

This is a casual position, offering variable hours including evenings and weekends on a flexible assignment basis.

Liverpool FC is committed to safeguarding and promoting the welfare of children and vulnerable adults and expects all Colleagues and Volunteers to share this commitment.

job.title: Assistant Store Manager, ILAC Store (Ireland)
job.location: Ilac Retail Store - Dublin, Republic of Ireland
job.html_description:
Are you a natural leader with a passion for retail and delivering outstanding customer experiences? We're looking for a confident and motivated Assistant Store Manager to help drive the success of our ILAC Store in Ireland. 

In this hands‑on role, you’ll play a key part in running the store day‑to‑day, inspiring the team, and ensuring every customer enjoys a world‑class LFC shopping experience. 

What will you be doing? 

As Assistant Store Manager, you’ll take ownership of operational excellence while supporting and developing your team. Your responsibilities will include: 

  • Open and close the store, carry out till counts, investigate, resolve, action or escalate any till losses or discrepancies, cashing up, safe counts, banking and manage Securicor collections. 
  • Manage the shopfloor and back-office areas, including queues, customer issues, stock availability issues and printing issue.  
  • Ensure all deliveries are received and processed in most efficient manner and the shopfloor is commercially merchandised.  
  • Provide the store team with direction and guidance to complete daily tasks and achieve targets. 
  • In conjunction with the Store Manager, manage the store team’s performance daily and manage any store and/or staff issues to successful conclusion.  
  • Support the Store Manager to ensure that your store is effectively resourced to meet the needs of the store and customer base at key trading times including planning rotas and the recruitment of employees.  
  • Make decisions that will generate sales and enhance store profitability.      
  • Complete all paperwork and store actions to deliver consistent results above the retail benchmark for Store Compliance Audits.  
  • Deliver refresher training to the existing store team, whilst supporting the induction and training of new store team members.  
  • Coordinate shopfloor moves and any promotional activities and product launches, for example Kit launch and taking responsibility for any key actions in preparation for match days.  
  • Oversee the transfer of stock to other stores or the Distribution Centre, ensuring all paperwork is completed and the stock is tracked.  
  • Oversee the completion of stock counts and stock takes. 
  • Undertake all tasks in accordance with our retail standards and to participate in regular meetings and training. 

Who are we looking for? 

You’ll thrive in this role if you: 

  • Have experience in a managerial or supervisory role within a Retail Store (essential)
  • Minimum of 3 years’ experience in a similar role within Retail (essential)
  • Experience working in a customer-facing role within the Retail industry
  • Experience working to and delivering against KPIs within a Retail store environment
  • Are confident motivating, coaching and leading a team to success.
  • Are commercially aware with strong product knowledge and a passion for creating standout customer experiences.
  • Bring strong numerical, analytical and problem‑solving skills.
  • Are detail‑driven, organised, and comfortable making operational decisions in a fast‑paced environment.
  • Can work flexibly to meet the needs of the store.

Why should you apply? 

This is a full-time permanent contract, working 40 hours per week on a 5 from 7-day basis. Your main base will be our ILAC Store, in Ireland. 

At Liverpool Football Club, we have an unwavering commitment to equality, diversity and inclusion and are always looking to making a positive difference in the communities that we operate within. We are proud of our achievements in this area; maintaining the Premier League Equality Standard Advanced Level, becoming a founding signatory of the Football Association’s Football Leadership Diversity Code and being recognised as a leader in this important area on and off the pitch. We take our responsibilities in this area seriously and through the work being done across the club, we are committed to increasing the diversity of our people and becoming an increasingly inclusive workplace for all. We are committed to hiring great people representative of diverse backgrounds, perspectives, and skills across our entire business. If you share our enthusiasm and passion for inclusivity, then we want to hear from you.

Liverpool FC is committed to safeguarding and promoting the welfare of children and vulnerable adults and expects all Colleagues and Volunteers to share this commitment.

Who are we looking for?
You’ll thrive in this role if you:
- Have experience in a managerial or supervisory role within a Retail Store (essential) - Minimum of 3 years’ experience in a similar role within Retail (essential) - Experience working in a customer-facing role within the Retail industry - Experience working to and delivering against KPIs within a Retail store environment - Are confident motivating, coaching and leading a team to success. - Are commercially aware with strong product knowledge and a passion for creating standout customer experiences. - Bring strong numerical, analytical and problem‑solving skills. - Are detail‑driven, organised, and comfortable making operational decisions in a fast‑paced environment. - Can work flexibly to meet the needs of the store.
Why should you apply?
This is a full-time permanent contract, working 40 hours per week on a 5 from 7-day basis. Your main base will be our ILAC Store, in Ireland.

job.title: Retail Sales Advisor, London Store
job.html_description:
We have an exciting opportunity for an enthusiastic individual to join our Liverpool FC team as a Retail Sales Advisor at our brand-new London store! This is a unique chance to be part of something fresh and ambitious, playing a key role in the continued growth and evolution of LFC Retail.

It’s an incredible time to join us, with our standout collaborations with Adidas and Tommy Hilfiger bringing new energy, style, and global attention to the LFC brand.

We’re looking for passionate, customer-focused individuals who love working as part of a team. In this role, you’ll be at the heart of our in‑store experience, ensuring every visitor enjoys the highest possible standard of service and leaves feeling part of the LFC family.

What will you be doing?

  • Dealing with customer sales and refunds, ensuring a focus on customer care and the customer experience.
  • Till work including processing cash and card payments.
  • Gaining and building upon Product knowledge to provide advice and guidance on product selection to customers.
  • Basic Sales Management through driving the AVS, UPT and Conversion by offering add on sales and upselling.
  • Merchandising/display.
  • Stock shrinkage and cash flow controls.
  • Supporting in receiving and storing the delivery of large amounts of stock.

Who are we looking for?

To be successful, you must have recent retail store and customer service experience in a customer facing role and can demonstrate this important skill set, with excellent numeracy and literacy skills. Having strong interpersonal and communication skills, you will be confident in delivering excellent customer service and working as part of a team. A retail and/or customer service qualifications would be advantageous. You will be able to work flexible hours.

Why should you apply?

This position is offering permanent positions working various hours per week on a 5 from 7 day basis. Your main base will be our new London Store.

Please note, this vacancy may close early if we receive a high volume of applications, so we encourage you to apply as soon as possible.

To reward your hard work and commitment we offer a competitive salary, 25 days holiday (plus 8 bank holidays and the option to purchase up to an additional 5 days) and access to our benefits kit bag which includes but not limited to:

  • Contributory pension scheme matching up to 5.5%
  • Life Assurance Cover
  • Free financial guidance and mortgage advice
  • Car salary sacrifice scheme for affordable driving
  • Cycle to Work scheme to keep you active
  • Purchase season ticket loans for easier commuting
  • Exclusive shopping discounts & cashback with top retailers
  • Will Writing Service for future planning
  • Employee Assistance Programme for confidential support
  • Medicash Health Cash Plan for everyday healthcare needs
  • Volunteering opportunities to give back to the community
  • Special LFC perks – retail discounts, partner offers, free LFCTV GO access, and priority tickets for matches, events & concerts

…and much more! LFC Benefits.pdf

At Liverpool Football Club, we have an unwavering commitment to equality, diversity and inclusion and are always looking to making a positive difference in the communities that we operate within. We are proud of our achievements in this area; maintaining the Premier League Equality Standard Advanced Level, becoming a founding signatory of the Football Association’s Football Leadership Diversity Code and being recognised as a leader in this important area on and off the pitch. We take our responsibilities in this area seriously and through the work being done across the club, we are committed to increasing the diversity of our people and becoming an increasingly inclusive workplace for all. We are committed to hiring great people representative of diverse backgrounds, perspectives, and skills across our entire business. If you share our enthusiasm and passion for inclusivity, then we want to hear from you.

Liverpool FC is committed to safeguarding and promoting the welfare of children and vulnerable adults and expects all Colleagues and Volunteers to share this commitment.

#LFCJobs

job.title: Retail Store Supervisor, London Store
job.html_description:
We have an exciting opportunity for an individual to join our Liverpool FC team as a Retail Store Supervisor at our brand-new London store! This is a fantastic chance to be part of something fresh and innovative, contributing directly to the ongoing growth and development of LFC Retail. 

It’s an incredible time to join the LFC family, with our standout collaborations with Adidas and Tommy Hilfiger bringing new momentum, style, and global visibility to the brand.

As a key member of the store’s management team, you’ll work closely with the Store Manager and Assistant Manager to lead, inspire, and drive the team. Your role will be essential in delivering exceptional customer service, achieving strong results, and ensuring the store consistently performs against targets and plans.

What will you be doing?

  • Understanding store targets and KPIs to ensure that targets are delivered.
  • Taking responsibility for running departments within the store and running of the store and ensuring that all targets are met, and high standards are always maintained.
  • Ensuring all deliveries are received and processed in most efficient manner and the shop floor is commercially merchandised.
  • Responsibility for running the store and day to day management of the store and store staff and responding to call outs (as appropriate).
  • Opening and closing the store, carrying out till counts, investigating, resolving, actioning, or escalating any till losses or discrepancies, cashing up, safe counts, banking and managing collections, to complete all of this in accordance with Store standards and compliance checks.
  • Reviewing the daily handover documents and daily planner to allocate daily activities to the store team, using this detail to talk through the daily brief with the team, providing key targets for the day and allocating sections and/or jobs to the store team.
  • Completing management paperwork as required, including, but not limited to reviewing store reports and using these reports to implement any action and/or decisions, ensuring all paperwork and store actions will deliver consistent results above the retail benchmark for Store Compliance Audits.
  • Completing customer service observations and floor walks to ensure we maintain high store standards and direct the team to implement any actions.
  • Supporting with shop floor moves and any promotional activities and product launches, for example Kit launch and taking responsibility for any key actions in preparation for key launches/events.
  • Supporting the management team in delivering refresher training to the existing store team, whilst supporting the induction and training of new store team members.

Who are we looking for?

To be successful in this role you must have recent experience as a supervisor in a retail store environment, including an ability to motivate and drive a team. You will have strong product awareness and an understanding of what makes a great customer experience for our customers. You will have worked in a customer facing setting, where you have developed excellent customer service skills.

You’ll be able to demonstrate excellent numerical and analytical skills and be able to solve problems quickly and effectively. You will have an eye for detail and be confident working in a fast-paced environment, working as part of a team and independently, with the ability to make operational decisions.

Why should you apply?

This is a full-time permanent position working 40 hours per week on a 5 from 7 day basis. Your main base will be our new London Store.

Please note, this vacancy may close early if we receive a high volume of applications, so we encourage you to apply as soon as possible.

To reward your hard work and commitment we offer a competitive salary, 25 days holiday (plus 8 bank holidays and the option to purchase up to an additional 5 days) and access to our benefits kit bag which includes but not limited to:

  • Contributory pension scheme matching up to 5.5%
  • Life Assurance Cover
  • Free financial guidance and mortgage advice
  • Car salary sacrifice scheme for affordable driving
  • Cycle to Work scheme to keep you active
  • Purchase season ticket loans for easier commuting
  • Exclusive shopping discounts & cashback with top retailers
  • Will Writing Service for future planning
  • Employee Assistance Programme for confidential support
  • Medicash Health Cash Plan for everyday healthcare needs
  • Volunteering opportunities to give back to the community
  • Special LFC perks – retail discounts, partner offers, free LFCTV GO access, and priority tickets for matches, events & concerts

…and much more! LFC Benefits.pdf

At Liverpool Football Club, we have an unwavering commitment to equality, diversity and inclusion and are always looking to making a positive difference in the communities that we operate within. We are proud of our achievements in this area; maintaining the Premier League Equality Standard Advanced Level, becoming a founding signatory of the Football Association’s Football Leadership Diversity Code and being recognised as a leader in this important area on and off the pitch. We take our responsibilities in this area seriously and through the work being done across the club, we are committed to increasing the diversity of our people and becoming an increasingly inclusive workplace for all. We are committed to hiring great people representative of diverse backgrounds, perspectives, and skills across our entire business. If you share our enthusiasm and passion for inclusivity, then we want to hear from you.

Liverpool FC is committed to safeguarding and promoting the welfare of children and vulnerable adults and expects all Colleagues and Volunteers to share this commitment.

#LFCJobs

job.title: Assistant Store Manager, London Store
job.html_description:
We have an incredible opportunity for someone to join our Liverpool FC family as an Assistant Store Manager at our brand-new London store! This is a rare chance to help shape something from the ground up and play a key role in the continued growth and evolution of LFC Retail.

It’s an especially exciting moment to come onboard, with our standout collaborations with Adidas and Tommy Hilfiger bringing fresh energy and global attention to the brand.

As an Assistant Store Manager, you’ll be at the heart of the action, leading the day-to-day running of the store, inspiring your team to deliver exceptional customer experiences, and driving performance to achieve and exceed targets. This is your opportunity to make a real impact and be part of a dynamic, high‑performing team.

What will you be doing?

  • Open and close the store, carry out till counts, investigate, resolve, action or escalate any till losses or discrepancies, cashing up, safe counts, banking and manage Securicor collections. 
  • Manage the shopfloor and back-office areas, including queues, customer issues, stock availability issues and printing issue.  
  • Ensure all deliveries are received and processed in most efficient manner and the shopfloor is commercially merchandised.  
  • Provide the store team with direction and guidance to complete daily tasks and achieve targets. 
  • In conjunction with the Store Manager, manage the store team’s performance daily and manage any store and/or staff issues to successful conclusion.   
  • Support the Store Manager to ensure that your store is effectively resourced to meet the needs of the store and customer base at key trading times including planning rotas and the recruitment of employees.   
  • Make decisions that will generate sales and enhance store profitability.      
  • Complete all paperwork and store actions to deliver consistent results above the retail benchmark for Store Compliance Audits.  
  • Deliver refresher training to the existing store team, whilst supporting the induction and training of new store team members.  
  • Coordinate shopfloor moves and any promotional activities and product launches, for example Kit launch and taking responsibility for any key actions in preparation for match days.  
  • Oversee the transfer of stock to other stores or the Distribution Centre, ensuring all paperwork is completed and the stock is tracked.   
  • Oversee the completion of stock counts and stock takes. 
  • Undertake all tasks in accordance with our retail standards and to participate in regular meetings and training. 

Who are we looking for?

To be successful, you will have experience of working in a managerial/supervisory position within a retail store setting. You will have excellent team leadership skills with experience of motivating, coaching and supervising staff together with the ability to provide direction and drive performance. You must also have an understanding and experience working with KPIs. 

The ideal candidate will be customer focused and commercially aware, you’ll have knowledge of retail store and retail operations together with a track record of achieving targets and meeting KPI’s. You will have strong product knowledge and understand what makes an excellent shopping experience for the customer.

You’ll be able to demonstrate excellent numerical and analytical skills and be able to solve problems quickly and effectively. You will have an eye for detail and be confident working in a fast-paced environment, working as part of a team and independently, with the ability to make operational decisions. You will have the ability to work flexible hours

Why should you apply?

This is a full-time permanent position working 40 hours per week on a 5 from 7 day basis. Your main base will be our new London Store. 

Please note, this vacancy may close early if we receive a high volume of applications, so we encourage you to apply as soon as possible.

To reward your hard work and commitment we offer a competitive salary, 25 days holiday (plus 8 bank holidays and the option to purchase up to an additional 5 days) and access to our benefits kit bag which includes but not limited to:

  • Contributory pension scheme matching up to 5.5%
  • Life Assurance Cover
  • Free financial guidance and mortgage advice
  • Car salary sacrifice scheme for affordable driving
  • Cycle to Work scheme to keep you active
  • Purchase season ticket loans for easier commuting
  • Exclusive shopping discounts & cashback with top retailers
  • Will Writing Service for future planning
  • Employee Assistance Programme for confidential support
  • Medicash Health Cash Plan for everyday healthcare needs
  • Volunteering opportunities to give back to the community
  • Special LFC perks – retail discounts, partner offers, free LFCTV GO access, and priority tickets for matches, events & concerts

…and much more! LFC Benefits.pdf

At Liverpool Football Club, we have an unwavering commitment to equality, diversity and inclusion and are always looking to making a positive difference in the communities that we operate within. We are proud of our achievements in this area; maintaining the Premier League Equality Standard Advanced Level, becoming a founding signatory of the Football Association’s Football Leadership Diversity Code and being recognised as a leader in this important area on and off the pitch. We take our responsibilities in this area seriously and through the work being done across the club, we are committed to increasing the diversity of our people and becoming an increasingly inclusive workplace for all. We are committed to hiring great people representative of diverse backgrounds, perspectives, and skills across our entire business. If you share our enthusiasm and passion for inclusivity, then we want to hear from you.

job.title: Retail Store Manager, London Store
job.html_description:
We’re gearing up for something truly special, and you could be at the heart of it. Liverpool FC is opening a brand‑new store in London, and we’re searching for an inspiring Retail Store Manager to help bring this next chapter of LFC Retail to life.

This is your chance to join us at one of the most thrilling moments in our journey. With our incredible, high-profile collaborations such as Adidas and Tommy Hilfiger, the momentum around LFC Retail has never been stronger. Now, you can be part of creating something new, bold, and game‑changing.

As our Retail Store Manager, you’ll be the driving force behind the store’s performance and culture. You’ll lead the delivery of an unforgettable customer experience, champion operational excellence, and uphold world‑class brand standards, all within one of the Club’s most strategically important retail destinations.

If you're ready to take on a role where your leadership directly shapes the success of a landmark new store, this is the moment to step in and make your mark.

What will you be doing?

Leadership & People

  • Lead and develop the store team to deliver strong results.
  • Build a high performance, values driven culture.
  • Support recruitment, onboarding, and talent development.
  • Keep colleagues engaged through clear communication and recognition.
  • Promote an inclusive and supportive work environment.

Customer Experience

  • Deliver a world class experience for all fans and visitors.
  • Resolve escalated issues professionally.
  • Introduce initiatives that elevate service and engagement.
  • Champion Click & Collect and digital in store tools.

Commercial Performance

  • Own and drive KPIs across sales, conversion, ATV, UPT, payroll and stock.
  • Analyse trading results and act quickly on opportunities.
  • Prepare the store for peak periods and key launches.
  • Collaborate with central teams on promotions and campaigns.

Operational Excellence

  • Maintain full compliance across all operational policies.
  • Oversee stock accuracy, replenishment, and loss prevention.
  • Lead efficient scheduling and labour planning.
  • Ensure smooth daily operations and cash processes.
  • Manage facilities to maintain a safe, premium environment.

Visual Merchandising

  • Deliver high visual standards aligned with global brand guidelines.
  • Implement campaigns and product presentation updates on time.
  • Keep the store clean, organised, and brand led.

Safety & Risk

  • Ensure the safety and security of customers and colleagues.
  • Lead responses to incidents and emergencies.
  • Work with security teams and local authorities proactively.

Stakeholder Engagement

  • Act as a key contact for internal teams across the business.
  • Share insights on trade, customer behaviour, and local trends.
  • Provide local knowledge to support strategic decisions.

Who are we looking for?

To be successful in this role you’ll have proven experience leading a high‑football, commercially driven retail store, supported by strong leadership capability and a track record of managing and developing large teams. You will bring demonstrable commercial acumen with a history of delivering consistent KPI performance, alongside robust operational knowledge across stock management, retail systems, scheduling, and compliance. Excellent communication, organisation, decision‑making, and problem‑solving skills are essential.

In addition, it would be advantageous if you have experience within a global brand, sports retail, lifestyle retail, or a flagship environment, as well as knowledge of high‑tourism or destination retail operations. An understanding of football culture and the Liverpool FC brand would also be a welcome.

Why should you apply?

This is a full-time permanent position working 40 hours per week on a 5 from 7 day basis. Your main base will be our new London Store. 

Please note, this vacancy may close early if we receive a high volume of applications, so we encourage you to apply as soon as possible.

To reward your hard work and commitment we offer a competitive salary, 25 days holiday (plus 8 bank holidays and the option to purchase up to an additional 5 days) and access to our benefits kit bag which includes but not limited to:

  • Contributory pension scheme matching up to 5.5%
  • Life Assurance Cover
  • Free financial guidance and mortgage advice
  • Car salary sacrifice scheme for affordable driving
  • Cycle to Work scheme to keep you active
  • Purchase season ticket loans for easier commuting
  • Exclusive shopping discounts & cashback with top retailers
  • Will Writing Service for future planning
  • Employee Assistance Programme for confidential support
  • Medicash Health Cash Plan for everyday healthcare needs
  • Volunteering opportunities to give back to the community
  • Special LFC perks – retail discounts, partner offers, free LFCTV GO access, and priority tickets for matches, events & concerts

…and much more! LFC Benefits.pdf

At Liverpool Football Club, we have an unwavering commitment to equality, diversity and inclusion and are always looking to making a positive difference in the communities that we operate within. We are proud of our achievements in this area; maintaining the Premier League Equality Standard Advanced Level, becoming a founding signatory of the Football Association’s Football Leadership Diversity Code and being recognised as a leader in this important area on and off the pitch. We take our responsibilities in this area seriously and through the work being done across the club, we are committed to increasing the diversity of our people and becoming an increasingly inclusive workplace for all. We are committed to hiring great people representative of diverse backgrounds, perspectives, and skills across our entire business. If you share our enthusiasm and passion for inclusivity, then we want to hear from you.

Liverpool FC is committed to safeguarding and promoting the welfare of children and vulnerable adults and expects all Colleagues and Volunteers to share this commitment.

#LFCJobs

job.title: Casual Food Service Assistants
job.html_description:
Liverpool Football Club is delighted to offer exciting opportunities to join the retail catering team as a Casual Food Service Assistant. Based at our iconic Anfield Stadium, you will help serve over 45,000 guests during match days and special events at our stadium concourse kiosks.

This role offers the chance to work at one of the world’s most iconic football stadiums, in a supportive and professional team. With casual shifts and complimentary Stagecoach transport on match days.

What will you be doing in the role?

  • Serving food and drinks at our concourse kiosks during events.
  • Average shift being 6-hours.
  • Maintaining a high level of service during peak times.
  • Ensuring your area remains clean and well-stocked.

We are looking for people who:

  • Are eager to learn and contribute to delivering exceptional service. 
  • Friendly, approachable, and committed individuals to deliver an outstanding service.
  • Work well within a team. 
  • Comfortable working in a fast-faced environment with different types of food and beverages.

Whether you are returning to work, looking for a rewarding casual opportunity, or simply want to be part of the action at one of football’s most famous grounds, we would love to hear from you.

Please note, this role involves handling both alcoholic beverages and food products such as pork.

This is a casual position, offering variable hours including evenings and weekends on a flexible assignment basis.

Liverpool FC is committed to safeguarding and promoting the welfare of children and vulnerable adults and expects all Colleagues and Volunteers to share this commitment.

At Liverpool Football Club, we have an unwavering commitment to equality, diversity and inclusion and are always looking to making a positive difference in the communities that we operate within. We are proud of our achievements in this area; maintaining the Premier League Equality Standard Advanced Level, becoming a founding signatory of the Football Association’s Football Leadership Diversity Code and being recognised as a leader in this important area on and off the pitch. We take our responsibilities in this area seriously and through the work being done across the club, we are committed to increasing the diversity of our people and becoming an increasingly inclusive workplace for all. We are committed to hiring great people representative of diverse backgrounds, perspectives, and skills across our entire business. If you share our enthusiasm and passion for inclusivity, then we want to hear from you.

job.title: Casual Match Day Stewards
job.html_description:
Here at Liverpool Football Club, we are looking for Casual Match Day Stewards to join our team.

As part of the recruitment process, shortlisted candidates will be invited to attend a Group Induction & Interview. During the induction, members of our Safety Team will introduce the role, outline key responsibilities, and provide essential information. This will be followed by individual 121 interviews to assess your suitability for the position.

Please submit your application for the role to be considered for invitation to a Group Induction & Interview session.

What will you be doing in the role?

  • Working as part of a team to provide a safe and secure environment for match day events.
  • Ensuring compliance with stadium regulations, keeping spectators safe and informed.
  • Responding effectively to any incidents of disorder with a calm and focused approach.
  • Offering support and guidance to spectators when needed, ensuring they feel welcome and informed.
  • Conducting personal searches of spectators entering the stadium to maintain security.
  • Documenting and reporting incidents, ensuring clear and accurate records are kept.

We are looking for people who:

  • Have strong communication skills who can confidently engage with supporters, even in challenging situations. 
  • Can handle conflict calmly and professionally.
  • Can write clear reports and possess a basic understanding of conflict management.
  • Are over 18, due to the nature of the role.

If you are someone who can bring experience, calm, and a professional approach to this important role, we would love to hear from you.

By applying for the position of Casual Match Day Steward with Liverpool Football Club, please be aware that this role requires the completion of a Level 2 Certificate in Spectator Safety (as per the Sports Grounds Safety Authority), which must be completed within 12 months from the date of your first induction training.

By submitting your application, you confirm that you can commit to completing the Level 2 Certificate in Spectator Safety within 12 months from your first induction training. As this qualification is essential for the role, if you are unable to complete it within this timeframe, we may not be able to progress your application.

This is a casual position, offering variable hours including evenings and weekends on a flexible assignment basis.

Liverpool FC is committed to safeguarding and promoting the welfare of children and vulnerable adults and expects all Colleagues and Volunteers to share this commitment.

At Liverpool Football Club, we have an unwavering commitment to equality, diversity and inclusion and are always looking to making a positive difference in the communities that we operate within. We are proud of our achievements in this area; maintaining the Premier League Equality Standard Advanced Level, becoming a founding signatory of the Football Association’s Football Leadership Diversity Code and being recognised as a leader in this important area on and off the pitch. We take our responsibilities in this area seriously and through the work being done across the club, we are committed to increasing the diversity of our people and becoming an increasingly inclusive workplace for all. We are committed to hiring great people representative of diverse backgrounds, perspectives, and skills across our entire business. If you share our enthusiasm and passion for inclusivity, then we want to hear from you.

job.title: Employability Program Coordinator (US based)
job.html_description:
The LFC Foundation is the official charity of Liverpool Football Club. The Foundation has been delivering employability programs in Liverpool for many years and more recently in the US. This is an exciting opportunity to create and deliver high-quality employability programs to provide life-changing opportunities in the most deprived areas of New York and New Jersey, helping to raise the aspiration and job prospects for all participants.

THE LFC FOUNDATION’S WORKS PROGRAMS

This role will support the development of the LFC Foundation’s flagship international employability programs in New York and New Jersey. Working across all employability programs, including Creative Works, a creative-based skills development program and Soccer Works, a sport-based skills development program, which both develop the employability skills of young people at risk of long-term unemployment.

The Program Coordinator will be responsible for effective delivery of LFC Foundation employability program, taking participants from initial engagement during program delivery, through their mentoring and ultimately supporting their pathway into work experience and eventually meaningful paid employment.

Key Responsibilities

· Design and deliver engaging creative employability sessions that build skills and elevate career aspirations.

· Develop curriculum and collaborate with external partners to meet KPIs.

· Support participant identification and recruitment in coordination with UK-based teams.

· Coordinate program logistics including venues, transportation, meals and accommodations.

· Support the Senior Coordinator in building relationships with existing and new schools and organizations, and assisting with funder reporting.

· Facilitate high-quality work experience placements.

· Work closely with the Impact and Insights team to ensure the monitoring and evaluation framework is carried out effectively.

· Manage risk across employability programmes to ensure the safety and wellbeing of all young people and staff.

· Continually implement quality assurance procedures to ensure consistent delivery of a world-class programme.

· Embed the highest safeguarding standards across all areas of work.

Required Knowledge, Skills & Experience

· Experience planning and delivering creative educational workshops or training sessions.

· Experience working with vulnerable populations.

· Understanding of inclusive, youth-centered work practices.

· Knowledge of youth mentoring models.

· Experience guiding young adults to additional opportunities.

· Strong alignment with LFC Foundation mission and values.

· Excellent verbal, written, and presentation skills.

· Strong organizational and time-management skills.

· Experience within creative, community, or education settings.

· Knowledge of local industries in New York and/or New Jersey.

Technical Skills

· Safeguarding Young People – Level 1 (or U.S. equivalent).

· Proficiency in Microsoft Office 365 including Word, Excel, and PowerPoint.

· Experience collecting and managing program data

The role involves work across many locations throughout New York and New Jersey in order to deliver the programme in the community. So, if you enjoy making a difference to real people out in the community, this role is for you!

This is a Fixed Term Contract.

To reward your hard work and commitment we offer a competitive salary, 10 days holiday (plus national US holidays) and a contributory pension scheme. 

You will have access to our benefits kit bag where you can get high street discounts, and a selection of benefit schemes you can join. There are opportunities to get involved with volunteering through our LFC Foundation to give back to the local community.

Please note, this vacancy may close early if we receive a high volume of applications, so we encourage you to apply as soon as possible.

At Liverpool Football Club, we have an unwavering commitment to equality, diversity and inclusion and are always looking to making a positive difference in the communities that we operate within. We are proud of our achievements in this area; maintaining the Premier League Equality Standard Advanced Level, becoming a founding signatory of the Football Association’s Football Leadership Diversity Code and being recognised as a leader in this important area on and off the pitch. We take our responsibilities in this area seriously and through the work being done across the club, we are committed to increasing the diversity of our people and becoming an increasingly inclusive workplace for all. We are committed to hiring great people representative of diverse backgrounds, perspectives, and skills across our entire business. If you share our enthusiasm and passion for inclusivity, then we want to hear from you.

Liverpool FC is committed to safeguarding and promoting the welfare of children and vulnerable adults and expects all Colleagues and Volunteers to share this commitment. This role is subject to a satisfactory enhanced DBS check.

#LFCJobs

job.title: Casual Soccer Schools Coach
job.html_description:
We are looking for highly skilled coaches who have the potential to deliver our unique Liverpool FC programmes to our set criteria. If you believe that you have the personality, experience and qualifications required to coach `The Liverpool Way' then the world's greatest football family is waiting for you to submit your application. 

Coaching at LFC International Academy 

Liverpool FC International Academy has a fantastic team of coaches who operate in many different countries. Whichever part of the world we are in, whether it is a Soccer School in Liverpool or at an International Academy, our coaches deliver the highest standard of football coaching in line with the values of `The Liverpool Way'. Many of our coaches get to experience coaching trips overseas, giving them the opportunity to represent the club around the globe. 

Casual coach requirements: 

- To learn the values and history of Liverpool FC 

- To create a positive, enjoyable environment in which each individual can thrive 

- Have the ability to work alongside a team of coaches 

- Effectively communicate with players, parents and partners 

- Have a passion to progress your coaching development 

- Have the confidence to deliver high quality football sessions `The Liverpool Way' 

- Be organised, punctual, self-motivated and reliable 

- Provide each player with an experience they will never forget. 

- Minimal qualifications required: 

• FA Coaching Level 2 (UEFA C) 

• FA Safeguarding Certificate 

• FA Emergency Aid Certificate 

Why should you apply? 

This is a casual position, offering variable hours including evenings and weekends on a flexible assignment basis. 

Please note, this vacancy may close early if we receive a high volume of applications, so we encourage you to apply as soon as possible.

At Liverpool Football Club, we have an unwavering commitment to equality, diversity and inclusion and are always looking to making a positive difference in the communities that we operate within. We are proud of our achievements in this area; maintaining the Premier League Equality Standard Advanced Level, becoming a founding signatory of the Football Association’s Football Leadership Diversity Code and being recognised as a leader in this important area on and off the pitch. We take our responsibilities in this area seriously and through the work being done across the club, we are committed to increasing the diversity of our people and becoming an increasingly inclusive workplace for all. We are committed to hiring great people representative of diverse backgrounds, perspectives, and skills across our entire business. If you share our enthusiasm and passion for inclusivity, then we want to hear from you.

Liverpool FC is committed to safeguarding and promoting the welfare of children and vulnerable adults and expects all Colleagues and Volunteers to share this commitment. This role is subject to a satisfactory enhanced DBS check.

Please note, this vacancy may close early if we receive a high volume of applications, so we encourage you to apply as soon as possible.
At Liverpool Football Club, we have an unwavering commitment to equality, diversity and inclusion and are always looking to making a positive difference in the communities that we operate within. We are proud of our achievements in this area; maintaining the Premier League Equality Standard Advanced Level, becoming a founding signatory of the Football Association’s Football Leadership Diversity Code and being recognised as a leader in this important area on and off the pitch. We take our responsibilities in this area seriously and through the work being done across the club, we are committed to increasing the diversity of our people and becoming an increasingly inclusive workplace for all. We are committed to hiring great people representative of diverse backgrounds, perspectives, and skills across our entire business. If you share our enthusiasm and passion for inclusivity, then we want to hear from you.
Liverpool FC is committed to safeguarding and promoting the welfare of children and vulnerable adults and expects all Colleagues and Volunteers to share this commitment. This role is subject to a satisfactory enhanced DBS check.

job.title: Senior Manager, Partnerships Delivery
job.html_description:
We are looking for a Partnership Delivery - Senior Manager to join the team at Liverpool FC. 

Do you want to play a pivotal role in the Partnership Delivery Team at Liverpool FC, delivering best in class service levels, commercial relationships and activations, whilst driving our reputation and engagement with fans globally? 

We are looking for an experienced Senior Manager to join the Partnership Delivery Team, and you will be responsible for implementing flexible, partner centric servicing and ensuring strong relationship development and renewal of the key partnerships within your portfolio. You will be supported by a team, and you will lead in objective setting, strategic direction, creative development, and delivery of campaign effectiveness. You will be accountable for ensuring key deliverables are achieved to a high standard and that your team prioritises and communicates transparently and effectively.

What will you be doing?

  • Manage a diverse partnership portfolio, including one Principal Partner and multiple secondary partners.
  • Deliver full 360° account management across planning, activation, measurement, reporting, and feedback.
  • Work with Directors and peers to develop strategic plans that enhance partner servicing and maximise contractual rights.
  • Ensure transparency across the Partnerships Team to align partner objectives and maintain a coordinated activation calendar.
  • Build and maintain strong relationships with allocated partners, leading a structured contact strategy including regular calls, video meetings, and face‑to‑face sessions.
  • Maintain consistent, high‑quality communication with partner contacts and develop excellent working relationships.
  • Implement creative, insight‑led marketing activation plans that drive measurable ROI.
  • Lead partner reporting, providing data‑driven updates on global reach, KPI delivery, and ensuring accurate Cost of Sale documentation.
  • Maintain clear visibility of all partner activations, working closely with the Partnership Experience Team to align delivery.
  • Oversee the onboarding and activation of new partnership deals, collaborating with Directors, Commercial teams, and wider stakeholders.
  • Evolve team ways of working and ensure delivery against key objectives and deadlines.
  • Provide day‑to‑day line management, offering guidance to ensure best‑in‑class service from direct reports.
  • Manage team priorities, resources, travel, training, and Cost of Sale budgets across partner accounts.
  • Ensure regular information flow to and from the Directors of Partnership Delivery through structured meetings and status updates.
  • Maintain up‑to‑date knowledge of partner industries and market trends, working closely with the Insights Team.
  • Support leadership in benchmarking Liverpool FC’s partnership management against global sports and entertainment best practice.
  • Build strong, influential relationships with internal departments across the Club.
  • Participate in Club working groups and steering committees where required.
  • Attend matches, international events, industry gatherings, and partner meetings as appropriate.

Who are we looking for?

The successful candidate will bring a minimum of five years’ relevant experience within the sports or entertainment marketing industry, gained on the sponsor, agency, or rightsholder side. They will demonstrate strong integrated marketing knowledge and proven account management expertise across multiple clients or partners. A solid understanding of digital marketing and industry trends, combined with a methodical working style and a high level of accuracy and quality, is essential.

Why should you apply?

This is a full-time perm position working 35 hours per week. Your main base will be our city centre office in Liverpool.

Please note, this vacancy may close early if we receive a high volume of applications, so we encourage you to apply as soon as possible.

To reward your hard work and commitment we offer a competitive salary, 25 days holiday (plus 8 bank holidays and the option to purchase up to an additional 5 days) and access to our benefits kit bag which includes but not limited to:

  • Contributory pension scheme matching up to 5.5%
  • Life Assurance Cover
  • Free financial guidance and mortgage advice
  • Car salary sacrifice scheme for affordable driving
  • Cycle to Work scheme to keep you active
  • Purchase season ticket loans for easier commuting
  • Exclusive shopping discounts & cashback with top retailers
  • Will Writing Service for future planning
  • Employee Assistance Programme for confidential support
  • Medicash Health Cash Plan for everyday healthcare needs
  • Volunteering opportunities to give back to the community
  • Special LFC perks – retail discounts, partner offers, free LFCTV GO access, and priority tickets for matches, events & concerts

…and much more! LFC Benefits.pdf

At Liverpool Football Club, we have an unwavering commitment to equality, diversity and inclusion and are always looking to making a positive difference in the communities that we operate within. We are proud of our achievements in this area; maintaining the Premier League Equality Standard Advanced Level, becoming a founding signatory of the Football Association’s Football Leadership Diversity Code and being recognised as a leader in this important area on and off the pitch. We take our responsibilities in this area seriously and through the work being done across the club, we are committed to increasing the diversity of our people and becoming an increasingly inclusive workplace for all. We are committed to hiring great people representative of diverse backgrounds, perspectives, and skills across our entire business. If you share our enthusiasm and passion for inclusivity, then we want to hear from you.

Liverpool FC is committed to safeguarding and promoting the welfare of children and vulnerable adults and expects all Colleagues and Volunteers to share this commitment.

#LFCJobs

job.title: Casual Hospitality Hosts
job.html_description:
Liverpool Football Club is seeking confident, professional individuals who are passionate about providing exceptional customer service. As a Casual Hospitality Host, you will play a key role in creating a memorable experience for our guests during match days and events throughout the season.

This role offers the chance to work at one of the world’s most iconic football stadiums, in a supportive and professional team. With casual shifts and complimentary Stagecoach transport on match days. If you have a wealth of experience and a professional, friendly approach, this is a great opportunity to bring your skills to a dynamic environment.

What will you be doing in the role?

  • Conducting ticket checks, bag searches, and security wanding to ensure a safe environment for all guests.
  • Escorting guests to their designated boxes, suites, and tables with professionalism and care.
  • Overseeing guest access at doors, escalators, and lifts to ensure smooth and easy transitions.
  • Collaborating with the management team, stewards, and other safety hosts to ensure efficient operations.
  • Offering a high level of customer service to enhance the guest experience.

We are looking for people who:

  • Have excellent communication skills, and a calm and steady approach to challenges.
  • Have a commitment to providing excellent customer service. 
  • Have previous experience in front-of-house or customer service role.
  • Take pride in offering a great service and creating a welcoming environment.
  • Comfortable remaining in one position for extended periods during events.

 A strict dress code applies (all uniform will be provided).

By applying for the position of Casual Hospitality Host with Liverpool Football Club, please be aware that this role requires the completion of a Level 2 Certificate in Spectator Safety (as per the Sports Grounds Safety Authority), which must be completed within 12 months from the date of your first induction training.

By submitting your application, you confirm that you can commit to completing the Level 2 Certificate in Spectator Safety within 12 months from your first induction training. As this qualification is essential for the role, if you are unable to complete it within this timeframe, we may not be able to progress your application.

This is a casual position, offering variable hours including evenings and weekends on a flexible assignment basis.

Liverpool FC is committed to safeguarding and promoting the welfare of children and vulnerable adults and expects all Colleagues and Volunteers to share this commitment.

At Liverpool Football Club, we have an unwavering commitment to equality, diversity and inclusion and are always looking to making a positive difference in the communities that we operate within. We are proud of our achievements in this area; maintaining the Premier League Equality Standard Advanced Level, becoming a founding signatory of the Football Association’s Football Leadership Diversity Code and being recognised as a leader in this important area on and off the pitch. We take our responsibilities in this area seriously and through the work being done across the club, we are committed to increasing the diversity of our people and becoming an increasingly inclusive workplace for all. We are committed to hiring great people representative of diverse backgrounds, perspectives, and skills across our entire business. If you share our enthusiasm and passion for inclusivity, then we want to hear from you.
