job.title: Casual Commis Chef
job.html_description:
Here at Liverpool Football Club, we are looking for Commis Chefs to join our casual culinary team.

As a Casual Commis Chef, you will support the senior chefs in delivering high-quality food across our stadium on a match day. This is a fantastic opportunity for someone at the early stages of their chef career who is eager to learn, develop their skills, and gain experience within a fast-paced stadium environment.

What will you be doing?

  • Assisting in the preparation and cooking of food to the highest standards.
  • Supporting the kitchen team.
  • Ensuring all food is prepared in line with recipes, portion control, and presentation standards.
  • Maintaining high levels of cleanliness and organisation within your section.
  • Following all food safety, allergen, and hygiene procedures at all times.
  • Supporting stock rotation and minimising waste in line with company guidelines.
  • Working collaboratively with colleagues to ensure smooth and efficient service.

Who are we looking for?

  • Some previous experience in a professional kitchen environment (placement, apprenticeship, or similar desirable).
  • A basic food hygiene certificate.
  • Ability to work in a fast-paced environment and remain calm under pressure.
  • Strong team player with a positive, proactive attitude.
  • Willingness to learn from senior chefs and take on feedback.

Why should you apply?

This is a great opportunity to build your culinary career within a dynamic and exciting environment. You will gain exposure to large-scale hospitality environment, working alongside experienced chefs who are passionate about developing talent.

This is a casual position, offering variable hours including evenings and weekends on a flexible assignment basis.

Liverpool FC is committed to safeguarding and promoting the welfare of children and vulnerable adults and expects all Colleagues and Volunteers to share this commitment.

At Liverpool Football Club, we have an unwavering commitment to equality, diversity and inclusion and are always looking to making a positive difference in the communities that we operate within. We are proud of our achievements in this area; maintaining the Premier League Equality Standard Advanced Level, becoming a founding signatory of the Football Association’s Football Leadership Diversity Code and being recognised as a leader in this important area on and off the pitch. We take our responsibilities in this area seriously and through the work being done across the club, we are committed to increasing the diversity of our people and becoming an increasingly inclusive workplace for all. We are committed to hiring great people representative of diverse backgrounds, perspectives, and skills across our entire business. If you share our enthusiasm and passion for inclusivity, then we want to hear from you.

job.title: Casual Chef de Partie
job.html_description:
Here at Liverpool Football Club, we are looking for experienced Chef de Parties to join our casual culinary team on a match day.

As a Casual Chef de Partie, you will take ownership of a designated section within the kitchen, supporting the delivery of high-quality food across our stadium hospitality on a match day. This is an exciting opportunity for a skilled and motivated chef looking to progress within a fast-paced, high-volume environment.

What will you be doing?

  • Leading and managing your designated section to ensure food is prepared and presented to the highest standards.
  • Preparing, cooking and finishing dishes in line with recipes, specifications and service requirements.
  • Supporting junior chefs and commis chefs with guidance, training and day to day supervision.
  • Ensuring consistency in quality, portion control and presentation.
  • Maintaining full compliance with food safety, allergen and hygiene regulations.
  • Monitoring stock levels within your section and supporting stock rotation to minimise waste.
  • Assisting senior chefs with menu preparation, service planning and kitchen organisation.
  • Contributing to a positive, professional and high-performing kitchen culture.

Who are we looking for?

  • Proven experience working at Chef de Partie level or in a similar role within a professional kitchen.
  • Strong technical cooking skills and a solid understanding of kitchen operations.
  • Level 2 Food Safety certificate (minimum).
  • Ability to lead by example and support the development of junior team members.
  • Calm and organised approach under pressure.
  • A genuine passion for food, quality and continuous improvement.

Why should you apply?

This is an excellent opportunity to join a dynamic culinary team. You will play a key role in delivering exceptional food experiences to guests and supporters.

This is a casual position, offering variable hours including evenings and weekends on a flexible assignment basis.

Liverpool FC is committed to safeguarding and promoting the welfare of children and vulnerable adults and expects all Colleagues and Volunteers to share this commitment.

At Liverpool Football Club, we have an unwavering commitment to equality, diversity and inclusion and are always looking to making a positive difference in the communities that we operate within. We are proud of our achievements in this area; maintaining the Premier League Equality Standard Advanced Level, becoming a founding signatory of the Football Association’s Football Leadership Diversity Code and being recognised as a leader in this important area on and off the pitch. We take our responsibilities in this area seriously and through the work being done across the club, we are committed to increasing the diversity of our people and becoming an increasingly inclusive workplace for all. We are committed to hiring great people representative of diverse backgrounds, perspectives, and skills across our entire business. If you share our enthusiasm and passion for inclusivity, then we want to hear from you.

job.title: LFCW Kit and Equipment Manager
job.location: AXA Melwood Training Centre, United Kingdom
job.html_description:
We have an exciting opportunity for an individual to join our LFC Women’s team as a Kit and Equipment Manager. 

Liverpool FC Women is embarking on an exciting and transformative phase, with a clear ambition to compete consistently at the top of the Women’s game. Central to this vision is the development of a world class team on and off the pitch. 

As part of this journey, we are seeking a highly skilled Kit & Equipment Manager to support Liverpool FC Women’s first team players and staff. This role is instrumental in the day-to-day operations of the football club and we are looking for an extremely organised individual with a keen eye for detail.

Working closely with cross functional teams including coaching, performance, medical and football leadership – you will be a flexible team player with strong time management skills. 

What will you be doing?

  • Lead the LFC Women Kit Department to ensure there are effective processes and practices in place that can service the team to the best possible standard; liaising with the LFCW management, coaching and performance staff on all matters concerning kit and equipment. 
  • Develop strong working relationships with LFC external partners and internal staff from cross-club departments. 
  • Develop and maintain an electronic monitoring and logging system for kit and equipment that provides live data of stock levels and planned distribution.
  • Ensuring the effective logistical planning for all required kit and equipment, attending home/away games as required in liaison with the Management and Coaching Staff.
  • Organise and co-ordinate all kit and equipment for away travel and the loading of club transport ready for departure.
  • Ensure all kit is fully laundered (washed and dried) correctly and packed away within the required timelines.
  • Maintain the quality and cleanliness of all kit and equipment assigned to LFCW.
  • Mark and maintain all kit with the relevant initials/numbers, as directed by the Operations Manager.
  • Maintain accurate records of all kit logs and report discrepancies to the Operations Manager.

What we are looking for: 

The successful candidate will hold a clean driving licence and demonstrate meticulous attention to detail, consistently upholding high standards of behaviour and appearance. Previous experience in inventory management is essential, along with a background in working within a professional football club or sports-based environment, particularly with high-performance athletes. A clean driving licence and having the flexibility to travel nationally and internationally are essential.  Candidates must possess certification in Safeguarding and Protecting Children (or FA equivalent) prior to starting the role. While not essential, the ability to speak additional languages and prior experience in the women’s game are highly desirable.

Please note, this vacancy may close early if we receive a high volume of applications, so we encourage you to apply as soon as possible.

To reward your hard work and commitment we offer a competitive salary, 25 days holiday (plus 8 bank holidays and the option to purchase up to an additional 5 days) and access to our benefits kit bag which includes but not limited to:

  • Contributory pension scheme matching up to 5.5%
  • Life Assurance Cover
  • Free financial guidance and mortgage advice
  • Car salary sacrifice scheme for affordable driving
  • Cycle to Work scheme to keep you active
  • Purchase season ticket loans for easier commuting
  • Exclusive shopping discounts & cashback with top retailers
  • Will Writing Service for future planning
  • Employee Assistance Programme for confidential support
  • Medicash Health Cash Plan for everyday healthcare needs
  • Volunteering opportunities to give back to the community
  • Special LFC perks – retail discounts, partner offers, free LFCTV GO access, and priority tickets for matches, events & concerts

…and much more! LFC Benefits.pdf

At Liverpool Football Club, we have an unwavering commitment to equality, diversity and inclusion and are always looking to making a positive difference in the communities that we operate within. We are proud of our achievements in this area; maintaining the Premier League Equality Standard Advanced Level, becoming a founding signatory of the Football Association’s Football Leadership Diversity Code and being recognised as a leader in this important area on and off the pitch. We take our responsibilities in this area seriously and through the work being done across the club, we are committed to increasing the diversity of our people and becoming an increasingly inclusive workplace for all. We are committed to hiring great people representative of diverse backgrounds, perspectives, and skills across our entire business. If you share our enthusiasm and passion for inclusivity, then we want to hear from you.

Liverpool FC is committed to safeguarding and promoting the welfare of children and vulnerable adults and expects all Colleagues and Volunteers to share this commitment. This role is subject to a satisfactory enhanced DBS check.

Candidates must possess certification in Safeguarding and Protecting Children (or FA equivalent) prior to starting the role. While not essential, the ability to speak additional languages and prior experience in the women’s game are highly desirable.
Please note, this vacancy may close early if we receive a high volume of applications, so we encourage you to apply as soon as possible.
To reward your hard work and commitment we offer a competitive salary, 25 days holiday (plus 8 bank holidays and the option to purchase up to an additional 5 days) and access to our benefits kit bag which includes but not limited to:
- Contributory pension scheme matching up to 5.5% - Life Assurance Cover - Free financial guidance and mortgage advice - Car salary sacrifice scheme for affordable driving - Cycle to Work scheme to keep you active - Purchase season ticket loans for easier commuting - Exclusive shopping discounts & cashback with top retailers - Will Writing Service for future planning - Employee Assistance Programme for confidential support - Medicash Health Cash Plan for everyday healthcare needs - Volunteering opportunities to give back to the community - Special LFC perks – retail discounts, partner offers, free LFCTV GO access, and priority tickets for matches, events & concerts
…and much more! LFC Benefits.pdf

job.title: Data Engineer
job.html_description:
We are recruiting for a Data Engineer to be responsible for the continued development and improvement of the club’s data platform. Responsibility for technical direction and architectural standards across data pipelines, warehouse infrastructure and model deployment.

Working closely with Research Systems, Data Science and Performance Insights, the Data Engineer ensures that our data infrastructure remains reliable, scalable and aligned with the evolving needs of the department.
job.plain_text_description:
We are recruiting for a Data Engineer to be responsible for the continued development and improvement of the club’s data platform. Responsibility for technical direction and architectural standards across data pipelines, warehouse infrastructure and model deployment.
Working closely with Research Systems, Data Science and Performance Insights, the Data Engineer ensures that our data infrastructure remains reliable, scalable and aligned with the evolving needs of the department.

job.title: Marketing Salesforce Administrator
job.html_description:
Are you a Salesforce Administrator with a passion for driving impactful marketing campaigns and transforming data into powerful insights? We’re looking for an energetic, forward‑thinking individual to join our Marketing team and play a key role in shaping the future of our club’s marketing and sales success.

In this role, you’ll take ownership of the management of our Salesforce environment, technically owning the development of the platform, sending out marketing campaigns and supporting the sales team to ensure that leads and contacts are managed efficiently.

As part of a highly collaborative team, you will support the club’s data and marketing technology capabilities, driving sales lead management, marketing automation, sales process optimisation, and insightful reporting. Your work will directly support our sales and account management teams to achieve ambitious targets in the acquisition and retention of Hospitality and non‑matchday events.

What will you be doing?

• Continue the enhancement of the platform by gathering requirements, investigating and proposing solutions, integrating with new apps and collaborating with third parties.

• Salesforce configuration and maintenance including Flow, fields, page layouts, record types and custom settings.

• Be the first point of contact for all daily system admin, user and access management, telephony management, support and training for all users.

• Collaborate with the wider Customer Marketing team with the planning of campaigns and be responsible for segmenting campaign audiences, testing, execution, and post campaign analysis of all Salesforce email campaigns.

• Use of key technologies, such as marketing automation, to drive efficiency in the sales process ensuring the most effective leads are acquired and prioritised for direct contact by the Sales Team.

• Produce a range of dashboards, reports and insight using existing club and third-party data enrichment to ensure senior management and the Sales Team have the necessary information to drive business decisions.

• Manage data quality within Salesforce, keep a monthly tracker of data quality changes and identify and rectify issues.

• Work with the Hospitality and Legal teams to ensure all products and electronic contracts are created and maintained within Salesforce available for the sales team to send to clients.

• Work with the Senior Manager – Marketing Data Environments to use data to help shape the Hospitality and Events sales and service strategies set by the Sales Director.

Who are we looking for?

To succeed in this role, you’ll bring a strong track record as a Salesforce Administrator and, ideally, hold a Salesforce Administrator certification. You will have hands-on experience delivering marketing campaigns in a B2B or B2C environment and be highly numerate, with advanced Microsoft Excel skills.

With a logical and analytical mindset, you’ll be confident working with large datasets, able to manipulate, segment, analyse and translate complex information into clear, actionable insights for stakeholders.

You will have excellent organisational and prioritisation skills, with the ability to work accurately under pressure and to tight deadlines. Strong interpersonal skills are essential, and you’ll thrive in a fast-paced marketing and sales environment. You’ll be equally comfortable working collaboratively as part of a team or independently.

Why should you apply?

This is a full-time perm position working 35 hours per week. Your main base will be our city centre office in Liverpool.

Please note, this vacancy may close early if we receive a high volume of applications, so we encourage you to apply as soon as possible.

To reward your hard work and commitment we offer a competitive salary, 25 days holiday (plus 8 bank holidays and the option to purchase up to an additional 5 days) and access to our benefits kit bag which includes but not limited to:

· Contributory pension scheme matching up to 5.5%

· Life Assurance Cover

· Free financial guidance and mortgage advice

· Car salary sacrifice scheme for affordable driving

· Cycle to Work scheme to keep you active

· Purchase season ticket loans for easier commuting

· Exclusive shopping discounts & cashback with top retailers

· Will Writing Service for future planning

· Employee Assistance Programme for confidential support

· Medicash Health Cash Plan for everyday healthcare needs

· Volunteering opportunities to give back to the community

· Special LFC perks – retail discounts, partner offers, free LFCTV GO access, and priority tickets for matches, events & concerts … and much more! LFC Benefits.pdf

At Liverpool Football Club, we have an unwavering commitment to equality, diversity and inclusion and are always looking to making a positive difference in the communities that we operate within. We are proud of our achievements in this area; maintaining the Premier League Equality Standard Advanced Level, becoming a founding signatory of the Football

Association’s Football Leadership Diversity Code and being recognised as a leader in this important area on and off the pitch. We take our responsibilities in this area seriously and through the work being done across the club, we are committed to increasing the diversity of our people and becoming an increasingly inclusive workplace for all. We are committed to hiring great people representative of diverse backgrounds, perspectives, and skills across our entire business. If you share our enthusiasm and passion for inclusivity, then we want to hear from you.

Liverpool FC is committed to safeguarding and promoting the welfare of children and vulnerable adults and expects all Colleagues and Volunteers to share this commitment.

#LFCJobs

job.title: Partnership Operations Manager
job.html_description:
We are looking for a Manager, Partnership Operations to join the team at Liverpool FC. 

As a Manager within the Partnerships Operations team, you will provide strategic direction and operational leadership across all partnership delivery activity. The role spans multiple LFC sites, including Anfield, St Helens Stadium, and the AXA Melwood Training Centre.

Operating at both a strategic and hands-on level, the post holder ensures the seamless execution of branding, activation, and event delivery across all venues. Acting as the central coordination point between the Partnership Delivery team and wider club stakeholders, the Partnership Operations Manager ensures that all partnership rights are delivered to the highest standard aligned with club values and reflective of a world‑class global sports organisation.

What will you be doing?

Multi- Site Operational Leadership 

  • Day to Day operational readiness across - Anfield, St Helens and AXA Melwood. 
  • Ensure all processes across sites are efficient and impactful. Lead on reviewing and updating processes as and when required.  
  • Ensure consistency in safety and presentation across all partner activations across all sites.  
  • Collaborate with internal departments and build strong relationships to ensure effective communication of partnership plans are filtered out across the club. 
  • Act as a senior escalation point for operational challenges, driving resolution and continuous improvement across sites. 

Partnership Branding & Delivery 

  • Align and work closely with the delivery team to ensure effective communication of contractual rights delivery of all rights – from planning to delivery stage. 
  • Provide strategic oversight of seasonal branding inventories (e.g. LED, matchday programme, static signage), ensuring optimisation and compliance 
  • Oversee the tracking of branding rights throughout the season e.g. matchday programme, LED etc. 
  • Overseeing the management and planning on the usage of rights – specifically Play on Pitch, Open Training, LED Boards, Static signage, Hospitality Branding, Dressing Room Branding, Fan zone activations, digital integrations and LFC Legends Match. 

Matchday, Fixture Operations & Events 

  • Ensure all relevant health and safety documentation is provided in a timely manner. 
  • Oversee contractor management, risk assessments, and method statements. 
  • Lead partnership operational contact for any adhoc matches held at Anfield eg Play On pitch. 
  • Oversee all partner related events, private hire or activity across all venues. 

 Team leadership 

  • Lead and develop the Partnerships Operations team, embedding clear processes, accountability and performance standards. 
  • Mentor the Senior Executive across operational delivery areas, providing strategic direction and professional development support 
  • Promote a positive and collaborative team culture 

Who are we looking for? 

The ideal candidate will hold a bachelor’s degree and bring proven experience in commercial partnerships, sponsorship operations, or similar roles within the sports or media industry. They will demonstrate strong project management capability, with the ability to prioritise effectively, manage timelines, and coordinate multiple stakeholders. Excellent leadership, organisational skills, and meticulous attention to detail are essential to ensure accuracy and consistency across operational processes and contract management. Strong communication and interpersonal skills are also key, enabling effective collaboration and relationship-building with both internal teams and external partners.

Why should you apply?

This is a full-time perm position working 35 hours per week. Your main base will be our city centre office in Liverpool.

Please note, this vacancy may close early if we receive a high volume of applications, so we encourage you to apply as soon as possible.

To reward your hard work and commitment we offer a competitive salary, 25 days holiday (plus 8 bank holidays and the option to purchase up to an additional 5 days) and access to our benefits kit bag which includes but not limited to:

  • Contributory pension scheme matching up to 5.5%
  • Life Assurance Cover
  • Free financial guidance and mortgage advice
  • Car salary sacrifice scheme for affordable driving
  • Cycle to Work scheme to keep you active
  • Purchase season ticket loans for easier commuting
  • Exclusive shopping discounts & cashback with top retailers
  • Will Writing Service for future planning
  • Employee Assistance Programme for confidential support
  • Medicash Health Cash Plan for everyday healthcare needs
  • Volunteering opportunities to give back to the community
  • Special LFC perks – retail discounts, partner offers, free LFCTV GO access, and priority tickets for matches, events & concerts

…and much more! LFC Benefits.pdf

At Liverpool Football Club, we have an unwavering commitment to equality, diversity and inclusion and are always looking to making a positive difference in the communities that we operate within. We are proud of our achievements in this area; maintaining the Premier League Equality Standard Advanced Level, becoming a founding signatory of the Football Association’s Football Leadership Diversity Code and being recognised as a leader in this important area on and off the pitch. We take our responsibilities in this area seriously and through the work being done across the club, we are committed to increasing the diversity of our people and becoming an increasingly inclusive workplace for all. We are committed to hiring great people representative of diverse backgrounds, perspectives, and skills across our entire business. If you share our enthusiasm and passion for inclusivity, then we want to hear from you.

Liverpool FC is committed to safeguarding and promoting the welfare of children and vulnerable adults and expects all Colleagues and Volunteers to share this commitment.

#LFCJobs

job.title: Casual Kitchen Assistant
job.html_description:
We are looking for experienced Kitchen Assistants to join our casual catering team at our AXA Training Centre. You’ll work in our state-of-the-art kitchens alongside our talented and creative chefs, supporting the delivery of first-class food and service.

 What will you be doing?

  • Assisting with basic food preparation.
  • Unloading and storing deliveries safely and efficiently.
  • Maintaining cleanliness by emptying bins, sweeping, and mopping floors.
  • Ensuring all kitchen equipment is cleaned and stored correctly.
  • Deep cleaning fridges, storage areas, and other kitchen spaces to maintain hygiene standards.
  • Operating automated dishwashers to clean crockery and utensils used by guests.
  • Supporting with porter duties, ensuring all back-of-house areas are tidy and ready for the next event.

Who are we looking for?

We’re looking for hardworking individuals who thrive in a fast-paced kitchen environment.

Please note you must be 18 or over to be considered for this position.

 You’ll have:

  • Previous experience as a Kitchen Assistant, Kitchen Porter, or in a similar role.
  • A good understanding of manual handling techniques and safe working practices.
  • Experience using a range of kitchen equipment.
  • A positive attitude and a commitment to maintaining high standards of cleanliness and teamwork.

Why should you apply?

This is a casual position, offering variable hours including evenings and weekends on a flexible assignment basis.

Liverpool FC is committed to safeguarding and promoting the welfare of children and vulnerable adults and expects all Colleagues and Volunteers to share this commitment.

At Liverpool Football Club, we have an unwavering commitment to equality, diversity and inclusion and are always looking to making a positive difference in the communities that we operate within. We are proud of our achievements in this area; maintaining the Premier League Equality Standard Advanced Level, becoming a founding signatory of the Football Association’s Football Leadership Diversity Code and being recognised as a leader in this important area on and off the pitch. We take our responsibilities in this area seriously and through the work being done across the club, we are committed to increasing the diversity of our people and becoming an increasingly inclusive workplace for all. We are committed to hiring great people representative of diverse backgrounds, perspectives, and skills across our entire business. job.title: Casual Match Day Line Chef
job.html_description:
We are currently recruiting for experienced Line Chefs to support our casual catering team.

This is a fast-paced role, ideal for chefs who are confident working in high-volume environments and delivering consistent, quality food service.

What will you be doing?

  • Preparing and cooking core match day menu items (e.g. burgers, chicken wings and other grab and go favourites).
  • Supporting with preparation and delivery of any match day specials (varies per fixture).
  • Ensuring food is prepared, cooked and presented to company standards.
  • Maintaining high standards of food hygiene, safety and cleanliness at all times.
  • Working collaboratively with the wider kitchen and hospitality teams to ensure smooth service.

Who are we looking for?

  • Previous experience at prep chef level or similar.
  • Level 2 Food Hygiene Certificate (essential).
  • Confident cooking a consistent menu in a high-volume environment.
  • Strong team player with a positive, reliable attitude.

Why should you apply?

This is a casual position, offering variable hours including evenings and weekends on a flexible assignment basis.

Liverpool FC is committed to safeguarding and promoting the welfare of children and vulnerable adults and expects all Colleagues and Volunteers to share this commitment.

job.title: In-House Employment Lawyer
job.html_description:
In-House Employment Lawyer

We have an exciting opportunity for an individual to join our Liverpool FC, in-house Legal team. We’re looking for an individual with expert commercial employment lawyer to provide pragmatic, timely advice across the Club, helping to ensure our people practices, decision making and policies comply with employment legislation, data protection requirements and international employment standards.

In this high volume, fast paced environment, you’ll report to the Vice President, Legal and partner closely with the People & Culture team, offering hands on case management., and proactive legal insight to minimise risk and strengthen internal capability. 

You will be expected to handle complex ER casework, DSARs, contractual queries and global employment issues. 

What will you be doing?

Employment Law Advise 

  • Provide expert, commercially focused employment law advice to leaders and People & Culture teams and business leaders.. 
  • Advise across all areas of UK employment law, 
  • Monitor developments in employment legislation and ensure the Club remains compliant and future‑ready. 
  • Produce clear legal guidance, risk assessments and recommended options. 

Complex Case Management 

  • Lead ER cases 
  • Guide the ER Manager and People team 
  • Coach managers and People Partners on case strategy and risk management.  

Data Protection & DSARs 

  • Lead all employment‑related DSAR responses in partnership with People Services and Data Protection teams.
  • Advise on GDPR compliance in investigations, suspensions and information sharing.
  • Recommend process improvements to reduce risk and improve efficiency. 

Contracts, Policies & Governance 

  • Draft and review employment contracts, senior agreements, and people policies.
  • Ensure policies are legally compliant and aligned with Club culture.
  • Support global/overseas contract implementation and compliance. 

Global & Overseas Employment Support 

  • Provide employment law advice for international hires, assignments and cross‑border working. 
  • Ensure alignment with local labour laws and global mobility requirements. 

Stakeholder Management & Collaboration

  • Build strong partnerships across Legal, People & Culture, Safeguarding, Finance and operational teams.
  • Manage relationships with external legal advisers to ensure quality and cost control.

Capability Building & Continuous Improvement 

  • Deliver training on employment law, ER processes, DSARs and governance. 
  • Embed strong legal reasoning across people processes to reduce risk. 
  • Promote consistent, transparent and fair decision‑making across the Club

Who are we looking for?

  • Able to translate complex legal principles into clear, practical, and commercially focused advice.
  • Handle sensitive information with discretion, sound judgment, and absolute integrity.
  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
  • Confident presenting to senior stakeholders.
  • Strong analytical ability and able to work accurately under pressure.
  • Highly organised, with the ability to manage a high volume of varied work.
  • Collaborative, people‑focused and solutions‑oriented approach.
  • Acts as a role model for the Club’s values, demonstrating professionalism, trust and accountability in all situations.
  • Calm, pragmatic, and with strong attention detail.
  • Committed to continuous improvement and developing capability across the organisation.
  • Qualified solicitor (England & Wales) with minimum 5 years’ PQE in employment law.
  • Current practicing certificate (SRA) with up-to-date CPD 
  • Strong background in high risk, complex employee relations and Employment Tribunal litigation.
  • Experience working in fast paced, high profile or highly regulated environments (e.g. sport, media, large corporates).
  • Demonstrable experience handling DSARs, GDPR related issues and data heavy case management.
  • Confident in drafting and negotiating employment contracts, policies, settlement agreements and international terms.

Desirable Experience

  • Experience advising on overseas employment matters or global mobility 
  • Experience of working in house 
  • Experience of commercial legal work 

Please note, this vacancy may close early if we receive a high volume of applications, so we encourage you to apply as soon as possible.

To reward your hard work and commitment we offer a competitive salary, 25 days holiday (plus 8 bank holidays and the option to purchase up to an additional 5 days) and access to our benefits kit bag which includes but not limited to:

  • Contributory pension scheme matching up to 5.5%
  • Life Assurance Cover
  • Free financial guidance and mortgage advice
  • Car salary sacrifice scheme for affordable driving
  • Cycle to Work scheme to keep you active
  • Purchase season ticket loans for easier commuting
  • Exclusive shopping discounts & cashback with top retailers
  • Will Writing Service for future planning
  • Employee Assistance Programme for confidential support
  • Medicash Health Cash Plan for everyday healthcare needs
  • Volunteering opportunities to give back to the community
  • Special LFC perks – retail discounts, partner offers, free LFCTV GO access, and priority tickets for matches, events & concerts

…and much more! LFC Benefits.pdf

At Liverpool Football Club, we have an unwavering commitment to equality, diversity and inclusion and are always looking to making a positive difference in the communities that we operate within. We are proud of our achievements in this area; maintaining the Premier League Equality Standard Advanced Level, becoming a founding signatory of the Football Association’s Football Leadership Diversity Code and being recognised as a leader in this important area on and off the pitch. We take our responsibilities in this area seriously and through the work being done across the club, we are committed to increasing the diversity of our people and becoming an increasingly inclusive workplace for all. We are committed to hiring great people representative of diverse backgrounds, perspectives, and skills across our entire business. If you share our enthusiasm and passion for inclusivity, then we want to hear from you.

Liverpool FC is committed to safeguarding and promoting the welfare of children and vulnerable adults and expects all Colleagues and Volunteers to share this commitment.

#LFCJobs
job.plain_text_description:
In-House Employment Lawyer
We have an exciting opportunity for an individual to join our Liverpool FC, in-house Legal team. We’re looking for an individual with expert commercial employment lawyer to provide pragmatic, timely advice across the Club, helping to ensure our people practices, decision making and policies comply with employment legislation, data protection requirements and international employment standards.
In this high volume, fast paced environment, you’ll report to the Vice President, Legal and partner closely with the People & Culture team, offering hands on case management., and proactive legal insight to minimise risk and strengthen internal capability.
You will be expected to handle complex ER casework, DSARs, contractual queries and global employment issues.
What will you be doing?
Employment Law Advise
- Provide expert, commercially focused employment law advice to leaders and People & Culture teams and business leaders.. - Advise across all areas of UK employment law, - Monitor developments in employment legislation and ensure the Club remains compliant and future‑ready. - Produce clear legal guidance, risk assessments and recommended options.
Complex Case Management
- Lead ER cases - Guide the ER Manager and People team - Coach managers and People Partners on case strategy and risk management.
Data Protection & DSARs
- Lead all employment‑related DSAR responses in partnership with People Services and Data Protection teams. - Advise on GDPR compliance in investigations, suspensions and information sharing. - Recommend process improvements to reduce risk and improve efficiency.
Contracts, Policies & Governance
- Draft and review employment contracts, senior agreements, and people policies. - Ensure policies are legally compliant and aligned with Club culture. - Support global/overseas contract implementation and compliance.
Global & Overseas Employment Support
- Provide employment law advice for international hires, assignments and cross‑border working. - Ensure alignment with local labour laws and global mobility requirements.
Stakeholder Management & Collaboration
- Build strong partnerships across Legal, People & Culture, Safeguarding, Finance and operational teams. - Manage relationships with external legal advisers to ensure quality and cost control.
Capability Building & Continuous Improvement
- Deliver training on employment law, ER processes, DSARs and governance. - Embed strong legal reasoning across people processes to reduce risk. - Promote consistent, transparent and fair decision‑making across the Club
Who are we looking for?
- Able to translate complex legal principles into clear, practical, and commercially focused advice. - Handle sensitive information with discretion, sound judgment, and absolute integrity. - Excellent written and verbal communication skills. - Confident presenting to senior stakeholders. - Strong analytical ability and able to work accurately under pressure. - Highly organised, with the ability to manage a high volume of varied work. - Collaborative, people‑focused and solutions‑oriented approach. - Acts as a role model for the Club’s values, demonstrating professionalism, trust and accountability in all situations. - Calm, pragmatic, and with strong attention detail. - Committed to continuous improvement and developing capability across the organisation. - Qualified solicitor (England & Wales) with minimum 5 years’ PQE in employment law. - Current practicing certificate (SRA) with up-to-date CPD - Strong background in high risk, complex employee relations and Employment Tribunal litigation. - Experience working in fast paced, high profile or highly regulated environments (e.g. sport, media, large corporates). - Demonstrable experience handling DSARs, GDPR related issues and data heavy case management. - Confident in drafting and negotiating employment contracts, policies, settlement agreements and international terms.
Desirable Experience
- Experience advising on overseas employment matters or global mobility - Experience of working in house - Experience of commercial legal work
Please note, this vacancy may close early if we receive a high volume of applications, so we encourage you to apply as soon as possible.
To reward your hard work and commitment we offer a competitive salary, 25 days holiday (plus 8 bank holidays and the option to purchase up to an additional 5 days) and access to our benefits kit bag which includes but not limited to:
- Contributory pension scheme matching up to 5.5% - Life Assurance Cover - Free financial guidance and mortgage advice - Car salary sacrifice scheme for affordable driving - Cycle to Work scheme to keep you active - Purchase season ticket loans for easier commuting - Exclusive shopping discounts & cashback with top retailers - Will Writing Service for future planning - Employee Assistance Programme for confidential support - Medicash Health Cash Plan for everyday healthcare needs - Volunteering opportunities to give back to the community - Special LFC perks – retail discounts, partner offers, free LFCTV GO access, and priority tickets for matches, events & concerts
…and much more! LFC Benefits.pdf

job.title: Casual VIP Hospitality & Bar Staff
job.html_description:
Here at Liverpool Football Club, we are recruiting experienced Hospitality & Bar staff to deliver exceptional service within our VIP and premium lounges on matchdays, on a casual basis.

This role is suited to hospitality professionals who are confident working in premium, fast-paced environments.

What will you be doing?

  • Delivering high-quality table service within VIP and corporate hospitality lounges.
  • Providing professional bar service including draft, bottled and premium beverages.
  • Serving plated meals, set menus and drinks efficiently during peak service periods.
  • Supporting smooth and coordinated service during high-volume matchday intervals.
  • Maintaining exceptional presentation standards across dining and bar areas.
  • Demonstrating strong allergen awareness and product knowledge.

Who are we looking for?

  • Previous experience within a premium hospitality environment, such as fine dining restaurants, corporate hospitality, VIP lounges or stadium hospitality.
  • Experience working within high-volume, time sensitive service environments (e.g. matchdays, large scale events, peak restaurant service).
  • Confident providing professional bar service including draft, bottled and premium beverages.
  • Strong understanding of service etiquette, guest engagement and presentation standards.
  • Demonstrable allergen awareness and food safety knowledge.

This is a casual position, offering variable hours including evenings and weekends on a flexible assignment basis.

Liverpool FC is committed to safeguarding and promoting the welfare of children and vulnerable adults and expects all Colleagues and Volunteers to share this commitment.

At Liverpool Football Club, we have an unwavering commitment to equality, diversity and inclusion and are always looking to making a positive difference in the communities that we operate within. We are proud of our achievements in this area; maintaining the Premier League Equality Standard Advanced Level, becoming a founding signatory of the Football Association’s Football Leadership Diversity Code and being recognised as a leader in this important area on and off the pitch. We take our responsibilities in this area seriously and through the work being done across the club, we are committed to increasing the diversity of our people and becoming an increasingly inclusive workplace for all. We are committed to hiring great people representative of diverse backgrounds, perspectives, and skills across our entire business. If you share our enthusiasm and passion for inclusivity, then we want to hear from you.

job.title: Casual Match Day Stewards
job.html_description:
Here at Liverpool Football Club, we are looking for Casual Match Day Stewards to join our team.

As part of the recruitment process, shortlisted candidates will be invited to attend a Group Induction & Interview. During the induction, members of our Safety Team will introduce the role, outline key responsibilities, and provide essential information. This will be followed by individual 121 interviews to assess your suitability for the position.

Please submit your application for the role to be considered for invitation to a Group Induction & Interview session.

What will you be doing in the role?

  • Working as part of a team to provide a safe and secure environment for match day events.
  • Ensuring compliance with stadium regulations, keeping spectators safe and informed.
  • Responding effectively to any incidents of disorder with a calm and focused approach.
  • Offering support and guidance to spectators when needed, ensuring they feel welcome and informed.
  • Conducting personal searches of spectators entering the stadium to maintain security.
  • Documenting and reporting incidents, ensuring clear and accurate records are kept.

We are looking for people who:

  • Have strong communication skills who can confidently engage with supporters, even in challenging situations. 
  • Can handle conflict calmly and professionally.
  • Can write clear reports and possess a basic understanding of conflict management.
  • Are over 18, due to the nature of the role.

If you are someone who can bring experience, calm, and a professional approach to this important role, we would love to hear from you.

By applying for the position of Casual Match Day Steward with Liverpool Football Club, please be aware that this role requires the completion of a Level 2 Certificate in Spectator Safety (as per the Sports Grounds Safety Authority), which must be completed within 12 months from the date of your first induction training.

By submitting your application, you confirm that you can commit to completing the Level 2 Certificate in Spectator Safety within 12 months from your first induction training. As this qualification is essential for the role, if you are unable to complete it within this timeframe, we may not be able to progress your application.

This is a casual position, offering variable hours including evenings and weekends on a flexible assignment basis.

Liverpool FC is committed to safeguarding and promoting the welfare of children and vulnerable adults and expects all Colleagues and Volunteers to share this commitment.

At Liverpool Football Club, we have an unwavering commitment to equality, diversity and inclusion and are always looking to making a positive difference in the communities that we operate within. We are proud of our achievements in this area; maintaining the Premier League Equality Standard Advanced Level, becoming a founding signatory of the Football Association’s Football Leadership Diversity Code and being recognised as a leader in this important area on and off the pitch. We take our responsibilities in this area seriously and through the work being done across the club, we are committed to increasing the diversity of our people and becoming an increasingly inclusive workplace for all. We are committed to hiring great people representative of diverse backgrounds, perspectives, and skills across our entire business. If you share our enthusiasm and passion for inclusivity, then we want to hear from you.

job.title: Operational Administrator, Distribution Centre
job.html_description:
We have an exciting opportunity for an individual to join our Liverpool FC team as a Operational Administrator at our Distribution Centre. This role will be working side by side with our operational team and is a warehouse shop-floor based role.

In this role, you will provide vital administrative support to Supervisors and operational teams, ensuring smooth order processing, accurate reporting, and ongoing SLA compliance. You will investigate exceptions, monitor stock and operational data, compile reports, and work closely with internal and external stakeholders to resolve queries. You’ll also support team rostering, training, and onboarding activities, while contributing to process improvements across the operation. Your work will ensure data is accurate, compliant, and clearly communicated to management, helping to inform decisions and drive strong operational performance.

What will you be doing?

Order Processing & Administration

  • Process and release orders in line with operational schedules and SLAs.
  • Investigate order delays or exceptions, identifying root causes and escalating where needed.
  • Maintain accurate records and ensure data integrity across WMS/ERP systems.
  • Carrying out all operational administration tasks related to Retail Store deliveries.
  • Undertaking all operational administration tasks related to customer ecommerce orders and returns.  

Internal & External Support

  • Provide administrative support to retail stores, customer service, planning, finance, and other teams.
  • Respond to queries on orders, stock, and deliveries from internal stakeholders and external partners.
  • Prepare and distribute reports, dashboards, and operational metrics for management.
  • Stock & Data Management
  • Support stock administration, system updates, and discrepancy reconciliation.
  • Assist with cycle counts and inventory activities.
  • Monitor data accuracy and help maintain loss‑prevention processes.

Staffing & Training Support

  • Assist Supervisors with rostering, absence tracking, and shift planning.
  • Support onboarding and training of colleagues on systems and processes.
  • Act as a point of contact for administrative guidance.
  • Process Improvement & Compliance
  • Identify recurring issues and contribute to process improvements.
  • Support implementation of new systems or procedures.
  • Ensure administrative tasks comply with company policies, audit standards, and regulatory requirements.

Communication & Coordination

  • Maintain strong communication with operational teams and cross‑functional departments.
  • Provide timely updates on order exceptions, stock challenges, and admin bottlenecks.
  • Act as a liaison to ensure smooth and efficient information flow.

Who are we looking for?

To be successful, you will have proven experience and knowledge of working in a administrative position in a distribution centre environment or a similar setting. You will be analytical and with great troubleshooting skills when dealing with stock investigations. You will understand business key trading periods and WMS experience and knowledge ideally PVX and Netsuite. 

You will have good organisational skills with excellent attention to detail and the ability to work to and meet deadlines. You will have strong verbal and written communication skills with a "can do" approach and have the flexibility to work within our operational administration team. You will be able to work in a fast-paced environment and work well within a team environment.

Why should you apply?

This is a full-time perm position working 35 hours per week working any five days out of seven which will include regular weekend working. Your main base will be our Distribution centre.

Please note, this vacancy may close early if we receive a high volume of applications, so we encourage you to apply as soon as possible.

To reward your hard work and commitment we offer a competitive salary, 25 days holiday (plus 8 bank holidays and the option to purchase up to an additional 5 days) and access to our benefits kit bag which includes but not limited to:

  • Contributory pension scheme matching up to 5.5%
  • Life Assurance Cover
  • Free financial guidance and mortgage advice
  • Car salary sacrifice scheme for affordable driving
  • Cycle to Work scheme to keep you active
  • Purchase season ticket loans for easier commuting
  • Exclusive shopping discounts & cashback with top retailers
  • Will Writing Service for future planning
  • Employee Assistance Programme for confidential support
  • Medicash Health Cash Plan for everyday healthcare needs
  • Volunteering opportunities to give back to the community
  • Special LFC perks – retail discounts, partner offers, free LFCTV GO access, and priority tickets for matches, events & concerts

…and much more! LFC Benefits.pdf

At Liverpool Football Club, we have an unwavering commitment to equality, diversity and inclusion and are always looking to making a positive difference in the communities that we operate within. We are proud of our achievements in this area; maintaining the Premier League Equality Standard Advanced Level, becoming a founding signatory of the Football Association’s Football Leadership Diversity Code and being recognised as a leader in this important area on and off the pitch. We take our responsibilities in this area seriously and through the work being done across the club, we are committed to increasing the diversity of our people and becoming an increasingly inclusive workplace for all. We are committed to hiring great people representative of diverse backgrounds, perspectives, and skills across our entire business. If you share our enthusiasm and passion for inclusivity, then we want to hear from you.

Liverpool FC is committed to safeguarding and promoting the welfare of children and vulnerable adults and expects all Colleagues and Volunteers to share this commitment.

#LFCJobs

job.title: Academy Regional Scout - Grassroots
job.location: London Discipline: Coaching and development Job type: Permanent Job ref: 008322 Published: 14 days ago Expiry date: 09 Mar 2026 12:00
job.html_description:
Academy Regional Scout – London Grass Roots

Newcastle United

Permanent / Part Time

London 

Competitive Salary

We are the heartbeat of the city. Come and be a part of a long and proud history where we strive to be the best in everything we do, on and off the pitch. Bringing the people and communities together, join the Newcastle United Family, as we embark on the next steps of our exciting journey.

About the role

If you have a passion for spotting emerging talent and helping young players reach their potential, this is your opportunity to play a key role within our Academy Recruitment team and be part of the team shaping the future of Newcastle United.

You’ll be integral to identifying high-quality grassroots players in the London area and ensuring our club continues to grow a diverse, exciting pipeline of young talent.

You will identify, assess, and recommend talented young players across your designated grassroots region, working closely with the London Grassroots & UK Non‑League Scout and the wider Academy Recruitment team. You’ll represent Newcastle United in the local football community, uphold our values, and ensure our talent identification processes align with EPPP and governing body regulations.

What we’re looking for - essential

You will possess:

*       FA Talent ID Level 2

*       FA Safeguarding Certificate

*       Experience working within a professional football academy or recruitment environment

*       Proven experience identifying talented young athletes

*       Ability to maintain confidentiality of sensitive information

*       Experience working with both live and video‑based talent assessment

*       Strong report‑writing and communication skills

*       Knowledge of the local talent pool within your designated region

*       Strong organisational skills and ability to work independently

*       Competent in Microsoft Office

*       Flexibility to work evenings and weekends

*       Ability to travel regularly across your region and occasionally to Newcastle

*       Knowledge of the EPPP framework and academy regulations

*       Ability to align with and demonstrate the club’s values

Desirable (nice to have but not essential)

*       FA Talent ID Level 3

*       Experience within a Premier League or Category 1 Academy

*       Previous experience as a regional or part‑time scout

*       Established network of relationships across clubs, leagues, and associations in your region

About the team

At Newcastle United, we’re building something special – on and off the pitch. You’ll be part of a professional, forward-thinking Academy Recruitment team that works closely with colleagues across the club to ensure we find and support the very best young players.

Why choose us?

We’ve got a range of great benefits and rewards, from participation in our non-contractual employee bonus scheme, NUFC life assurance, free parking, discount at Shearers Bar and the Club Shop, Helping Hand – where you can access free GP appointments, Wellbeing Resources, Legal and Financial Support, pension contribution, free lunch and the best part, free tea and coffee. In addition, we run a salary sacrifice scheme which includes, tech, car, cycle to work and many more.

United As One

We’re committed to equality, diversity and inclusion and believe in equal opportunities for all. We recognise that the diversity of our people is one of our greatest strengths. We work together to reflect the communities we serve and to maintain an inclusive environment in which everyone can be their authentic self and is enabled to achieve their full potential.

Safeguarding

We’re committed to being a place where everyone is safe, heard, valued and able to thrive so we place high value on the safeguarding and welfare of everyone we engage with.

How to apply

To access the full job description for this role please click here.

For further information on this role and about the club and our values please visit our careers page.

Please apply as soon as possible as this vacancy may close early should we receive a high volume of suitable candidates.

Recruitment Agencies – please note that we do not accept unsolicited / speculative candidate details or applications. Any candidates supplied, unless formally requested, will be taken as a direct / free candidate.

About the role
If you have a passion for spotting emerging talent and helping young players reach their potential, this is your opportunity to play a key role within our Academy Recruitment team and be part of the team shaping the future of Newcastle United.
You’ll be integral to identifying high-quality grassroots players in the London area and ensuring our club continues to grow a diverse, exciting pipeline of young talent.
You will identify, assess, and recommend talented young players across your designated grassroots region, working closely with the London Grassroots & UK Non‑League Scout and the wider Academy Recruitment team. You’ll represent Newcastle United in the local football community, uphold our values, and ensure our talent identification processes align with EPPP and governing body regulations.
What we’re looking for - essential
You will possess:
* FA Talent ID Level 2
* FA Safeguarding Certificate
* Experience working within a professional football academy or recruitment environment
* Proven experience identifying talented young athletes
* Ability to maintain confidentiality of sensitive information
* Experience working with both live and video‑based talent assessment
* Strong report‑writing and communication skills
* Knowledge of the local talent pool within your designated region
* Strong organisational skills and ability to work independently
* Competent in Microsoft Office
* Flexibility to work evenings and weekends
* Ability to travel regularly across your region and occasionally to Newcastle
* Knowledge of the EPPP framework and academy regulations
* Ability to align with and demonstrate the club’s values
Desirable (nice to have but not essential)
* FA Talent ID Level 3
* Experience within a Premier League or Category 1 Academy
* Previous experience as a regional or part‑time scout
* Established network of relationships across clubs, leagues, and associations in your region
About the team
At Newcastle United, we’re building something special – on and off the pitch. You’ll be part of a professional, forward-thinking Academy Recruitment team that works closely with colleagues across the club to ensure we find and support the very best young players.
Why choose us?
We’ve got a range of great benefits and rewards, from participation in our non-contractual employee bonus scheme, NUFC life assurance, free parking, discount at Shearers Bar and the Club Shop, Helping Hand – where you can access free GP appointments, Wellbeing Resources, Legal and Financial Support, pension contribution, free lunch and the best part, free tea and coffee. In addition, we run a salary sacrifice scheme which includes, tech, car, cycle to work and many more.
United As One
We’re committed to equality, diversity and inclusion and believe in equal opportunities for all. We recognise that the diversity of our people is one of our greatest strengths. We work together to reflect the communities we serve and to maintain an inclusive environment in which everyone can be their authentic self and is enabled to achieve their full potential.
Safeguarding
We’re committed to being a place where everyone is safe, heard, valued and able to thrive so we place high value on the safeguarding and welfare of everyone we engage with.
How to apply
To access the full job description for this role please click here.
For further information on this role and about the club and our values please visit our careers page.
Please apply as soon as possible as this vacancy may close early should we receive a high volume of suitable candidates.
Recruitment Agencies – please note that we do not accept unsolicited / speculative candidate details or applications. Any candidates supplied, unless formally requested, will be taken as a direct / free candidate.

job.title: Academy Regional Scout - Bedford and South Coast
job.html_description:
Academy Regional Scout – Bedford and South Coast

Newcastle United

Permanent / Part Time

Bedford and surrounding areas

Competitive Salary

We are the heartbeat of the city. Come and be a part of a long and proud history where we strive to be the best in everything we do, on and off the pitch. Bringing the people and communities together, join the Newcastle United Family, as we embark on the next steps of our exciting journey.

About the role

We’re looking for passionate, knowledgeable Academy Regional Scouts to identify and assess young players (U13–U18) with the potential to represent Newcastle United and help shape the next generation of Newcastle United talent.

If you know your local talent landscape inside out, love watching youth football, and can spot potential others miss, this could be the perfect next step.

What you’ll do

Working closely with the Academy Scouting Lead and the wider recruitment team, you will:

Identify and assess talented young players (U13–U18) within your designated region (Bedfordshire / South Coast & surrounding area).

Provide clear, objective scouting reports through the club’s reporting systems, with recommendations to sign, monitor or release from consideration.

Build and maintain strong knowledge of local fixtures, clubs, leagues and emerging talent.

Use both live games and video footage to analyse players against the club’s agreed profiles and methodology.

Collaborate with colleagues to ensure effective, accountable coverage across your age groups and region.

Uphold the highest standards of safeguarding, welfare, equality, and professionalism when representing Newcastle United.

This role involves frequent travel across your region and occasional travel to Newcastle to support player inductions and key meetings.

What we’re looking for – Essential

You must possess the following:

*       FA Talent ID Level 2 (or working towards, with a clear plan to complete).

*       FA Safeguarding Certificate (or willingness to update/complete before starting).

*       Experience working within a professional football academy or recruitment environment.

*       Proven experience of identifying talented young players and producing structured, evidence-based scouting reports.

*       Experience of watching and assessing players via live games and video footage.

*       Strong knowledge of the local talent pool in Bedford / South Coast and surrounding area.

*       Ability to work flexibly, including evenings and weekends.

*       Good communication, organisation and time-management skills, with the ability to work independently.

*       Competent with Microsoft Office and comfortable using digital scouting/reporting systems.

*       Willingness and ability to travel regularly within your region.

*       Alignment with the values and culture of Newcastle United, including safeguarding, welfare, equality, diversity and inclusion.

Desirable

It would be great if you have the following, but not essential:

*       FA Talent ID Level 3.

*       Experience within a Premier League or Category 1 Academy.

*       Previous experience as a part-time or regional scout for a professional club.

*       An established network of contacts within local clubs, leagues and football associations.

About the team

At Newcastle United, we’re building something special – on and off the pitch. You’ll be part of a professional, forward-thinking Academy Recruitment team that works closely with colleagues across the club to ensure we find and support the very best young players.

Why choose us?

We’ve got a range of great benefits and rewards, from participation in our non-contractual employee bonus scheme, NUFC life assurance, free parking, discount at Shearers Bar and the Club Shop, Helping Hand – where you can access free GP appointments, Wellbeing Resources, Legal and Financial Support, pension contribution, free lunch and the best part, free tea and coffee. In addition, we run a salary sacrifice scheme which includes, tech, car, cycle to work and many more.

United As One

We’re committed to equality, diversity and inclusion and believe in equal opportunities for all. We recognise that the diversity of our people is one of our greatest strengths. We work together to reflect the communities we serve and to maintain an inclusive environment in which everyone can be their authentic self and is enabled to achieve their full potential.

Safeguarding

We’re committed to being a place where everyone is safe, heard, valued and able to thrive so we place high value on the safeguarding and welfare of everyone we engage with.

How to apply

To access the full job description for this role please click here.

For further information on this role and about the club and our values please visit our careers page.

Please apply as soon as possible as this vacancy may close early should we receive a high volume of suitable candidates.

Recruitment Agencies – please note that we do not accept unsolicited / speculative candidate details or applications. Any candidates supplied, unless formally requested, will be taken as a direct / free candidate.

job.title: Senior Partnership Development Manager
job.html_description:
Senior Partnership Development Manager

Newcastle upon Tyne Permanent, Full Time
Salary: Competitive

Closing date: 10 March 2026

Please apply early as this vacancy may close ahead of schedule.

Be part of something bigger. Build global partnerships that fuel our ambition.

We are the heartbeat of the city. As we continue our ambitious journey on and off the pitch, we’re growing a world‑class commercial operation that supports our long‑term success where it matters most — on the pitch.

This is your opportunity to join Newcastle United at a pivotal moment, helping us drive meaningful commercial revenue that makes a real difference to the Club’s future.

We’re looking for an experienced, confident and commercially driven Senior Partnership Development Manager who can hit the ground running. You’ll take ownership of securing high‑value global partnerships, driving a strong pipeline from day one, and delivering results in a fast‑paced, elite sporting environment. You’ll bring an established network of brand and contacts, enabling you to quickly open doors, start conversations and accelerate opportunities.

Working closely with the Head of Partnership Development, you’ll manage the full deal cycle — from prospecting and relationship‑building through to pitching, negotiation and closing. You’ll lead with energy, pace and precision, identifying opportunities, opening doors and creating compelling, insight‑led proposals that win.

What you’ll bring

We’re looking for someone who is proactive, resilient and thrives on delivering results. You’ll have:

*        A strong track record in closing significant commercial partnership deals and consistently exceeding B2B revenue targets

*        Demonstrable success in partnerships or sponsorship sales, within sport

*        Confidence creating and pitching high‑quality, bespoke commercial propositions

*        The ability to quickly build trusted relationships at senior levels, both internally and externally

*        A natural drive to grow pipelines, maximise opportunities and consistently move deals forward

*        An existing network of brands and contacts, enabling you to accelerate commercial conversations from day one

*        A strong ability to determine the Club’s unique ‘hook’ when responding to a brief, aligning partner needs with compelling NUFC‑led solutions

*        This role requires regular attendance at prospect meetings, industry events and conferences in the UK and internationally.

About the team

You’ll join a high‑performing, ambitious Partnerships team with deep expertise in sports sponsorship. We work creatively and collaboratively, shaping exciting propositions, exploring emerging categories, and building a diverse global partner portfolio that reflects the ambition of the Club.

Location

This role is based in Newcastle upon Tyne.

Why choose us?

We offer a fantastic range of rewards including, participation in our non‑contractual employee bonus scheme, NUFC life assurance, free parking, discounts at Shearers Bar and the Club Shop, access to the Helping Hand wellbeing platform, pension contributions, free lunch and refreshments, plus salary‑sacrifice benefits including tech, car and cycle‑to‑work schemes.

United As One

We are committed to equality, diversity and inclusion, ensuring everyone can be their authentic self and achieve their potential. We are proud to reflect the communities we serve.

Safeguarding

We are committed to creating safe environments for everyone we engage with. Safeguarding is everyone’s responsibility.

Apply now

To view the full job description, please click here.

For more information about the Club and our values, please visit our Careers Page.

job.title: Partnership Services Analyst
job.html_description:
Partnership Services Analyst

Permanent, Full Time

Newcastle upon Tyne
Competitive Salary

Be part of something bigger. Build global partnerships that fuel our ambition.

We are the heartbeat of the city. A club with bold ambitions on a global stage and deep roots in our community. If you’re driven to raise the bar and make an impact, you’ll find the platform, the people and the pride to do your best work here.

This is a great opportunity to develop, learn and make a real contribution in a fast paced and exciting environment.

About the role

We’re looking for a proactive and analytical Partnership Services Analyst to join our Partnerships team. In this role, you’ll use data, insight and market analysis to help shape the partnership strategy across the club. You’ll support both Partnership Development and Partnership Marketing by delivering high‑quality insights, building robust reporting frameworks and ensuring our teams have access to information that drives strong decision‑making.

You’ll play a key role in strengthening our understanding of categories, brands, audiences and performance—helping us build meaningful, evidence‑based partnerships.

What you’ll do

*                 Work collaboratively with the Partnership Services Executive to provide a first‑class service to our Partnership Development and Partnership Marketing teams.

*                 Create data‑driven approaches to identifying key categories and brands for outreach, securing accurate contact details for potential partners.

*                 Provide regular market insight and trend analysis to position the team as a trusted, knowledgeable resource across the club.

*                 Use strong data and insights skills to support the development of compelling sales proposals and outreach materials.

*                 Work with digital colleagues to build systems for analysing digital and data metrics.

*                 Develop reporting mechanisms that demonstrate ROI for existing partners and support the sales team in articulating value to prospects.

The team

You’ll be part of a friendly, supportive and dedicated team who will help you settle in and succeed. We value curiosity, teamwork and a positive approach.

What you’ll bring

*                 Strong analytical ability with experience gathering, interpreting and presenting data and insights.

*                 Calm and resilient mindset, especially in a fast‑paced environment.

*                 Good commercial awareness.

*                 Ability to plan and prioritise your workload effectively.

*                 A collaborative and curious approach, with the desire to generate fresh ideas.

*                 Ability to build strong networks across diverse stakeholder groups at the club.

*                 A degree or equivalent industry‑standard experience.

*                 Experience within sport and/or sponsorship sectors would be desirable.

United As One

We’re committed to equality, diversity and inclusion and believe in equal opportunities for all. We recognise that the diversity of our people is one of our greatest strengths. We work together to reflect the communities we serve and to maintain an inclusive environment where everyone can be their authentic self and achieve their full potential.

Safeguarding

We’re committed to being a place where everyone is safe, heard, valued and able to thrive. We place high value on the safeguarding and welfare of everyone we engage with.

How to apply 

To access the full job description for this role please click here.

For further information on this role and about the club and our values please visit our careers page.

Please apply as soon as possible as this vacancy may close early should we receive a high volume of suitable candidates.

Recruitment Agencies – please note that we do not accept unsolicited / speculative candidate details or applications. Any candidates supplied, unless formally requested, will be taken as a direct / free candidate. 

Right to work

This position is UK‑based and requires proof of the right to live and work in the UK. All job offers are conditional upon background checks, including reference history, DBS checks and verification of your eligibility to work. Please note, we do not sponsor applicants without the right to work in the UK.

* A collaborative and curious approach, with the desire to generate fresh ideas.
* Ability to build strong networks across diverse stakeholder groups at the club.
* A degree or equivalent industry‑standard experience.
* Experience within sport and/or sponsorship sectors would be desirable.
United As One
We’re committed to equality, diversity and inclusion and believe in equal opportunities for all. We recognise that the diversity of our people is one of our greatest strengths. We work together to reflect the communities we serve and to maintain an inclusive environment where everyone can be their authentic self and achieve their full potential.
Safeguarding
We’re committed to being a place where everyone is safe, heard, valued and able to thrive. We place high value on the safeguarding and welfare of everyone we engage with.

For further information on this role and about the club and our values please visit our careers page.
